title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet's Session
content: The Council of Ministers renewed the Kingdom's condemnation of the terrorist acts that took place in Egypt and Somalia, and the terrorist assault on worshippers leaving the Finsbury Park Mosque in London yesterday, as well as the attack on security forces inn Champs-Elys?ā??es in Paris yesterday, expressing the Kingdom's firm rejection of all forms of terrorism and extremism. On the recent Qatif terrorist attack, the cabinet reiterated the Kingdom's keenness on fighting terrorism, protecting its homeland security. It also noted the efforts of the naval forces in confronting three boats that entered the Kingdom's territorial waters. Moreover, it considered the bombing of the three trucks belonging to King Salman Relief and Humanitarian Action Center while preparing for distribution of food aid in the city of Marib as a threat to humanitarian work by the Houthis. King Salman briefed the council on the results of his meeting with Pakistani President Mamnoon Hussain, Yemeni President Abdurbo Mansour Hadi, his talks with the Iraqi Prime Minister Dr. Haider Al-Abbadi and the phone calls received from Russian President Vladimir Putin, British Prime Minister Teresa May and Moroccan Minister of Foreign Affairs and Cooperation Nasser Bourita. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz chaired the meeting held by the Council of Ministers, on Monday evening at Al-Safa Palace in Makkah.

title: Kingdom Condemns Terror Attack in London
content: The Foreign Ministry today condemned the terrorist attack that took place on worshipers leaving the Finsbury Mosque in London yesterday.?é?? In a statement, the Foreign Ministry offered its condolences to the families of the victims and UK government and people, and expressed his country's firm rejection of all forms of terrorism and extremism.

title: King Salman Holds Talks With Iraqi Prime Minister
content: The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz held talks with the Iraqi Prime Minister Dr. Haider Al-Abbadi at Al-Safa Palace in Makkah today.?é?? During the meeting, they reviewed bilateral relations between the two brotherly countries and discussed regional developments.

title: King Salman Receives Turkish Foreign Minister
content: The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz received at Safa Palace in Makkah this evening Turkish Foreign Minister Mevlut Cavusoglu.?é?? During the meeting, they reviewed the bilateral relations between the kingdom and Turkey and discussed developments in the region.

title: Kingdom Rejects Turkish Offer to Establish Military Bases in Saudi Arabia
content: The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia today rejected an offer by Turkey to set up a military base on Saudi territory.?é?? In a statement carried the Saudi Press Agency, the Kingdom said it ???Ć?£cannot allow Turkey to establish military bases on its territories???Ć?and noted that the Kingdom has no need for this since its armed forces and military capabilities are at the best level and it has military presence in a number of countries, including the Incirlik Air Base.

title: Statement Following the Conclusion of Pensacola Naval Air Station Attack Investigation
content: Finally, close to a million Saudis have come to the US for their education, training and other reasons over several decades. The overwhelming majority of them are law-abiding citizens who view themselves as unofficial ambassadors of the kingdom. Many consider the United States as a second home and treat their American colleagues, classmates and neighbors as an extended part of their family. Thousands of them took to social media to condemn the attacker and to extend their condolences to Americans, as did our leadership and our ambassador to the United States. Saudi Arabia has been at the forefront of the international community???Ć?Ös effort to counter terrorist groups for many years.?é?? The kingdom has used every means at its disposal to counter the men, mindset and money that allow Al Qaeda, ISIS and other terrorist groups to recruit followers and to threaten communities and even entire nations.?é?? In addition, the terrorists who have struck the US and many other nations have also targeted Saudi Arabia???Ć?Ös people, leadership, military personnel and even our holiest religious institutions in Mecca and Medina on multiple occasions. Violent extremists continue to pose a serious challenge to the security of the United States, Saudi Arabia and most countries around the world. Terror groups have recruited thousands of people from dozens of countries. The kingdom has worked closely with the US to counter this global threat. It is worth noting that the military training that the US provides to Saudi military personnel has enabled Saudi soldiers, pilots and sailors to fight along their American counterparts and against our common foes. The close cooperation between the two nations on intelligence matters and issues related to counter terrorism has saved the lives of many in the US, Saudi Arabia and elsewhere and has made the world a safer place.?é?? Approximately 28,000 Saudis have undergone military training in the US over the course of several decades without incident. Saudi Arabia has fully cooperated with American investigators looking into the motivations of the attacker. Saudi Arabia will continue to cooperate with US authorities should they require additional information. In addition, the Kingdom has determined that 21 cadets have demonstrated conduct unbecoming of an officer in the Saudi Royal Air Force or Royal Navy and therefore have been unenrolled from their training program in the U.S. military. They will be returning to the Kingdom. The disturbed and radicalized individual who carried out this terrible attack acted alone. He does not represent the hundreds of thousands of Saudis who have lived, studied and trained in the United States over the past several decades, nor does his heinous act represent the values of Saudi Arabia. title: King Salman Deputizes Crown Prince to Administer Affair of State in his Absence
content: The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz issued today a issued a royal decree deputizing Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman to administer the state's affairs and take care of the interests of the people during the period of our absence from the Kingdom.

title: Prince Abdulrahman bin Abdulaziz Passed Away, Royal Court announces
content: The Royal Court announced today the death of Prince Abdulrahman bin Abdulaziz Al-Saud.?é?? Funeral prayers for his soul will be performed at the Grand Mosque in Makkah on Friday following Isha (evening) prayer.

title: Prince Mohammed bin Naif Pledges Allegiance to Prince Mohammed bin Salman as Crown Prince
content: On the occasion of his selection as the Crown Prince at Al-Safa Palace in Makkah on Wednesday June 20, 2017; Prince Mohammad bin Naif bin Abdulaziz pledged allegiance to Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, wishing him success in his new position.

title: King Salman Names New Ministers, Governors, Ambassador to the US
content: Hail Province Governor Prince Saud bin Abdulmohsin has been replaced by Prince Abdulaziz bin Saad and Prince Faisal bin Khalid bin Sultan replaced Prince Mishal bin Abdullah as governor of the Northern Border Province. Prince Abdulaziz bin Salman was named minister of state for energy affairs, Prince Khalid bin Salman was appointed ambassador to the United States, Abdullah bin Amer Al-Sawaha was appointed Minister of Communications and Information Technology, and Dr. Awwad bin Al-Awwad was appointed Minister of Culture and Information. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz today issued a number of royal orders, including the appointments of a new ambassador to the United States and ministers of Culture and Information, and Communications and Information Technology.?é?? King Salman removed Civil Service Minister Khaled Al-Arj and ordered an investigation of possible?é??abuse of?é??office. The royal orders also?é??included the appointment of two new governors and a number of deputy governors. King Salman ordered?é??the restoration of?é??financial allowances for civil servants and military personnel that had been suspended under earlier austerity measures.

title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet's Session
content: The Cabinet lauded efforts security forces in pursuing terrorists and anyone who tries to undermine the Kingdom's security and threaten its citizens and residents.?é?? The Cabinet commended the security forces for their recent success in raiding a terrorist hideout north of Al-Awamiya in Qatif Governorate and foiling their terror plans. The Council of Ministers renewed the Kingdom's condemnation terrorist attack in Parachinar, Pakistan and expressed its condolences to the families of the victims, Pakistan and its people. The Council of Ministers condemned the declaration by the Israeli occupation authorities to establish a 2,000 new settlement on 241 acres of Palestinian land in the West Bank for the first time in 20 years. The Council reaffirmed the Kingdom???Ć?Ös positions towards the Palestinian cause, including its condemnation of all actions taken by the Israeli occupation authorities and the Kingdom???Ć?Ös repeated calls to the international community to compel Israel to cease all arbitrary and racist measures against the Palestinian people. King Salman also briefed the Cabinet on the results of his meetings with Arab leaders on the sidelines of the summit expressing his pleasure over the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia's hosting of the 29th Arab Summit in Riyadh. King Salman began the weekly session by expressing his deep thanks and appreciation to King Abdullah II bin Al-Hussein of Jordan for the warm welcome and generous hospitality accorded to him and the accompanying delegation during his visit to Jordan.?é?? King Salman also expressed his appreciation to the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan, the King, the Government and people for their great efforts exerted to ensure the success of the 28th Arab Summit Conference. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz chaired the Cabinet's session at Al-Yamamah Palace this afternoon.

title: Vice Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Chairs Cabinet Session
content: The Cabinet reiterated the condemnation of terrorist attacks which took place at the military hospital in Kabul, Afghanistan and a military base in Mali. The Cabinet stressed the decisions of the session, confirming the full commitment and adherence to the Arab Peace Initiative without change, and the need to enable the Palestinian people to exercise their inalienable rights. The Cabinet was then briefed on the latest developments on the Arab and Islamic arenas. The Cabinet lauded the decisions adopted by the 147th session of the Council of Arab Foreign Ministers held in Cairo. In this context, the Cabinet expressed appreciation to the Arab foreign ministers for thanking the Saudi-led Arab Coalition to Support Legitimacy in Yemen in its support of Yemen's legitimacy and people, the resumption of the peace process, and endeavors to stop war and restore the Yemeni state.?é?? The Cabinet also appreciated the humanitarian efforts of the King Salman Humanitarian Aid & Relief Center (KSRelief) in providing assistance to the Yemeni people.?é?? The Cabinet also welcomed the Council of Arab Foreign Ministers???Ć?Ös statements unilaterally condemning the coup leaders in Yemen, their violations against the people of Yemen as well as the Iran???Ć?Ös continual interventions that violate the security and stability of Yemen. Crown Prince Mohammed briefed the Cabinet on the contents of the message sent by the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz to Sheikh Sabah Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah of Kuwait, the telephone call King Salman had with President Joko Widodo of Indonesia, and his meetings in Indonesian.?é?? Crown Prince Mohammad also briefed the cabinet on his meeting with New Zealand???Ć?Ös Foreign Minister Murray MuCully. Vice Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Prince Mohammad bin Naif chaired the Cabinet's weekly session at Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh this afternoon.

title: Crown Prince Chairs Cabinet Session
content: The Cabinet also approved a scientific cooperation agreement with Belarus; authorized King Saud University to discuss a MoU for academic cooperation with Shanghai University in China.?é?? The Cabinet also authorized the Minister of Culture and Information, or his designate, to discuss with Indonesia and Malaysia a program to exchange news between the Saudi Press Agency and Indonesian and Malaysian news agencies. The Cabinet finally adjourned after discussing a number of topics including cooperation agreements between the Kingdom and each of Kyrgyzstan and Russia in combating crime and protection of classified information; approved the memorandum of understanding with Afghanistan on cooperation in judiciary and legal affairs and the memorandum of understanding with Japan in the field of social development. The Cabinet reviewed the latest developments at the regional and international arenas including the resumption of Syrian negotiations in Geneva under the auspices of the United Nations. The Cabinet expressed hope that the negotiations would bring about a comprehensive ceasefire and a political solution to the Syrian crisis based on Geneva Declaration I and the UN Security Council Resolution 2254. The Cabinet was briefed on the outcomes of the visit of Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir to Iraq, where he held talks with a number of Iraqi officials, reiterating the affirmation of the ties binding the two countries. Crown Prince Mohammad briefed the Cabinet on the outcomes of the talks that the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman held in Riyadh during the week with President Mohammed Abdullah Farmajo of Somalia and President Prokopios Pavlopoulos of Greece. Vice Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Prince Mohammed bin Naif Saud chaired the Cabinet session Monday afternoon at Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh.

title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet's Session
content: The Cabinet authorized the General Commission for Statistics to conduct the population general census for 2020. The Cabinet approved the incentives and privileges offered to donors who purchase antiquities or masterpieces or special collections displayed in national museums; approved the regulatory provisions of the General Authority for Sports; The Cabinet approved the security cooperation agreement with Djibouti signed in Riyadh on 10/07/1437 AH; approved the memorandum of understanding (MoU) between the Ministry of Interior???Ć?Ös Saudi Financial Investigations Administration (SAFA) and the Financial Investigation Unit (FIU) of India as regards cooperation on the exchange of the investigations related to money laundering, terror financing and related crimes, signed in Riyadh on 25/06/1437 AH; approved MoU for cooperation in the field of renewable energy between King Abdullah City for Atomic and Renewable Energy and China???Ć?Ös National Power Management, signed in Riyadh on 09/04/1437H. The Cabinet finally adjourned after discussing a number of domestic issues. The Council strong condemnation of the murder of Russia's Ambassador to Turkey, Andrey Karlov and the terror attack in Berlin, Germany, that resulted in the death of 12 people.?é?? The Cabinet also the double terrorist bombing of a market in Baghdad and the attack in Istanbul.?é?? The Cabinet offered its condolences to the families of the victims. The Cabinet also reiterated the Kingdom's appreciation to U.S. Secretary of State John Kerry???Ć?Ös statement on the final solution of the Palestinian-Israeli conflict, as well as the adoption by Security Council Resolution 2334, which condemns Israeli settlement and calls for stopping it in the occupied Palestinian territories. The Cabinet also discussed the outcome of the 97th OPEC meeting in Cairo and commitment by OPEC members to honor the November 2016 agreement on reducing oil production. The Cabinet welcomed Security Council Resolution 2328 on the deployment of international observers in Aleppo to supervise the evacuation of civilians. The Cabinet was briefed on a number of reports including the emergency meeting of the Executive Committee of Foreign Ministers of Organization of Islamic Cooperation that the massacres being committed in Aleppo, Syrian, can be classified as a war crime and crimes against humanity. King Salman briefed the Cabinet on the results of his two phone calls with King Abdullah II bin Al-Hussein of Jordan and President Abed Rabbo Hadi of Yemen, as well as his two meetings with Palestinian President Mahmud Abbas and Chairman of the Board of Directors of Al-Quds Fund Muneeb Al-Masri, in addition to the messages received from King of Jordan; Emir of Kuwait and President of Comoros. The Cabinet appreciated King Salman???Ć?Ös order to organize a popular campaign for the Syrian people relief of the Syrian people.?é?? King Salman???Ć?Ös directive to allocate SR 100 million ($26.67 million) for the campaign and his personal SR 20 million ($5.33 million) donation.?é?? The Cabinet also express its appreciation to the SR 10 million ($2.67 million) donation by Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif and the SR 10 million ($2.67 million) Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman???Ć?Ös SR 8 million ($2.13 million) to alleviate the sufferings of Syrian people, especially the displaced from Aleppo. The Cabinet began by congratulating King Salman on the second anniversary of his ascension to the throne.?é?? The Cabinet then commended the State???Ć?Ös general budget for the fiscal year 2017; for its sufficient strength to cope with economic and financial challenges. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz chaired the Cabinet's session at Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh on Monday afternoon.

title: Kingdom Issues 2017 Budget, Projects Major Deficit Reduction
content: Saudi Arabia is a member of the G20 and ranks as the 29th most competitive economy in the world, according to World Economic Forum???Ć?Ös 2016-2017 Global Competitiveness Report. Revenue is projected to reach SAR 692 billion ($184.5 billion) in 2017, a 31 percent increase from initial projections. Oil revenues are expected to increase by 46 percent, and non-oil revenues are estimated to grow by 6.5 percent. Moreover, the budget deficit is expected to reach SAR 198 billion in 2017, reflecting 7.7 percent of the GDP. Combined, these efforts will move the Kingdom closer to its Vision 2030 goal of balancing the budget by 2020. In reaching these goals, the 2017 budget expenditures will focus primarily on the following key sectors: ???Ć?£Our economy is firm and it has sufficient strength to cope with the current economic and financial challenges,???Ć?said King Salman. ???Ć?£We have sought through this budget and its programs to improve the efficiency of capital and operational expenditures in the state, strengthen the situation of public finances, enhance their sustainability, give priority to developmental and service projects and programs that serve citizens directly, contribute to activating the role of the private sector and increase its contribution to the Gross Domestic Product.???Ć? is estimated to reach SAR 890 billion ($237.3), an 8 percent increase from 2016, and is projected to reflect a record 33 percent decrease in the Kingdom???Ć?Ös national deficit. title: Saudi King Says Nation Is Committed to Regional Security and Economic Development
content: ???Ć?£I am optimistic about a better tomorrow,???Ć?King Salman said. ???Ć?£These conditions that we are now experiencing are not more difficult than their previous ones. We will overpass them to a better and brilliant future.???Ć?King Salman noted that economic reforms implemented to date have focused on diversifying the nation???Ć?Ös economy and re-distributing resources to expand job opportunities. Saudi Arabia has ???Ć?£come out of [the past three years] with a strong economy and continuous growth; we have set out our reforms today to look forward to the future,???Ć?he noted. The strength of these structural reforms fall under Vision 2030, Saudi Arabia???Ć?Ös economic transformation plan. The region???Ć?Ös stability directly impacts the Kingdom???Ć?Ös economic development, and ensuring both remains fundamental, King Salman stressed. ???Ć?£Our domestic policy is based on pillars of maintaining security, stability and prosperity to achieve the development that meets the needs of the present and preserves the right of future generations.???Ć????Ć?£We in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia believe that the security of our dear neighbor Yemen is the security of the Kingdom, and will not accept any interference in its internal affairs,???Ć?he stated. King Salman pledged to uphold Yemen???Ć?Ös right to security. Yemen, he said, cannot be the ???Ć?£corridor???Ć?for countries trying to undermine the security of the Kingdom, nor can Saudi Arabia allow other nations to undermine its sovereignty and security. Among the core foreign policy priorities, King Salman vowed to continue cooperating with the international community in combatting the extremism that threatens its borders and the region at large. Political solutions to international crises can safeguard values of tolerance and coexistence, paving the way for nations to ensure peace and achieve development, King Salman said. In his annual address to the Consultative Council, King Salman presented an optimistic outlook for the nation???Ć?Ös foreign and domestic policies. He said that Saudi Arabia has played an active and constructive role in the region and in important global undertakings with the international community is proceeding with ???Ć?£firm steps???Ć?to maintain its active role in the region and around the world. (WASHINGTON, DC) ???Ć?The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz reinforced today that Saudi Arabia remains fully committed to acting vigorously to ensure its security and the security of the region while moving forward on the Kingdom???Ć?Ös economic development and reforms.

title: Deputy Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Chairs Cabinet's Session
content: The Cabinet has also reviewed a number of issues on its agenda, including the annual report of the general organization for social insurance on a previous fiscal year. The Cabinet issued relevant instructions towards them. The cabinet also approved the appointment of Jamal bin Ali Al-Kishi and Eng. Omar bin Nabil Al-Khudairi as two members of the private sector in the Board of Directors of the Saline Water Conversion Corporation for a period of three years. The Cabinet approved an amendment to Table (2) regarding the joint committees list attached to the Cabinet's decision No. 2 dated 03/01/1428H, so that the Saudi side at the Saudi-Indonesian Joint Committee is chaired by the Minister of Foreign Affairs, and that the Committee shall meet every six months. The Cabinet concluded by approving a memorandum of understanding the Peoples Republic of China on enhancing joint cooperation on the silk road economic belt and the initiative of naval silk road for the 21st century and cooperation on productive energy which was signed in Riyadh on 9/4/1437 H;?é?? authorized the Minister of Islamic Affairs, Call and Guidance, or his deputy, to negotiate a memorandum of understanding in the field of Islamic Affairs with Mauritania, sign it, and forward the final version for taking further procedures; approved a general cooperation agreement Mauritius which was signed in New York on 12/12/1436H; authorized the Minister of Foreign Affairs, or his deputy, to negotiate with Norway and New Zealand draft memoranda of understanding on political consultations, sign them, and submit the final versions for taking further authentication;?é?? authorized the Acting Secretary General of King Abdulaziz Foundation for Research and Archives, or his deputy, to discuss with the United Arab Emirate a draft memorandum of cooperation between King Abdulaziz Foundation for Research and Archives Saudi Arabia and the National Archive of the Ministry of Presidential Affairs of the United Arab Emirates, sign it, and submit the signed final version. Also praised was the Kingdom's participation in the international conference on the preservation of endangered cultural heritage, held in Abu-Dhabi in the attendance of Prince Khalid Al-Faisal, Advisor to the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosque and Governor of Makkah Region, who led the Kingdom's delegation on behalf of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosque. The Cabinet lauded the holding of a number of cultural activities, citing the opening by the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques of Trade Roads in the Arab peninsula exhibition, the masterpieces and antiquities of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia across history, held at King Abdulaziz international cultural center in Dhahran, and the king's okay of the exhibition's tour of east Asia following its successful nine tours of Europe and America. To mark the occasion of the International Day for Solidarity with the Palestinian people, the Cabinet reiterated the Kingdom's call for the world community to remain committed towards the rights of Palestinians and retard Israel from its procrastinating policies against them. The Cabinet praised the 7th Bosphorus Summit.?é?? The Cabinet hailed the cooperation agreements in the fields of investment signed by Saudi Arabia and Turkey during the Summit. The Cabinet also praised the cooperation and contribution of the main oil producing countries outside OPEC to the restoration of stability and balance of international petroleum market. The Cabinet was briefed on the outcome of OPEC ministerial meeting which was held last week in Vienna. The Cabinet praised the outcome of the meeting, the decisions that were taken as well as the clear cooperation among all OPEC countries to restore stability in the international oil market by reducing excess supply. The Cabinet commended the outcome of the 35th Meeting of GCC Interior Ministers, expressing hope that the outcome of the meeting will contribute to enhancing the efforts of GCC countries in the field of security. The Cabinet reviewed a number of reports on regional, Arab and international developments, and looked forward to the outcome of meetings the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques will hold with leaders of the United Arab Emirates, the State of Qatar, the Kingdom of Bahrain and the State of Kuwait during his tour that emanates from his keenness to communicate with Gulf Cooperation Council leaders to serve the interests of their peoples and to enhance ties of brotherhood among GCC countries. Crown Prince Mohammad welcomed Minister of Labour and Social Development Dr. Ali bin Nasser Al-Ghafis in his new portfolio, wishing him success. He also thanked the outgoing officials and members for their efforts. The Deputy Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques congratulated the Kingdom's Grand Mufti; members of the Senior Scholars Commission; full-time sheikhs, members of the Standing Committee for Fatwa (affiliated to the Senior Scholars Commission); and Speaker and members of Majlis Al-Shura on the royal trust bestowed on them through renewing their memberships to their agencies, wishing them all success. Deputy Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Prince Mohammad bin Naif bin Abdulaziz Al Saud chaired the Cabinet's session which was held at Al-Yamamah Palace today.

title: Royal Order Appoints Al-Sadhan as Royal Court Advisor
content: The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz issued a royal order appointing Abdulrahman bin Mohammed Al-Sadhan as an advisor at the Royal Court, at ministerial rank, with immediate effect.

title: King Salman Recomposes Majlis Al-Shura (Consultative Council)
content: The Speaker of Majlis Al-Shura is instructed to carry out this royal order issued by King Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud. The newly reformed Majlis Al-Shura is to consist of these following members: King Salman appointed Sheikh Dr. Abdullah bin Mohammed bin Ibrahim Al-AsSheikh as Speaker of the Majlis Al-Shura, with Dr. Mohammed bin Amin bin Ahmed Al-Jiffri asDeputy Speaker, Dr. Yahya bin Abdullah bin Abdulaziz Al-Samaan as the Assistant to the Speaker of Majlis Al-Shura. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz issued a royal order to recompose the formation of Majlis Al-Shura for four years beginning of 3/3/1438 (Dec. 2, 2016).

title: Four Royal Orders Issued
content: Four royal orders were issued today.:

title: Security Forces Foil Drug Trafficking
content: The Ministry of Interior announced today that a security patrol intercepted an attempt to smuggle 5,173,000 amphetamine tablets. The ministry said that the suspects, nine Saudis and two Sudanese, were also in possession of three grams of heroin, a machine gun and a pistol.

title: Crown Prince Arrives in Ahsa Governorate
content: Crown Prince Mohammed bin Naif bin Abdulaziz, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior, arrived in Ahsa today to offer condolences to the families of those killed in yesterday???Ć?Ös attack at Al-Ridha Mosque.

title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet Session
content: The Cabinet approved a memorandum of understanding relating to the nuclear cooperation programs on establishing a partnership on small-compact-unit reactors technology and building of mutual nuclear human capabilities and academic research between King Abdullah City for Atomic and Renewable Energy and the Ministry of Science, Information and Communications Technology and future planning of the Republic of Korea, which was signed on 12/5/1436 H.; and renewed the membership of Abdul Aziz bin Mohammed Al-Athel to the board of directors of the Saudi Arabian Monetary Agency (SAMA) and appointed Hamad bin Saud Al-Sayari and Khalid bin Ahmed Al-Jaffali as members of the Board of Directors of SAMA, for a period of five years retroactive to 13/03/1437 H. The Cabinet adjourned after discussing a number of other local issues and approved the basic agreement on exchange and cooperation in the fields of geospatial intelligence with the United Stat, which was signed in Springfield, Virginia on 21/03/1436 H.; deputized the President of Saudi Commission for Tourism and National Heritage, or his deputy, to discuss and sign draft memorandum of understanding with China on cooperation and knowledge exchange in the field of cultural heritage; approved the Constituent Convention of the Asian Investment Bank on infrastructure, which was signed in Beijing on 12/9/1436 H.; authorized the Minister of Communications and Information Technology, or his deputy, to discuss with the Chinese a draft memorandum of understanding on cooperation in the field of promoting the Silk Road Information development aiming to convey information between the Ministry of Communications and Information Technology of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and the State Committee for Development and Reforms of the People's Republic of China, sign it, and forward the final version for further authentication.; adopted the guiding directory on beautification of cities in the municipalities of the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) member states, in an implementation of the GCC Supreme Council resolution, taken at its 35th session which was held in Doha on 17/02/1436 H. On domestic affairs, the Cabinet appreciated the directives of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques during his chairmanship of the 3rd meeting of the Board of Trustees of King Fahd National Library in its 9th session. The meeting stressed on the importance of the library in research. The Cabinet commended the forces of Coalition to Support Legitimacy in Yemen and the King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Works for the delivery of medical and relief assistance to the besieged people in the city of Taiz. The Cabinet was then briefed on the results of the visits of the Deputy Crown Prince to the United Arab Emirates and Pakistan. The Cabinet restated the Kingdom's emphasis before the UN???Ć?Ös annual meeting in New York that the Kingdom was and is still a leader in the fight against terrorism and extremism in all their forms. The Cabinet confirmed the Kingdom's strong condemnation and denunciation of terrorist attacks that took place in Iraq, Turkey, Somalia, Burkina Faso, Pakistan, Indonesia, and Cameroon. King Salman began by briefing the Cabinet on the telephone calls and messages with Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan, Yemeni President Abd Rabbou Mansour Hadi, Indonesian President Joko Widodo and Emir of Qatar Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani, as well as the outcome of his talks with Mexican President Enrique Pena Nieto, and the Speaker of the parliament of Kyrgyzstan Asylbek Jeenbekov. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz chaired the Cabinet's session at Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh this afternoon.

title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet Session
content: Finally, the Cabinet closed the session after discussing a number of local affairs and approved the following: the amendment of several articles of the weapons and ammunition system, issued according to the Royal Decree No. M /45 of 25/7/1426 H.; an agreement with Romania with respect to reciprocal treatment to exempt diplomatic missions from added value taxation, which was signed in Bucharest on 14/05/1436 H; authorized the Minister of Petroleum and Mineral Resources, or his deputy, to discuss and sign a draft memorandum of understanding with Mexican on cooperation in the oil and gas sectors of oil and gas; approved changing the name of the Institute of Diplomatic Studies to Prince Saud Al-Faisal Institute for Diplomatic Studies; approved the Air Services Agreement with Kyrgyzstan, which was signed in Riyadh on 10/02/1436 H; and approved several arrangements on incursions on government land. The Cabinet reviewed the Arab League Council's reconfirmation of its condemnation of the Iranian government for interfering in the internal affairs of Arab countries, considering it as a violation of the rules of the international law and the principle of good neighborliness, and a serious threat to regional and international security and peace. The Cabinet noted the position of Foreign Affairs Ministers of Arab Gulf states, their strong condemnation of Iranian attacks, their categorical rejection of them, and holding the Iranian authorities full responsibility according to their commitment to Vienna Conventions of 1961 and 1963, and the international law. The Cabinet also thanked the countries, which announced their solidarity with the Kingdom in its decision, and decided to recall their ambassadors and cut off diplomatic relations with the Iran. The Cabinet condemned the attacks, destruction, burning and looting of the contents of the Kingdom's Embassy in Tehran and Consulate in Mashhad, noting that the attacks came after Iranian aggressive regime's comments which formed a blatant incitement encouraged the attacks on the Kingdom's missions. The Cabinet expressed its appreciation to countries and Arab and international organizations, parliaments and bodies that supported the measures taken by the Kingdom to fight terrorism, track down perpetrators of terrorist acts, prosecute them, and implement judicial rulings against them in application of the Islamic Sharia. The Cabinet appreciated the King's directive to the Council of Economic and Development Affairs following the approval of the State's General Budget to work on launching a comprehensive economic, financial and structural reform program. The Cabinet also appreciated the King's directive to officials to give priority to complete the implementation of projects approved in previous budgets, many of them are under construction. The Cabinet stressed the Kingdom's proceeding in its foreign policy on firm principles committed to international covenants and defending Islamic and Arab issues aiming to fight terrorism and achieve security and stability in the world. The Cabinet congratulated the King on the first anniversary of his ascension to the throne, highlighting the continuing interest of the Government of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques in the sectors of health, education, housing, employment, transport, economy, and other sectors, and the provision of unlimited material, human and organizational support. The Cabinet also appreciated the annual address of the King during the inauguration of the fourth year of the sixth session of the Shura Council, and the King's developmental vision with integrated political, economic and security aspects. King Salman began by briefing the Cabinet on the outcomes of his talks with the presidents of Tunisia, Turkey, Palestine Tajikistan and former Lebanese President NAME. King Salman also briefed the cabinet on his telephone conversations the former Emir of Qatar Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al-Thani, President Abdel Fattah el-Sisi of Egypt, and King Abdullah II of Jordan The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz chaired the Cabinet's session at Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh this afternoon.

title: Kingdom Should be Commended, not Condemned for Fighting Terrorism
content: ???Ć?£As far as the Iranians are concerned, what I find very puzzling is this individual is a Saudi citizen, he committed a crime in Saudi Arabia, he was sentenced in Saudi courts, and the sentence was carried out by Saudi authorities,???Ć?said Minister Al-Jubeir. ???Ć?£For the Iranians to inject themselves into our domestic affairs is in line with what Iran has been doing for years throughout the region.???Ć?The foreign minister urged Iran to end its subversive activities and support for terrorism throughout the region, including its meddling in the domestic affairs of Syria, Iraq, Bahrain, Lebanon and Yemen. ???Ć?£In Saudi Arabia, we are very determined to confront terrorism and confront it robustly and firmly. We will show no mercy to those who kill the innocent. The individual [Al-Nimr], along with the other 46 individuals had their due process,???Ć?said Minister Al-Jubeir. ???Ć?£The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia should be commended for showing resolve and taking a firm position against people who kill the innocent, not condemned for it.???Ć?In an interview with CNBC yesterday, Saudi Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir reiterated the Kingdom???Ć?Ös commitment to confronting terrorism in all its forms and urged Iran to end its destabilizing activities throughout the region. Minister Al-Jubeir said that Nimr Al-Nimr was a terrorist and that he received a fair trial.

title: Preliminary Municipal Election Results
content: Provinces have begun releasing preliminary municipal election results of Saturday???Ć?Ös elections in which women were allowed to vote and run as candidates.

title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet Session
content: The Cabinet was briefed on the annual report of the Ministry of Economy and Planning for the fiscal year 1434 / 1435 H. It took the necessary instructions. Upon recommendations of the General Establishment for Pensions and the Shura Council decision No. 64/34 dated 9/7/1436 H, the Cabinet approved the inclusion of the service of whoever falls under Para I of the Cabinet's decision No. 368 dated 18/4/1392 H. which falls after 1/7/1388 H. up to 1/7/1435 H. because of retirement, provided that he/she was on a regular position. This will be in force until a new updated decision based on a study issued by the (abrogated) Civil Service Council on the situations of the companions, their heads and aides referred to in Para I of the legal, regulatory and financial aspects is issued or until 1/7/1440 H. which is first. The Cabinet approved the executive regulation detailing the real estate expropriation for public use system as well as the temporary seizure of real estate. The Cabinet approved amendments endorsed by the International Maritime Organization on July 1, 2012 on the sixth appendix of the international agreement to prevent sea pollution caused by vessels 1973. The Cabinet authorized the Minister of Justice, or his deputy, to discuss and sign a draft memorandum of understanding in the judicial and legal fields with Belarus; authorized the Minister of Finance, or his deputy, to sign a draft agreement on taxes on income and prevention of tax evasion with Kenya; authorized the Minister of Foreign Affairs, or his deputy, to discuss and sign a general agreement on cooperation with Ghana; authorized the Minister of Education,or his deputy,to discuss and sign general agreement on cooperation with UNESCO in the field of establishing a training course for beginner vocational engineers. The Cabinet passed, upon recommendation of the Shura Council, the undeveloped urban lands system. According to the new measure, an annual fee worth 2.5% of the value of any empty plot of land initially allotted for residential or commercial usage inside the city's urban zone for the benefit of a person or more, or non-governmental legal personality will be levied from the owner/s. The executive regulation of the system will fix the standard of how to estimate the land's value and the timetable for gradually imposing the charges, as well as the necessary regulations that guarantee its fair implementation and prevention of evading paying it. The levied fees will be deposited at a special account at the Saudi Arabian Monetary Agency which will be, in turn, appropriated for disbursing on housing projects and providing them with public utilities and service supply. According to the decision, the Cabinet will issue a new updated decision within 180 days based on the system's executive regulation, to be issued by the Ministry of Housing in collaboration with relevant bodies. The new system will go into force after 180 days from publishing it in the official gazette. The Cabinet finalized by discussing a number of local affairs issues including efforts exerted to facilitate the measures for Umrah performers and stressing the importance of 'Family Safety Program' organized by the Department of Health Affairs of the National Guard aimed at strengthening the role of the family in the renunciation of violence, extremism and terrorism. The Cabinet commended the results of Arab Justice Ministers in their 31st session in Cairo under the Kingdom's chairmanship and their agreement on the need for decisive confrontation of the threat of terrorism and the establishment of Arab Network for Judicial Cooperation to fight it. The Cabinet emphasized the importance of the events of The Future of Energy in the Middle East and North Africa Forum organized by the Arab Petroleum Investments Corporation (APICORP) in Manama, showing the Kingdom???Ć?Ös cooperation with OPEC and non-OPEC oil producers and consumers to preserve the stability of oil market and prices. The Cabinet reconfirmed the Kingdom's condemnation of all terrorist acts, including the armed terrorist attack on the hotel in the Mali. The Cabinet condemned the continuation of Israeli occupation authorities of arbitrary measures against the Palestinian people and the arrest operations of Palestinians in a number of provinces. The Cabinet welcomed the return of the Yemeni legitimate government to Aden and its direct supervision of the extensive military operations to liberate the rest of the provinces from Houthi and anti-government militias. The Cabinet commended the positions of countries supporting the resolution drafted by the Kingdom condemning intervention in Syria and endorsed by the UN Human Rights Committee and its 115 members states.?é?? The Cabinet was also briefed on the results of the Ministerial Council of the Gulf Cooperation Council for Arab States in its 137th session held in Riyadh. The Cabinet appreciated the King's directives for donation in the name of the Kingdom to support the various development projects of Digital Good Platform whose goal is to collect donations from individuals around the world to serve international development projects beyond 2015. King Salman began by briefing the Cabinet on his meetings with the leaders of the Group of Twenty in Antalya, during which they reviewed aspects of bilateral relations with the Kingdom and keenness on developing them in various fields.?é?? The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz chaired the Cabinet's session at Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh this afternoon.

title: King Salman Receives Princes, Islamic Scholars, Ministers, and Citizens
content: The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz received at Al-Salam Palace in Jeddah today princes, Ulema (Islamic scholars), ministers, and citizens who came to greet him.

title: Royal Court Announced Death of Prince Nawwaf bin Abdulaziz
content: The Royal Court announced today the death of Prince Nawwaf bin Abdulaziz Al-Saud, the Special Advisor to the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques, who passed away today. Funeral prayer will be performed on Wednesday, September 30 at the Grand Mosque in Makkah.

title: Saudi Minister of Hajj and Umrah Meets Iranian Hajj Officials
content: Minister of Hajj and Umrah Dr. Mohammad Saleh Bin Taher Benten met in Riyadh today Chairman of Iranian Hajj and Visit Organization Dr. Hameid Mohammadi to discuss Iranian pilgrims affairs arrangements for the this year???Ć?Ös Hajj season.

title: Supreme Court Declares Thursday, September 24 Eid Al-Adha
content: The Supreme Court of Saudi Arabia announced this evening Waqfat Arafat (standing at Mt. Arafat in Makkah) will be Wednesday, Sept. 23, 2015 and that Thursday, Sept. 24 will be the first day of Eid Al-Adha.

title: Saudi Arabia Received 19 Million Foreign Visitors in 2014
content: About 750,000 people are employed in the tourism industry in the Kingdom, a figure that is expected to reach 1.7 million by 2020. Tourism contributes over $21 billion to the economy (2.7 percent of the total GDP), excluding Hajj and Umrah pilgrims. Saudi Arabia received 19 million foreign visitors in 2014, half of them were for Hajj and Umrah. This was announced by Hamad Al-Sheikh, vice president of marketing and programs at the General Authority for Tourism and National Heritage, at the World Travel Market London this week.

title: Kingdom Clarifies Hajj Death Toll
content: Interior Ministry spokesman Maj. General Mansour al-Turki said the pictures also include people who died of natural causes, including foreign residents who performed the pilgrimage without the legal permits, as well as laborers from South Asia who choose to work in Saudi Arabia in order to perform the pilgrimage. Maj. Gen. Al-Turki added that the list also includes unidentified victims from the September 11 crane accident over the Grand Mosque. The Ministry of Interior today said the nearly 1,100 photos distributed to foreign diplomats to help identify nationals who have died in this year???Ć?Ös pilgrimage to Makkah are from the entire event, not just the disaster in Mina. The death toll for the Mina tragedy on September 24th remains 769 people, with another 934 injured.

title: Foreign Minister Al-Jubeir Discusses Iran???Ć?Ös Escalation of Regional Conflicts
content: The foreign minister concluded by saying, ???Ć?£The Iranians know very well that the Kingdom will do everything in its capacity to provide and facilitate visits to the holy sites in Saudi Arabia. The Iranians realize that the Kingdom is doing tremendous work for the service of the guests of Makkah. And I believe what they said contradicts the idea of sovereignty and non-interference. The Custodian of The Two Holy Mosques was clear when he ordered a thorough investigation and will hold any individual or organization accountable if found guilty. This investigation is ongoing and we will make the results public. The Iranians are the last to speak of Hajj and pilgrims, because they have caused chaos multiple times in the past, through protests in the 80s, which resulted in a number of death due to their riots.???Ć?In response to Rouhani comments on the Mina stampede, Foreign Minister Al-Jubeir stressed that Tehran is politicizing the human tragedy. ???Ć?£We have previously said that it is unacceptable to use such human tragedy politically, which happened while people were performing a religious ritual,???Ć?Minister Al-Jubeir said.?é?? Responding to President Rouhani???Ć?Ös comment that Iran is willing to contribute to a political solution in Yemen, Foreign Minister Al-Jubeir said that ???Ć?£the problem in Yemen was due to Houthis, along with former president Ali Abdullah Saleh overthrowing the government. The Houthis have gone to war multiple times in Yemen. Iran has relations with the Houthis, as it provides them with weapons and specialists, and Iran is one of the main reasons behind the war in Yemen. Iran is trying to fan the flames in Yemen by smuggling weapons to the Houthis in violation of UN Resolution 2216. Their last attempt was on Saturday, when an Iranian ship loaded with weapons was intercepted. So, Iran is the last country to speak of stability and security in Yemen. Iran is one of the main reasons for what is happening in Yemen today.???Ć?Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir responded to President Hassan Rouhani???Ć?Ös address to the 70th session of the UN General Assembly today by saying Iran should not be talking about seeking stability in the region as they are behind regional instability. In an interview with Al-Arabiya in New York, Minister Al-Jubeir said President Rouhani is the last to discuss democracy in Syria. "If it was not for Iran, there would not be the destruction and killing we see today in Syria. Iran supports Bashar Al-Assad by sending thousands of militants and fueling sectarianism between Sunnis and Shiites in Syria and the whole region. They recruit Hezbollah and other militias in the area and send them to Syria to defend Bashar Al-Assad???Ć?Ös regime,???Ć?said Minister Al-Jubeir.

title: Saudi Arabia Rejects Iran???Ć?Ös Criticism, Politicization of Hajj Tragedy
content: He continued, ???Ć?£We will reveal the facts when they emerge, and we will not hold anything back. ???Ć?ÄIf mistakes were made, those who made them will be held accountable, and we will ???Ć?Ämake sure that we will learn from this in order to ensure that it doesn???Ć?Öt happen again. But I want ???Ć?Äto repeat again, this is not a situation with which to play politics. I would hope that the Iranian ???Ć?Äleaders would be more sensible and more thoughtful with regards to those who perished in this ???Ć?Ätragedy and wait until we see the results of the investigation.???Ć?Ø???Ć?Minister Al-Jubeir stated, ???Ć?£The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques has directed to launch a ???Ć?Äthorough investigation that will be transparent. I believe that the Iranians should know better ???Ć?Äthan to try to make ???Ć?play politics with a tragic event that has befallen people who were ???Ć?Äperforming the most sacred religious duty, which is the pilgrimage. We, the Kingdom of Saudi ???Ć?ÄArabia, have a long history of expending tremendous resources to care for the pilgrimage and to ???Ć?Äensure that the pilgrims can come and have an extremely successful pilgrimage.???Ć?Ø???Ć?Saudi Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir firmly rejected Iran???Ć?Ös ???Ć?Äcriticism of Saudi Arabia???Ć?Ös handling of the Hajj. The foreign minister, who is in New York for ???Ć?Äthe U.N. General Assembly, made his remarks on Saturday alongside U.S. Secretary of State ???Ć?ÄJohn Kerry.???Ć?

title: Saudi Arabia Denies Reports that Motorcades Contributed to Hajj Tragedy
content: Saudi Arabia today denied that motorcades contributed to the trampling of the pilgrims in Mina yesterday. In response to inquiries from Western media, Ambassador Osama Ahmed Nugali, manager of the Media Department at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, cited the statement by Maj. Gen. Mansour Al-Turki, spokesman for the Ministry of Interior, that ???Ć?£motorcades of guests of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and other dignitaries do not pass through the area in question and that these reports are unfounded.???Ć?

title: Stampede Kills More than 700 in Mina
content: The Saudi Civil Defense announced today that 717 pilgrims were killed and another 805 wounded in a stampeded in Mina. The stampede occurred at 9:00 am at the intersection of 204th and 223rd Streets in Mina. The Civil Defense said it deployed some 4,000 workers along with over 220 ambulances and other vehicles, in response to the disaster. Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif bin Abdulaziz, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior, and Chairman of Hajj Higher Committee, instructed the formation of a special high-level investigative committees to investigate the cause of the stampede and submit its findings to the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosquys King Salman bin Abdulaziz.

title: Crown Prince Hold Emergency Meeting over Mina Stampede
content: Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif bin Abdulaziz, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior, who is also Chairman of Hajj Higher Committee, chaired today in Mina an extraordinary meeting of Hajj security leaders to discuss the stampede in Mina earlier today. During the meeting, the Crown Prince instructed the formation of a special high-level investigative committees to investigate the cause of the stampede and submit its findings to the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz.

title: 1,384,941 Foreign Pilgrims Participated in Hajj
content: Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif bin Abdulaziz Al Saud, Deputy Prime Minister, Minister of Interior and Chairman of Supreme Hajj Committee announced today that 1,384,941 foreign pilgrims from 164 countries have arrived in the Kingdom for this year???Ć?Ös Hajj. This year???Ć?Ös number reflect a 0.5 percent drop from the previous year.

title: 1,372,148 Foreign Pilgrims Arrived for this Year???Ć?Ös Hajj
content: The General Authority for Civil Aviation (GACA) has reported that as of yesterday, 1,372,148 pilgrims have arrived in the Kingdom since the beginning of this year???Ć?Ös Hajj season. 1,324,175 pilgrims arrived by air, 35,050 pilgrims by land, and 12,923 pilgrims by sea.

title: King Reviews Report on Makkah Crane Accident
content: Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz reviewed the report of the Accident Investigation Committee which concluded the absence of criminal suspicion and that the main reason for the accident is the strong winds while the crane was in a wrong position. King Salman issued directives to refer the results of the investigation to the Bureau of Investigation and Public Prosecution to complete investigation procedures with the Saudi Binladen Group (SBG). King Salman also issued directives to impose travel ban on all members of the SBG???Ć?Ös board of directors, Bakr bin Mohammed bin Laden, senior executives of SBG and others persons connected with the project until the completion of investigations and the issuance of judicial decisions in this regard.

title: King Orders Payments to Victims of Crane Accident in Makkah
content: The King also issues directives to host two family members of the deceased as the King's guests to perform Hajj next year. The injured who cannot perform Hajj this year can perform Hajj next year as the King's guests. The families of those hospitalized will be granted visas to take care of the injured during the remaining period of Hajj and return back to their country. Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz today issued an order to pay assistant to the families of the deceased as well as the injured in the crane accident at the Grand Mosque in Makkah. The family of the deceased or those who suffered permanent disability will receive SR 1 million ($267,000) and individuals who suffered other injuries will receive SR 500,000 ($133,333) in assistance. King Salman also issued directives that such payment would not deprive the families of the victims from filing private claims before the competent judicial authorities.

title: Over 1 Million Foreign Pilgrims have Arrived in the Kingdom
content: The General Directorate of Passports reported today that as of Sunday, Sept. 13, 1,030,584 pilgrims have arrived in the kingdom from abroad. 995,684 arrived by air; 4,111 by land, and 10,789 by sea.

title: Bad Weather Blamed for Crane Accident at the Grand Mosque
content: The Official Spokesman of the Presidency of the Grand Mosque and the Prophet Mosque Ahmed bin Mohammed Al-Mansouri said that it is believed that bad weather conditions, including strong sandstorms and torrential rains, were the reasons for the collapsed the of crane on the Grand Mosque.

title: 796,581 Foreign Pilgrims Arrived for this Year???Ć?Ös Hajj
content: The General Authority for Civil Aviation (GACA) has reported that as of yesterday, 796,581 pilgrims have arrived in the Kingdom since the beginning of this year???Ć?Ös Hajj season. 780,474 pilgrims arrived by air, 9,039 pilgrims by land, and 7,068 pilgrims by sea.

title: $667 Million to be Spent on Hajj Cleanup Operations
content: ??????Clean up of the holy sites during this year's Hajj expect to cost SR2.5 ($667 million), Municipal and Rural Affairs Minister Abdul Lateef Al-Asheikh announced yesterday.

title: Hajj Arrival Procedures to Drop to 45 Minutes at Jeddah Airport
content: The English daily reported that Sulaiman Al-Hamdan, president of the General Authority of Civil Aviation, has instructed all employees at both KAIA in Jeddah and Prince Muhammad bin Abdulaziz Airport in Madinah to exert all efforts to serve the pilgrims and visitors to the Prophet's Mosque. The Haj and Umrah terminal at KAIA has 143 immigration. Saudi Gazette reported today that airport officials have implemented an integrated plan to best serve the pilgrims. The plans calls for greater coordination with other government departments and the private sector. The new process is expected to reduce wait time from 4 hours to 45 minutes. Pilgrims arrival processing times will be cut to 45 minutes at Jeddah's King Abdulaziz International Airport (KAIA), officials announced.

title: First Batch of Pilgrims Arrive in Jeddah
content: The first batch of pilgrims, comprising of 107 people from South Africa, arrived at King Abdulaziz International Airport in Jeddah today.

title: King Salman Launches Five Projects within Third Expansion of the Grand Mosque
content: At the launching ceremony for the expansion projects, Sheikh Abdurrahman bin Abdulaziz Al-Sudais, General President of the Affairs of the Grand Mosque and the Prophet's Mosques, said that these historic achievements will have great and positive impact to highlight pioneering role of this blessed country and its Islamic message in spreading security, moderation, peace, love, tolerance, dialogue, harmony and convenience. The main building of the expansion project consists of six floors for praying, 680 escalators and 24 elevators for the people with special needs and 21,000 toilets and places of ablution. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz today launched five projects as part of the Third Saudi Expansion for the Grand Mosque in Makkah. The projects include expansion of buildings, squares, tunnels, service buildings and a ring road. The expansion include 1,470,000 square meters (15.8 million sq. ft.) and will accommodates 1.85 million worshipers.

title: Saudi Arabia Donates $66.7 Million to Combats Cholera in Yemen
content: Saudi Arabia has announced a donation of $66.7 million to the United Nations Children's Fund (UNICEF), the World Health Organization (WHO) and their partners in response to the cholera outbreak in Yemen.

title: Fire in Jizan Hospital Kills 25
content: A fire broke out at dawn today in Jazan General Hospital resulted in the death of 25 people and injury of 107 others. The Ministry of Health announced that all death from were due to a smoke inhalation. The spokesman of the Civil Defense Directorate in Jazan Province Maj. Yahya Al-Qahtani reported that twenty-one ambulances and fire units were sent to the hospital after an emergency call at 2:10 a.m. Maj. Al-Qahtani said that an investigation is still underway to determine the causes of the incident.

title: Saudi Arabia Joins Protocol on Eradicating Tobacco
content: The World Health Organization announced today that the Saudi Arabia has officially joined the WHO Framework Convention on Tobacco Control, including the Protocol to Eliminate Illicit Trade in Tobacco Products. Saudi Arabia has become the 11th country to accede to the Protocol. The other states are Austria, Republic of the Congo, Ecuador, Gabon, Mongolia, Nicaragua, Portugal, Spain, Turkmenistan, and Uruguay.

title: King Reviews Report on Makkah Crane Accident
content: Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz reviewed the report of the Accident Investigation Committee which concluded the absence of criminal suspicion and that the main reason for the accident is the strong winds while the crane was in a wrong position. King Salman issued directives to refer the results of the investigation to the Bureau of Investigation and Public Prosecution to complete investigation procedures with the Saudi Binladen Group (SBG). King Salman also issued directives to impose travel ban on all members of the SBG???Ć?Ös board of directors, Bakr bin Mohammed bin Laden, senior executives of SBG and others persons connected with the project until the completion of investigations and the issuance of judicial decisions in this regard.

title: King Orders Payments to Victims of Crane Accident in Makkah
content: The King also issues directives to host two family members of the deceased as the King's guests to perform Hajj next year. The injured who cannot perform Hajj this year can perform Hajj next year as the King's guests. The families of those hospitalized will be granted visas to take care of the injured during the remaining period of Hajj and return back to their country. Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz today issued an order to pay assistant to the families of the deceased as well as the injured in the crane accident at the Grand Mosque in Makkah. The family of the deceased or those who suffered permanent disability will receive SR 1 million ($267,000) and individuals who suffered other injuries will receive SR 500,000 ($133,333) in assistance. King Salman also issued directives that such payment would not deprive the families of the victims from filing private claims before the competent judicial authorities.

title: 34 Positive Confirmed Reported Cases of Corona
content: In its weekly statement, the Ministry of Health said that it recorded 34 positive confirmed cases of respiratory Middle East syndrome virus (Corona) during the past week, including 33 cases in Riyadh and one case in Najran.

title: Beneficiaries of Mohammad bin Naif Center to Spend Eid Holiday with their Families
content: A spokesman of the Ministry of Interior announced today that in implementation of the directives of Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif bin Abdulaziz, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior, the beneficiaries of the branches of the Mohammad bin Naif Center for Counseling and Care in Riyadh and Jeddah will be allowed to spend the blessed Eid Al-Fitr with their families. The spokesman said the directive will be implemented effective from Thursday, 22 Ramadan until 5 Shawwal 1436 AH. (July 9-21, 2015).

title: Saudi Health Centers Provide Medical Assistance to some 1,800 Patients a Week in Zaatari Camp
content: The Saudi National Campaign to Support Syrian Brothers funded medical centers in Zaatari refugee camp in Jordan treats 1,800 Syrian patients per week during Ramadan, Medical Director of the Saudi Specialized Clinics Dr. Muhammad Ismail Zoubi said today. The campaign provides health services, including psychological support, to Syrian refuges inside Syria and in neighboring countries.

title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet Session
content: Minister of Culture and Information Al Toraifi said that the Cabinet was briefed on the topics on its agenda, including topics in which the Shura Council participated in their study, and on the reports of both its General Committee and Experts' Bureau regarding them, and decided the following: approved an amendment the Civil Defense Regulation; amendment the name of the Saudi Commission for Tourism and Antiquities (SCTA) to the Saudi Commission for Tourism and National Heritage; approved the two mutual memorandums between the United Nations Secretary-General and the Foreign Minister regarding the actions that can be taken by the Kingdom with respect to diplomatic bags of the United Nations. The Cabinet praised the efforts of Prince Sultan bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, chairman of the board of directors of the Society for Children with Disability and all those interested in charitable and volunteering works as well as all contributors and supporters of such charitable deeds for what has been achieved by the Society. Turning to domestic issues, the Cabinet was briefed on the results of the 22nd Annual Meeting of Governors of Regions. The meeting, under the chairmanship of Crown Prince Mohammed bin Naif, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of the Interior, discussed plans to boost development and enhance services for citizens. This year???Ć?Ös agenda focused on modernization and development of Provincial System, ways to measure the performance of governors and chiefs of administrative centers, and the state of unplanned communities. The Cabinet extended its thanks and appreciation to King Salman for patronizing laying of the foundation stone ceremony of Khair Makkah charitable investment project allocated for Society for Children with Disability, and contributing SR 50 million ($13.33 million) for the project. The Cabinet was briefed on the results of the official visit by Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense, to France and his meeting with President Francois Hollande and senior French officials.?é?? The Cabinet expressed the Kingdom???Ć?Ös condemnation of the continuing acts of violence, discourse of hatred, incitement and racial discrimination against the Muslim community in Myanmar. The Cabinet stressed that the humanitarian tragedy being experienced by Rohingya Muslims and their exposure to an ethnic cleansing campaigns require urgent action from the international community to call on the Government of Myanmar to adopt a transparent and comprehensive policy to stop all practices of persecution and deportation, and to speed the recognition of Rohingya Muslims minority without any discrimination or ethnic classification. The Cabinet re-emphasized in this regard, the Kingdom's rejection, condemnation and denunciation of all forms of violence and terrorism, and its solidarity and stand with sisterly and friendly countries in confronting terrorist acts to maintain their security and stability. The Cabinet also condemned today's terrorist bombing in Cairo which killed Egypt???Ć?Ös Prosecutor General Hisham Barakat. The Cabinet expressed its deepest condolences and sympathy to the government of Egypt and people, and to the family of the deceased, praying to Allah to have mercy on him and to inspire his family patience and solace. Minister of Culture and Information Dr. Adel Al Toraifi said following the session that the Cabinet reviewed the latest regional and international developments. The Kingdom re-affirming it condemnation of the terrorist bombings at the Imam Al-Sadeq Mosque in Kuwait, the attack at the Tunisian seaside resort of Sousse, and the terrorist attack at a chemical plant near Lyon, France. The Cabinet stressed that such terrorist acts are contrary to all Islamic values and principles and expressed its condolences to the governments and people of Kuwait, Tunisia, and France, and the families of the victims and wished a speedy recovery for the injured. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz chaired the Cabinet's session at Al-Salam Palace in Jeddah on this evening. At the outset of the session, King Salman briefed the Cabinet on the telephone conversation he had with President Nursultan Nazarbayev of Kazakhstan, and his meeting with the Secretary General of the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) Dr. Abdul Latif bin Rashid Al-Zayani.

title: Separation Surgery on Yemeni Conjoined Twins to Start Friday
content: As an extension of the humanitarian initiatives adopted by the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia towards the people of Yemen, and on the directives of Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz, separation surgery on Yemeni conjoined twins will be performed Friday. The surgery is expected to last up to 12 hours with the participation of nearly 30 specialists from the medical departments of anesthesia, surgery of children, urology, orthopedic, plastic, nursing, technicians and paramedics, adviser at the Royal Court, General Supervisor of King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Aid and head of the medical and surgical team, Dr. Abdullah Al-Rabiah said.

title: Saudi Clinics Treat some 350 Patients a Day at Al-Zaatari Camp in Jordan
content: Saudi Specialist Clinics provided medical services to some two thousand displaced Syrians at Al-Zaatari Camp in Jordan last week. The clinics treats an average of 300-350 patients a day. The clinics???Ć?pharmacies dispense on average over 1,300 prescriptions a day.

title: Foreign Ministry Issues Travel Warning to Iran
content: The Ministry also urges the Iranian government to shoulder its responsibilities towards accelerating the results of the investigation with full transparency, along with the provision of the medical care for the injured and the provision of the necessary protection for Saudi nationals in Iran. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs today warned Saudi citizens of the risks of traveling to Iran following the death of four and the injury to 29 Saudi citizens from poisoning in the Iranian city of Mashhad. Due to the mysterious circumstances surrounding the poisoning and lack of transparency in the investigation, the Foreign Ministry calls upon Saudi citizens traveling to Iran to take the utmost caution to preserve their safety and security as well as their families from any harm.

title: Foreign Ministry Summons Iranian Ambassador After Poisoning of Saudis
content: Ambassador Nugali added that Kingdom???Ć?Ös charge d???Ć?Öaffaires in Tehran visited the victims and to offer support. The Saudi Foreign Ministry summoned Iran's ambassador today after four Saudis died from poisoning in the northeast Iranian city of Mashhad. Ministry spokesman Ambassador Osama Nugali said in a statement that the Kingdom expressed deep concern over the death of the four children and the injury of 28 other Saudis, and urged the Iranian authorities to conduct a thorough investigation and reveal all circumstances surrounding the poisoning. Ambassador Nugali also said the Kingdom demanded that Iran cooperate with the Saudi embassy in Tehran and the Consulate General in Mashhad in order to allow Saudi embassy staff to follow up the medical condition of the patients and to provide all the necessary facilities and care needed by the Saudi citizens in Iran as well as to provide the necessary protection for them.

title: Interior Ministry Bans Citizens from Traveling to Ebola-Affected Countries
content: The Ministry of Interior has announced a travel ban against citizens to countries affected by Ebola. General Director of Public Relations and Media at the Ministry of Interior Maj. Gen. Dr. Mohammed bin Abdullah Al-Marul said that a High Directive has been issued banning Saudi citizens from traveling to Guinea, Sierra Leone and Liberia. Emergency travel to these countries will be considered on a case-by-case basis by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs in coordination with the Ministry of Health. Those permitted to travel, will be subject to preventive measures upon their arrival from the affected countries.

title: Royal Court: King's Efforts on Al-Aqsa are Culminated With Success
content: The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia affirms the right of Muslims in Al-Aqsa Holy Mosque and the performance of their worship in it with ease and tranquility. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman stressed the need for the return of calm at Al-Aqsa Mosque and its surroundings, the respect for the sanctity of the place, and that Muslims must return to enter the mosque and perform worship in it with all security, tranquility and peace effective from today. The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia emphasizes the importance of achieving a just and comprehensive peace for the Palestinian cause in accordance with the Arab peace initiative, the vision of a two-state solution and the relevant resolutions of the international legitimacy. In reference to the events that took place at Al-Aqsa Holy Mosque during the last period, the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz has held necessary contacts with a number of leaders of the world over the past few days.?é?? The Government of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia has also contacted the Administration of the United States of America to exert its efforts for not closing Al-Aqsa Mosque in front of Muslims, not preventing them from performing their duties and prayers in it, and canceling restrictions being imposed on the entry to the Mosque. These efforts have been culminated with success today and in a way that contributes to restore stability and tranquility to worshipers and preserve their dignity and security. The Royal Court issued today the following statement:

title: Embassy Hosts Annual Iftar
content: Saudi Deputy Chief of Mission to the U.S. Sami Al-Sadhan this evening hosted the traditional Iftar for Arab and Muslim ambassador at the Saudi Embassy in Washington. The event was also attended by Muslim leaders and members of the Saudi community in Washington.

title: Crown Prince Orders Distribute of Ramadan Breakfast Baskets to the Needy in Somalia
content: Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior launched Ramadan 1438 Project for Somalia to feed the needy.?é???é??Regional Director of the Saudi National Campaign for Relief of Somali People Sa'ad Al-Swayed said that group has allocated over 40,000 Ramadan baskets worth about $3.5 million for distributed in Somalia.

title: OIC, WML  Condemn Terrorist Attacks on Egypt's Coptic Church
content: Both Islamic organizations reaffirmed their resolute stand against terrorism in all its forms and manifestations. In a statement, the Secretary General of MWL Sheikh Dr. Mohammed Al-Isa said attacks against places of worship go against the authentic Islamic religion and its teachings of tolerance, but fail to undermine the unity of the Egyptian people. The Jeddah-based Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) and Makkah-based Muslim World League condemned?é?? the terrorist attacks on Mari Girgis (St. George's) Church in Tanta, Egypt, as a heinous crime against all Egyptians.

title: King Salman Leaves Indonesia on His Way to Japan
content: Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz today left Indonesia on his way to Japan on an official visit.

title: OIC Strongly Condemns Terrorist Attacks in Burkina Faso
content: Dr. Othaimeen has expressed his concern vis-?ā??-vis the persistence of such acts at the common borders of Burkina Faso and Mali and has called for better counter-terrorism coordination in the sub-region, encouraging the implementation of the joint force between Burkina Faso, Mali and Niger in order to fight insecurity in the area of Liptako-Gourma. In a statement, OIC Secretary General, Dr. Yousef A. Othaimeen, indicated that those people who use Islam in a fraudulent way to justify their terrorist acts are criminals who must be pursued and brought to justice for punishment. He pointed out that the principles of Islam are completely opposed to hatred and violence. The Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) has strongly condemned the Tuesday's terrorist attacks by Ansarul Islam of Ibrahim Malam Dicko against two police stations in Soum Province in northern Burkina Faso, where a civilian was wounded and some buildings and vehicles were burned.

title: Saudi Minister of Hajj and Umrah Meets Iranian Hajj Officials
content: Minister of Hajj and Umrah Dr. Mohammad Saleh Bin Taher Benten met in Riyadh today Chairman of Iranian Hajj and Visit Organization Dr. Hameid Mohammadi to discuss Iranian pilgrims affairs arrangements for the this year???Ć?Ös Hajj season.

title: Deputy Islamic Affairs Minister Receives Visiting USCIRF Delegation
content: At the end of the meeting, the members of USCIRF delegation praised the efforts being exerted by the Ministry of Islamic Affairs in the fight against extremism and terrorism, wishing to strengthen bilateral relations between the Commission and the Ministry to serve the principle of freedom. During the meeting, Dr. Al-Sudairy explained the overall rights and freedoms being guaranteed by Islam, the tolerance of Islam and its great values in all aspects of life, including the guarantee of freedom and peaceful coexistence among the followers of other religions. Deputy Minister of Islamic Affairs, Call and Guidance Dr. Tawfiq Abdulaziz Al-Sudairy received in Riyadh today a delegation from United States Commission on International Religious Freedom (USCIRF).

title: King Salman to Cover Cost of Jordanian With Special Needs Who Wish to Perform Umrah
content: ?é?? The Saudi Embassy in Amman today launched the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz?é??Umrah program for people with special needs.?é?? The program is in collaboration with the Handicapped Child Care Charity Association in Irbid, Jordan and the Medical Educational Center.?é??

title: Turkish Prime Minister Arrives in Medinah
content: Turkish Prime Minister Ahmet Davutoglu arrived today in Madinah to visit the Prophet's Mosque. During his arrival at Prince Mohammed bin Abdulaziz International Airport, he was received by Undersecretary of Al-Madinah Province Abdul Mohsen Al-Muneef.

title: King Salman Calls Indonesian President
content: The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques made today a telephone call to Indonesian President Joko Widodo. The King expressed sincere condolences on the victims of the terrorist attacks witnessed by Jakarta city yesterday and left several people dead and scores injured.

title: Islamic Relief Organization Conducts More than 500 Eye Surgeries in Morocco
content: The Jeddah-based the International Islamic Relief Organization (IIRO) of Muslim World League (MWL) has conducted 529 cataract surgery, including replacing lens with artificial lens. The medical team also performed eye examinations and distribution of glasses to a large numbers of patients from various towns and villages of Morocco. The medical team perform on average about 100 operations a day.

title: Supreme Magistracy Council Calls Islamic Military Alliance a Blessing
content: ???Ć?£The Islamic Alliance announces to the whole world that terrorism has no religion, and all have suffered from it, which requires decision-making process on the ground aiming at combating terrorism and responding militarily in accordance with the programs and mechanisms to support the joint efforts of countries allied to stand united against this serious challenge that surrounds the nations, undermines the security and threatens their stability.
