title: Saudi Campaign Distributes over 230,000 Winter Items to Syrians Refugees in Jordan
content: The Saudi National Campaign for the Support of Brothers in Syria has distributed 231,049 winter items to 73,401 Syrian refugees in Al-Zaatari Refugee Camp in Jordan as part of its winter campaign.

title: Foreign Minister receives UN Envoy to Yemen
content: Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir received in Riyadh today Special Envoy of UN Secretary-General to Yemen Ismail Ould Cheikh Ahmed.?é?? During the meeting, they discussed the latest developments on the Yemeni arena.

title: Saudi Arabia Steps Up Assistance to Care for Ill and Injured in Yemen
content: The organization recently transferred a group of injured Yemenis from Taiz for treatment in Sudan, paying for the full cost of their travel and treatment. KSRelief has executed 51 projects in health, nutrition, water and environmental sanitation in Yemen totaling more than $208 million and benefiting 24.5 million Yemeni citizens, in addition to programs providing medical care and treatment for people inside Yemeni territory. KSRelief has entered into a number of agreements with private hospitals in Aden and Taiz aimed at providing full medical care for more than 750 wounded in these provinces. KSRelief seeks to activate the role of government hospitals to treat the injured inside Yemeni territories by providing medical services and treatment devices. Saudi Arabia, working through KSRelief, is the largest donor of humanitarian assistance to the Yemeni people. KSRelief was established in May 2015 under the sponsorship of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz. (WASHINGTON, DC) ???Ć?The King Salman Humanitarian Aid & Relief Center (KSRelief) recently sent 58 trucks carrying medical supplies and equipment to a number of provinces in Yemen. Of these, 11 trucks delivered supplies to Republic Hospital in Aden and six trucks to Mareb Hospital.

title: Saudi Arabia Leads International Efforts to Save Children, Rehabilitate Yemen???Ć?Ös Health Sector
content: In collaboration with the World Health Organization (WHO), KSRELIEF provided basic services such as diabetes and cancer medicines, and support the operation of emergency rooms by providing 350,000 gallon of fuel to 88 hospitals and 19 health facilities in Yemen. To date, KSRELIEF provided 12,000,000 vaccines for Yemeni children against measles and rubella diseases and tetanus and whooping cough. KSRELIEF has sent 58 trucks carrying over 600 tons of medical and therapeutic supplies to all Yemeni provinces, starting with Aden and Marib.?é?? KSRELIEF also worked closely with the UN International Children???Ć?Ös Emergency Fund (UNICEF) in training 3,153 health workers on the social management of acute malnutrition and opened 854 centers for therapeutic nutrition program. The program also provided medical treatment and follow up cases of acute malnutrition.?é?? KSRELIEF mobile health teams have treated over a quarter of a million children suffering from acute malnutrition and provided immunization and postnatal care to over 1.2 million children throughout Yemen.?é?? The teams also provided services for pregnant women and post-delivery care for over 220,000 women. The Riyadh-based King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Centre (KSRELIEF) is leading international efforts to rehabilitate Yemen???Ć?Ös health sector which has nearly been destroyed since the Houthi militias and the forces loyal to deposed president Ali Abdullah Saleh usurped power in Sana???Ć?Öa.?é?? The United Nations detailed Houthi militias???Ć?gross violations of international humanitarian law, including attacks on hospitals and medical facilities.?é?? Houthi militias and Saleh loyalists have also prevented medical supplies from reached the needy.

title: GCC Chief Condemns Terror Attack on Police Bus in Bahrain
content: Dr. Al-Zayani described the incident as a cowardly criminal act that contradict with all human principles, expressing confidence that the Bahraini security is capable of finding the perpetrators of this crime and bringing them to justice.?é?? He reiterated GCC???Ć?Ös solidarity with Bahrain in all of the security measures taken to maintain the safety and security of its citizens, wishing the injured speedy recovery. title: Foreign Minister in Iraq for Talks Prime Minister Al-Abadi
content: The foreign minister congratulated Iraq on its achievements in countering terrorism, stressing the desire of both countries to eliminate the scourge of terrorism. During the meeting, they discussed issues of common interest in addition to means of enhancing bilateral relations.

title: Houthi, Saleh Militias Destroyed 1,700 Schools and Recruited Thousands of Students
content: The minister further said dispatching students program to Iran is being stopped which had already been approved by Houthis through sending students on scholarship to Iran whose number is estimated at 7,000 Yemeni students currently in Qom, Iran. ?é??Dr. Lamlas?é??pointing out that the Yemeni Ministry of Education will stand up against any attempt to implement a foreign agenda in Yemen. The Yemeni minister said that the war being waged by Houthi militias has destroyed the project of developing basic education accomplished over the past 15 years and financially supported by some regional and foreign countries along with international agencies, pointing out that through cooperation and assistance being provided by King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Aid, United Arab Emirates Red Crescent and some relief organizations, the rehabilitation and maintenance of destroyed schools in liberated areas are being carried out. He explained that the children who were forced to fight in the ranks of the Houthi militias would be given settlement programs to be enrolled with the rest of their colleagues, in compensation for school years they missed, noting that there are more than 1,700 schools destroyed by Houthi militias during the war and turned them into military barracks. Yemeni Minister of Education, Dr. Abdullah Lamlas, has stressed in previous statements that the ministry is working in full swing to promote the educational system and won't accredit any certificates for students who have been granted certificates after changing curriculums in Sana'a and the addition of new subjects that may serve the agenda of the Houthi and Saleh militias, pointing out that the standard will be only through the cumulative grade point average. On its part, the Ministry of Education of Yemen is exerting great efforts when developed a special program for a comprehensive rehabilitation of all students. Houthi militias have deprived more than 2.5 million Yemeni students of education, as a result of wars being waged across various governorates of Yemen; in addition to the displacement of hundreds of thousands of people and their closure of thousands of schools while hundreds of them have been turned into military barracks. During the period of the coup, education crisis has worsened, especially as a lot of students have been deprived of their schools due to the conversion of the remaining ones to evacuation centers for displaced people, which was confirmed by a report of United Nations International Children's Fund (UNICEF) that 3,584 schools were closed because of security risks or for being converted into military barracks by Houthi militias and Saleh forces and about 22 government and private universities have been damaged directly and indirectly. Educational system in Yemen is facing a major challenge in restoring the legitimacy as one of the most important pathways in building a new Yemen after Houthi militias?é??and former president Ali Abdullah Saleh forces?é??have led to a deep rift in the educational structure, in terms of material, moral and intellectual aspects, which notably represented in changing curriculums and introduction of revolutionary, sectarian, hostile tendencies thereof.

title: Coalition to Investigate Houthi Claims Civilians Killed in Air Raid
content: The Coalition Supporting Legitimacy in Yemen announced today that it will investigate Houthi claims that civilians were killed during an air raid on a target near the Yemeni capital Sana???Ć?Öa.?é?? In a statement issued in Riyadh, the Coalition said ???Ć?£We are aware of media reports that Houthi militias are claiming that Yemeni civilians were killed in an air raid overnight near Sana'a. There has been fighting between Yemeni armed forces and rebels in this area in recent days. We are investigating the reports and will release further information when we have it.???Ć?

title: Houthi Militias Mining Areas as They Retreat
content: The King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Aid (KSRELIEF) is carrying out numerous projects to alleviate the suffering of all civilians in Yemen, including supporting rehabilitation of injured civilians?é??and supporting International Committee of Red Cross's (ICRC) artificial limbs centers in Yemen. Houthi militias and forces loyal to deposed president Ali Abdullah Salah are minding areas liberate by the Yemeni national army in an effort to slow the advance of pro-government forces. The mines have already claimed the lives of many civilians and prevented the return of others.?é?? Former governor of Al-Jawf Governorate Hussein Awadi said that he believed that the Houthi militias and their allies?é??had planted over 30 thousand mines with varied sizes in the governorate.?é?? Saudi-led Arab Coalition to Support Legitimacy in Yemen are working with the Yemeni national army to clear landmines to help conflict-affected communities to return to their homes and rebuild their lives.

title: Saudi-Led Coalition in Yemen: Safeguarding Civilians Remains Foremost Priority
content: The Coalition cited an August 2016 United Nations report that accused Houthi militias and coup forces of consistently violating the Geneva Convention by using civilians as human shields. Moreover, the Coalition stated that rebel forces use child soldiers and use revenge killings and illegal detentions to terrorize Yemen???Ć?Ös population. The Coalition said that it works alongside United Nations agencies and the International Committee of the Red Cross as part of a collaborative approach to minimize the possibility of harm to civilians, medical personnel, journalists and relief organizations. Among the instituted mechanisms, the Coalition said that it has adopted???Ć?öand has been following???Ć?öthe policy to issue repeated warnings to Houthi militias and Ali Abdullah Saleh forces so that they can evacuate cities prior to a Coalition air strike. The Coalition also has heightened its emphasis on safeguarding critical infrastructure and, as a result, civilian lives in Yemen???Ć?Ös urban centers. The Coalition???Ć?Ös Joint Incidents Assessment Team (JIAT) is composed of military members, weapons experts and legal specialists in humanitarian rights. The independent group reviews reports of Coalition activities that are known to have led to civilian casualties, and offers recommendations for ways to avoid future incidents. (WASHINGTON, DC) ???Ć?In a statement issued today, the Saudi-led Arab Coalition to Support Legitimacy in Yemen restated its commitment to protecting civilians in Yemen and underscored the steps the alliance has taken to avoid causalities in an ongoing war zone. Among those steps, the Coalition has launched an independent assessment team, fostered active partnerships with relief organizations and focused on safeguarding Yemen???Ć?Ös urban centers as part of a concerted effort to protect civilians and uphold international humanitarian law.

title: Foreign Minister Meets with UN Secretary General
content: Minister of Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir met with UN Secretary General Antonio Guterres at the United Nations in New York.?é?? During the meeting, Minister Al-Jubeir stressed the Kingdom's keenness on working with the UN to meet the challenges facing the region and the world, including the Middle East peace process and the crisis in Yemen and Syria.

title: King Salman Relief Center Distributes 20,000 Food Baskets in Hodeidah
content: On January 9, KSRELIEF began the distribution of 300,000 food baskets in Hodeida Province to benefit 460,000 needy people. King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Centre (KSRELIEF) has recently distributed 20,000 food baskets in Hodeidah Province to meet the needs of 120,000 Yemeni civilians. The food baskets are being distributed through local partners.

title: Saudi Navy Frigate Attacked by Houthi Suicide Boats
content: ?é?? The command declares that the continuation of the Houthi militias' use of the port of Hodeida as a launching pad for terrorist operations is a serious development that would affect international navigation and the flow of humanitarian and medical assistance into the port for Yemeni population. The coalition said that the Saudi frigate dealt with the boats as necessary. However, one of the suicide boats managed to collide and detonate explosives against the rear of the frigate, killing two sailors and wounding three others.?é?? The explosion also resulted in a fire which the crew extinguished.?é?? The command added that the frigate continued its patrol in the area of operations while coalition air forces and the naval forces continued to chase the fleeing boats. The Command of the Coalition to Support Legitimacy in Yemen announced today that two Saudi navy sailors were killed in Houthi suicide boat attack on a Saudi frigate operating in the Red Sea.?é?? The command said that a Royal Saudi Naval frigate came under a terrorist attack by three Houthi suicide boats while on patrol west of the port of Hodeida.

title: Foreign Minister Underscored the Strength of Saudi-US Relations, Addressing Continued and Expanded Areas of Cooperation
content: ???Ć?ØOur hope is that wisdom will prevail and the Congress will do the right thing and make the adjustments,???Ć?the foreign minister added. ???Ć?£The country that has the most to lose from dilution of sovereign immunities is the United States itself. And American officials know this. Because America has the largest footprint in the world, they operate all over the world. They???Ć?Öre fighting wars all over the world. They provide weapons to countries all over the world. If that principle is eroded, then the US could be sued in virtually every country in the world.???Ć?Minister Al-Jubeir said that the Justice Against Sponsors of Terrorism Act (JASTA) legislation passed by the U.S. Congress last year has undercut the foundation of sovereign immunity, turning ???Ć?£the international order into the law of the jungle. On the Israeli-Palestinian peace talks, Minister Al-Jubeir stressed that the Arab Peace Initiative remains on the table. ???Ć?£Our hope is that there will be enough courage and enough political will on both sides to be able to move to settling this longest conflict in our region,???Ć?he said. Minister Al-Jubeir said that Iran must be held accountable for its support of terrorism, violation of ballistic missile arrangements and for its interference is the affairs of the countries in the region. ???Ć?£Iran has been single-handedly the most important supporter of terrorism in the region. Iran has supported, created and supported Hezbollah. Iran has harbored terrorists,???Ć?said the foreign minister. Speaking at the World Economic Forum in Davos, Foreign Minister Al-Jubeir said "the U.S. and Saudi Arabia have had a historic strategic partnership, if not alliance, since World War II; that relationship continues. We expect it to broaden and deepen and grow stronger. Our interests are aligned when it comes to the issues of the region.?é?? Minister Al-Jubeir added that the Kigdom and the U.S. "have a very, very strong people-to-people relationships with the U.S., and I expect that this will continue.???Ć?

title: King Salman holds talks with Lebanese President in Riyadh
content: On the Lebanese side, the talks were attended by Minister of Foreign Affairs and Expatriates Jibran Bassil; Minister of Education and Higher Education Marwan Hamadeh; Minister of Finance Ali Hassan Khalil; Minister of National Defense Yaqoub Al-Sarraf; Minister of Interior and Municipalities Nihad Al-Mashnouq; Minister of State for Presidential Affairs Pierre Raffoul; Minister of Information Melhem Riashy; Minister of Economy and Trade Raed Khoury; and Lebanese Ambassador to the Kingdom Abdul-Sattar Issa. The meetings was attended by Prince Faisal bin Bandar bin Abdulaziz, Governor of Riyadh Province; Minister of State and Royal Advisor Prince Dr. Mansour bin Miteb bin Abdulaziz; Prince Miteb bin Abdullah bin Abdulaziz, Minister of National Guard; Minister of State Dr. Musaed bin Mohammed Al-Aiban; Minister of State Dr. Ibrahim bin Abdulaziz Al-Assaf; Minister of Culture and Information Dr. Adel bin Zaid Altoraifi; Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir; Minister of Finance Mohammed bin Abdullah Al-Jadaan; Minister of State for Arab Gulf Affairs Thamer bin Sabhan Al-Sabhan and Saudi Charge' D'Affaires to Lebanon Walid bin Abdullah Bukhari. During the meeting, they reviewed bilateral relations between the two countries, ways of enhancing them in various fields, and latest developments at Arab and international arenas. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz held at Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh today official talks with President Michel Aoun of Lebanon.

title: KSRELIEF Distributes 300,000 Food Baskets in Yemen
content: The Riyadh-based King Salman bin Center for Relief and Humanitarian Works (KSRELIEF) is currently embarked on distributing 300,000 food baskets in Hodeida Province of Yemen.?é?? The Center has already has already executed phase one of 20,000 food baskets in 15 directorates through local partners as well as 150,000 food baskets benefiting 460,000 persons.

title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet's Session
content: The Cabinet authorized the General Commission for Statistics to conduct the population general census for 2020. The Cabinet approved the incentives and privileges offered to donors who purchase antiquities or masterpieces or special collections displayed in national museums; approved the regulatory provisions of the General Authority for Sports; The Cabinet approved the security cooperation agreement with Djibouti signed in Riyadh on 10/07/1437 AH; approved the memorandum of understanding (MoU) between the Ministry of Interior???Ć?Ös Saudi Financial Investigations Administration (SAFA) and the Financial Investigation Unit (FIU) of India as regards cooperation on the exchange of the investigations related to money laundering, terror financing and related crimes, signed in Riyadh on 25/06/1437 AH; approved MoU for cooperation in the field of renewable energy between King Abdullah City for Atomic and Renewable Energy and China???Ć?Ös National Power Management, signed in Riyadh on 09/04/1437H. The Cabinet finally adjourned after discussing a number of domestic issues. The Council strong condemnation of the murder of Russia's Ambassador to Turkey, Andrey Karlov and the terror attack in Berlin, Germany, that resulted in the death of 12 people.?é?? The Cabinet also the double terrorist bombing of a market in Baghdad and the attack in Istanbul.?é?? The Cabinet offered its condolences to the families of the victims. The Cabinet also reiterated the Kingdom's appreciation to U.S. Secretary of State John Kerry???Ć?Ös statement on the final solution of the Palestinian-Israeli conflict, as well as the adoption by Security Council Resolution 2334, which condemns Israeli settlement and calls for stopping it in the occupied Palestinian territories. The Cabinet also discussed the outcome of the 97th OPEC meeting in Cairo and commitment by OPEC members to honor the November 2016 agreement on reducing oil production. The Cabinet welcomed Security Council Resolution 2328 on the deployment of international observers in Aleppo to supervise the evacuation of civilians. The Cabinet was briefed on a number of reports including the emergency meeting of the Executive Committee of Foreign Ministers of Organization of Islamic Cooperation that the massacres being committed in Aleppo, Syrian, can be classified as a war crime and crimes against humanity. King Salman briefed the Cabinet on the results of his two phone calls with King Abdullah II bin Al-Hussein of Jordan and President Abed Rabbo Hadi of Yemen, as well as his two meetings with Palestinian President Mahmud Abbas and Chairman of the Board of Directors of Al-Quds Fund Muneeb Al-Masri, in addition to the messages received from King of Jordan; Emir of Kuwait and President of Comoros. The Cabinet appreciated King Salman???Ć?Ös order to organize a popular campaign for the Syrian people relief of the Syrian people.?é?? King Salman???Ć?Ös directive to allocate SR 100 million ($26.67 million) for the campaign and his personal SR 20 million ($5.33 million) donation.?é?? The Cabinet also express its appreciation to the SR 10 million ($2.67 million) donation by Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif and the SR 10 million ($2.67 million) Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman???Ć?Ös SR 8 million ($2.13 million) to alleviate the sufferings of Syrian people, especially the displaced from Aleppo. The Cabinet began by congratulating King Salman on the second anniversary of his ascension to the throne.?é?? The Cabinet then commended the State???Ć?Ös general budget for the fiscal year 2017; for its sufficient strength to cope with economic and financial challenges. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz chaired the Cabinet's session at Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh on Monday afternoon.

title: Kingdom Condemns Terrorist Attack On Prison in Bahrain
content: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs strongly condemned the yesterday???Ć?Ös terrorist attack on a prison in Bahrain which resulted in the death of a security officer. The Foreign Ministry reiterated the Kingdom's solidarity with Bahrain and its firm stance against terrorism and extended deep condolences to the relatives of the martyred security man, the government and people of Bahrain.

title: King Salman Receives Phone Call From Yemeni President
content: The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz received on Sunday a telephone call from Yemeni President Abed Rabbo Hadi.?é?? During the call, President Hadi congratulated the King Salman the second anniversary of his accession to the throne and praised the King Salman in support of the Yemeni people in restoring legitimacy to Yemen.

title: Kingdom Welcomes US Secretary of State Kerry's Proposals on Final Settlement of Palestinian-Israel Conflict
content: Saudi Arabia today welcomed the proposals put forth by U.S. Secretary of State John Kerry on a final settlement of the Palestinian-Israeli conflict. In a statement, the Saudi Foreign Ministry said the Kingdom views the proposals as being in accord with the majority of the resolutions of international legality and most of the elements of the Arab Peace Initiative adopted at the Arab Summit in Beirut in 2002 and by the Islamic Summit in Makkah in 2005, and that the proposals represent an appropriate basis for achieving a final settlement of the Palestinian-Israeli conflict.

title: King Salman to Cover Cost of Jordanian With Special Needs Who Wish to Perform Umrah
content: ?é?? The Saudi Embassy in Amman today launched the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz?é??Umrah program for people with special needs.?é?? The program is in collaboration with the Handicapped Child Care Charity Association in Irbid, Jordan and the Medical Educational Center.?é??

title: King Salman Ordered a Relief Campaign for the Syrian People
content: King Salan kicked off the campaign by donating SR 20 million, Crown Prince Mohammed bin Naif donated SR 10 million, and Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman donated SR 8 million. King Salman dedicated SR 100 million ($27 million) for the campaign.?é?? The campaign will be coordinated by the King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Works which will set up collection centers throughout the Kingdom to collect monetary and in-kind donations. The Royal Court announced today that the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz has ordered the formation of a relief campaign to help the suffering Syrian brethren, especially those evacuated from Aleppo and other areas, who have been displaced and because of the difficult situation they face.?é?? The campaign will begin Tuesday, December 27, 2016.

title: Ministry of Interior: Solder Killed by Houthi Militia Fire Inside Saudi Arabia
content: The Ministry of Interior announced today that 1 pm local time, a border outpost came under fire from Houthi militias from Yemeni territory.?é?? The ministry added that the Saudi Army responded and neutralized the source of the fire.?é???é?? The exchange resulted in death of Army Private Sultan bin Ayed Farhan Al-Sharari.

title: Foreign Ministry Welcomes UN Security Council Resolution Condemning Israeli Settlements
content: The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia hoped that the UN resolution will contribute to reviving the peace process in the region on the basis of relevant UN resolutions and Arab Peace Initiative, which will result in the establishment of an independent Palestinian State with East Jerusalem as its capital. In a statement, the Foreign Ministry said that the UN resolution confirms beyond any doubt that Israeli settlements on Palestinian territory, including East Jerusalem, have "no legal validity". The Ministry of Foreign Affairs today commended United Nations Security Council Resolution 2334 condemning the construction of Israeli settlements and demanding a halt of Israeli settlements on occupied Palestinian territory.

title: King Salman Offers Condolences to Jordanian King on Victims of Terrorist Attack in Karak
content: Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz held a telephone conversation yesterday with King Abdullah II Bin Al-Hussein of Jordan in which he offered his condolences to the victims of the terrorist attack Karak.?é?? King Salman condemned this criminal act, stressing the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia's support for the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan in the fight against extremism and the fight against terrorism.

title: Foreign Minister Al-Jubeir Call For Greater Pressure on Syrian Regime
content: The Arab foreign ministers said in their emergency meeting on Syria that the continuation of the tragic situation in Syria poses a threat to Arab national security and global stability, stressing the need to impose the necessary pressure on the Syrian regime to respond to the will of the international community represented in the UN resolutions. Addressed the extraordinary session of the Foreign Ministers of the Arab League in Cairo, Minister Al-Jubeir called on putting pressure on the Syrian regime to reach a political solution. Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir said today?é?? that the ???Ć?£Syrian regime is the one who refused to enter into any serious negotiations, continued the killing of his own people and brought terrorist organizations and sectarian militias into Syria, including the Iranian Revolutionary Guard."

title: Saudi Arabia Pays Share to Palestinian Authority's Budget for Eight Months
content: Saudi Development Fund has deposited $61.6 million to the Palestinian Ministry of Finance's account. Saudi Ambassador to Egypt, Ahmed Kattan explained that the amount represents the value of the Kingdom's monthly contributions to support the Palestinian National Authority's budget.?é?? The payment is for?é??April through?é??November?é??2016.

title: Ambassador to Egypt Conveys Kingdom's Condolences to Pope Tawadros II on Terrorist Attack
content: Ambassador Kattan stressed the Kingdom's denunciation and condemnation of this cowardly terrorist act, confirming the Kingdom's solidarity with Egypt and the Egyptian people against anyone who tries to undermine its security and stability. Ahmad bin Abdulaziz Kattan, Saudi Ambassador to Egypt and Permanent Representative of the Kingdom to the Arab League has visited Pope Tawadros II, Pope of Alexandria and Patriarch, at St. Mark Cathedral to conveyed condolences of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz; Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior, and Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense, to Pope Tawadros II on the victims of the terrorist bombing targeting St. Peter Church in Cairo.

title: Saudi King Says Nation Is Committed to Regional Security and Economic Development
content: ???Ć?£I am optimistic about a better tomorrow,???Ć?King Salman said. ???Ć?£These conditions that we are now experiencing are not more difficult than their previous ones. We will overpass them to a better and brilliant future.???Ć?King Salman noted that economic reforms implemented to date have focused on diversifying the nation???Ć?Ös economy and re-distributing resources to expand job opportunities. Saudi Arabia has ???Ć?£come out of [the past three years] with a strong economy and continuous growth; we have set out our reforms today to look forward to the future,???Ć?he noted. The strength of these structural reforms fall under Vision 2030, Saudi Arabia???Ć?Ös economic transformation plan. The region???Ć?Ös stability directly impacts the Kingdom???Ć?Ös economic development, and ensuring both remains fundamental, King Salman stressed. ???Ć?£Our domestic policy is based on pillars of maintaining security, stability and prosperity to achieve the development that meets the needs of the present and preserves the right of future generations.???Ć????Ć?£We in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia believe that the security of our dear neighbor Yemen is the security of the Kingdom, and will not accept any interference in its internal affairs,???Ć?he stated. King Salman pledged to uphold Yemen???Ć?Ös right to security. Yemen, he said, cannot be the ???Ć?£corridor???Ć?for countries trying to undermine the security of the Kingdom, nor can Saudi Arabia allow other nations to undermine its sovereignty and security. Among the core foreign policy priorities, King Salman vowed to continue cooperating with the international community in combatting the extremism that threatens its borders and the region at large. Political solutions to international crises can safeguard values of tolerance and coexistence, paving the way for nations to ensure peace and achieve development, King Salman said. In his annual address to the Consultative Council, King Salman presented an optimistic outlook for the nation???Ć?Ös foreign and domestic policies. He said that Saudi Arabia has played an active and constructive role in the region and in important global undertakings with the international community is proceeding with ???Ć?£firm steps???Ć?to maintain its active role in the region and around the world. (WASHINGTON, DC) ???Ć?The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz reinforced today that Saudi Arabia remains fully committed to acting vigorously to ensure its security and the security of the region while moving forward on the Kingdom???Ć?Ös economic development and reforms.

title: GCC Leaders Conclude 37th Summit in Bahrain
content: The Leaders of Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) concluded their 37th Summit of the GCC Supreme Council in Manama, Bahrian, this afternoon.?é?? The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz headed the Kingdom???Ć?Ös delegation to the summit.

title: GCC Leaders Hold Joint Meeting With British Prime Minister
content: Leaders of the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) held at today a joint meeting with the British Prime Minister Theresa May in Manama in the framework of the 37th Summit of the GCC Supreme Council.?é?? The Saudi Arabia???Ć?Ös delegation was headed by the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz.

title: Joint Incidents Assessment Team (JIAT) Response to Doctors Without Borders, Amnesty International
content: The JIST spokesman also refuted allegations by the Amnesty International that a cattle market in the village of Fayyoh, in Lahaj Province, was subject to shelling by Coalition Forces on July 6, 2015, resulted in the death of 40 people and injuring dozens.?é?? The spokesman confirmed that JIST has investigated the facts and circumstances of the incident, and found that the mentioned market was not bombed.?é?? He added that Coalition Forces bombed two legitimate military targets on July 6, a gathering of armed Houthi militia about 7 km northwest of the cattle market, and a armed Houthi military command building 10 km north of the market.?é?? The spokesman said JIST found that the cattle market was not bombed by Coalition Forces. Regarding the allegation of Doctors Without Borders (MSF) that Al-Fazel School in Saada Province was subject to shelling on Aug. 13, 2016, which resulted in death 10 children and injuring at least nine people, the spokesman said that JIST investigated the facts and circumstances of the incident, and based on the confirmed information were dispatched by MSF on the site of incident, and found clearly that the school was not targeted.?é?? Coalition Forces targeted weapons depots belonging to Houthi armed group, a distanced 10 km from the school.?é?? The Coalition Forces, therefore, rejects the MSF allegations. With regard of the allegation contained by the Swedish Embassy that a food factory owned by Mr. Abdullah Ahmad Al-Agel, Honorary Swedish Consul in Sana'a was subject to aerial shelling on Aug. 9, 2016, which resulted in death of 16 workers, JIAT spokesman said that the investigation in the circumstances of the incident, found that the Coalition Forces bombed two sites on Aug. 9, an antennae connections used for military purposes in the Ayban Mount west of Sana???Ć?Öa (7 km from the factory in question), and a cave in Eastern Altahedan Mount south of Sana???Ć?Öa (10 km from the factory in question) and used for military purposes.?é?? Al-Mansour indicated that the two mentioned sites were legitimate military targets in accordance to the rules of engagement and international humanitarian law and that JIAT did not find any evidence to support allegations that that Coalition Forces bombed the factory. The Coalition therefore the allegations by the Swedish Embassy. Accordingly, and based on the rules of the international humanitarian law, the legal protection supposed to be enjoyed by civilian sites is dropped off this site due to its being used as a tool within the military effort, taking into consideration that the classes of the school have gone at the time on an open vacation in view of the surrounding circumstances in addition to that investigations have not revealed the occurrence of any human losses or injuries, with the building sustaining partial damage estimated at no more than 25%.?é?? JIAT has, accordingly, concluded that the procedures followed by the Coalition Forces targeting the questionable site were intact and correct. Al-Mansour refuting allegations by Amnesty International that Asmaa School in the city of Al-Mansouriya in the Yemeni province of Al-Hodeida was subject to air shelling on Aug. 24, 2015. The spokesman said that it was revealed that the Coalition Forces have targeted the Asmaa School based on surveillance information that the armed Houthi militias and forces loyal to former president Abdullah Salah were using the site as a headquarters and a weapons storage and distribution center for weapons smuggled through Al-Hodeida port, thus making it a legitimate high value target that achieves certified military advantage. In a press conference held today at King Salman Airbase for Middle Sector, Al-Mansour said that ???Ć?£in light of what has been viewed from the facts of the incident, the team found that damages of the originally-untargeted building were due to targeting of the vehicle, a legitimate military target, which was next to the building, disclosing that the joint team confirmed that the coalition forces are to apologize for the unintentional error, provide appropriate assistance to those affected, and launch investigation with the persons in charge of the incident to look into whether they have violated the accredited rules of engagement and take appropriate action as regards the incident. The Coalition Forces targeted the location of that gathering. As a result, the air crew monitored a moving vehicle from the same targeted site heading southwards, pursued and shelled it immediately. The vehicle was next to the building which had no signs of a hospital before the bombing.?é?? It was later identified as Abs Hospital. Spokesman of Joint Incidents Assessment Team (JIAT), Mansour Ahmad Al-Mansour,?é?? stressed that with regard to an announcement issued by Doctors Without Borders on the alleged bombing carried out by the coalition forces on Abs Hospital in the city of Abs in Yemen???Ć?Ös Hjjah Province on Aug. 15, 2016, which resulted in death seven people and wounding 13 others, the JIAT verified facts and circumstances of the incident based on confirmed intelligence information about existence of a gathering of Houthi armed leaders in the north of the city of Abs.

title: King Salman Address 37th GCC Summit in Manama
content:
Walsalamu Alaykum Warahmatu Allahi Wabarakatu (May the peace, mercy, and blessings of Allah be with you) Finally, I wish my brother King Hamad bin Isa Al-Khalifa every success during his current chairmanship of this session. 
Despite the important achievements implemented by the GCC, we gear forward to better future that guarantees the aspirations of the GCC citizens towards more prosperity and dignified living, enhances the march of the GCC at the regional and international arenas through active foreign policies to achieve security and stability in the region and supports regional and international peace. Dear Brothers, As of the situation in Syria, we are all saddened to see what is happening there, including killings, displacement and suffering of the Syrian people, mounting pressure on the world community to intensify efforts to stop bloodshed and find a political solution guaranteeing the achievement of security and stability as well as preserving the unity and integrity of the Syrian territories. As for the situation in Yemen, efforts are being exerted to end the struggle there in what could achieve security and stability under the leadership of its legitimate government and according to the GCC initiative, outcomes of the national dialogue and UN Security Council Resolution 2216, thanks to the endeavors offered by the UN Special Envoy to Yemen. The sad situation being suffered by some Arab countries, characterized by terrorism, domestic struggle and bloodshed, is an imperative result of the alliance of terrorism, sectarianism and flagrant interference, leading to their destabilization and breach of security. 
The current highly complicated circumstances in our region are obvious to everyone as well as the crises it was facing, requiring all of us to work together to confront and deal with them in a spirit of responsibility and determination and intensify efforts in order to consolidate the foundations of security and stability of our region as well as development and prosperity for our countries and peoples. Dear brothers: I am pleased, at the outset of this summit, to express appreciation of King Hamad bin Isa Al- Khalifa, the government and people of the Kingdom of Bahrain for the warm hospitality and good reception. I wish all success for the King of Bahrain during his chairmanship of this session. I expressed satisfaction for what has been achieved by the competent agencies of the GCC council over the past year in accordance with mechanisms adopted by the council which aim to raise the level of cooperation and coordination, including the decision of establishing the Economic and Development Affairs Commission which began its work recently to promote joint action in the economic and development fields. 
Peace, Mercy and Blessing of Allah be Upon You, 
Leaders of the Gulf Cooperation Council Member States, King Hamad bin Isa Al-Khalifa, King of the Kingdom of Bahrain, 
Praise to Allah, prayer and peace be upon the Messenger of Allah, on his family and companions, "In the Name of Allah, the Most Gracious, Most Merciful, The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz today addressed the 37th Summit for the Supreme Council of the Gulf Cooperation Council hosted by King Hamad bin Isa Al-Khalifa of Bahrain.

title: Deputy Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Chairs Cabinet's Session
content: The Cabinet has also reviewed a number of issues on its agenda, including the annual report of the general organization for social insurance on a previous fiscal year. The Cabinet issued relevant instructions towards them. The cabinet also approved the appointment of Jamal bin Ali Al-Kishi and Eng. Omar bin Nabil Al-Khudairi as two members of the private sector in the Board of Directors of the Saline Water Conversion Corporation for a period of three years. The Cabinet approved an amendment to Table (2) regarding the joint committees list attached to the Cabinet's decision No. 2 dated 03/01/1428H, so that the Saudi side at the Saudi-Indonesian Joint Committee is chaired by the Minister of Foreign Affairs, and that the Committee shall meet every six months. The Cabinet concluded by approving a memorandum of understanding the Peoples Republic of China on enhancing joint cooperation on the silk road economic belt and the initiative of naval silk road for the 21st century and cooperation on productive energy which was signed in Riyadh on 9/4/1437 H;?é?? authorized the Minister of Islamic Affairs, Call and Guidance, or his deputy, to negotiate a memorandum of understanding in the field of Islamic Affairs with Mauritania, sign it, and forward the final version for taking further procedures; approved a general cooperation agreement Mauritius which was signed in New York on 12/12/1436H; authorized the Minister of Foreign Affairs, or his deputy, to negotiate with Norway and New Zealand draft memoranda of understanding on political consultations, sign them, and submit the final versions for taking further authentication;?é?? authorized the Acting Secretary General of King Abdulaziz Foundation for Research and Archives, or his deputy, to discuss with the United Arab Emirate a draft memorandum of cooperation between King Abdulaziz Foundation for Research and Archives Saudi Arabia and the National Archive of the Ministry of Presidential Affairs of the United Arab Emirates, sign it, and submit the signed final version. Also praised was the Kingdom's participation in the international conference on the preservation of endangered cultural heritage, held in Abu-Dhabi in the attendance of Prince Khalid Al-Faisal, Advisor to the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosque and Governor of Makkah Region, who led the Kingdom's delegation on behalf of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosque. The Cabinet lauded the holding of a number of cultural activities, citing the opening by the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques of Trade Roads in the Arab peninsula exhibition, the masterpieces and antiquities of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia across history, held at King Abdulaziz international cultural center in Dhahran, and the king's okay of the exhibition's tour of east Asia following its successful nine tours of Europe and America. To mark the occasion of the International Day for Solidarity with the Palestinian people, the Cabinet reiterated the Kingdom's call for the world community to remain committed towards the rights of Palestinians and retard Israel from its procrastinating policies against them. The Cabinet praised the 7th Bosphorus Summit.?é?? The Cabinet hailed the cooperation agreements in the fields of investment signed by Saudi Arabia and Turkey during the Summit. The Cabinet also praised the cooperation and contribution of the main oil producing countries outside OPEC to the restoration of stability and balance of international petroleum market. The Cabinet was briefed on the outcome of OPEC ministerial meeting which was held last week in Vienna. The Cabinet praised the outcome of the meeting, the decisions that were taken as well as the clear cooperation among all OPEC countries to restore stability in the international oil market by reducing excess supply. The Cabinet commended the outcome of the 35th Meeting of GCC Interior Ministers, expressing hope that the outcome of the meeting will contribute to enhancing the efforts of GCC countries in the field of security. The Cabinet reviewed a number of reports on regional, Arab and international developments, and looked forward to the outcome of meetings the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques will hold with leaders of the United Arab Emirates, the State of Qatar, the Kingdom of Bahrain and the State of Kuwait during his tour that emanates from his keenness to communicate with Gulf Cooperation Council leaders to serve the interests of their peoples and to enhance ties of brotherhood among GCC countries. Crown Prince Mohammad welcomed Minister of Labour and Social Development Dr. Ali bin Nasser Al-Ghafis in his new portfolio, wishing him success. He also thanked the outgoing officials and members for their efforts. The Deputy Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques congratulated the Kingdom's Grand Mufti; members of the Senior Scholars Commission; full-time sheikhs, members of the Standing Committee for Fatwa (affiliated to the Senior Scholars Commission); and Speaker and members of Majlis Al-Shura on the royal trust bestowed on them through renewing their memberships to their agencies, wishing them all success. Deputy Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Prince Mohammad bin Naif bin Abdulaziz Al Saud chaired the Cabinet's session which was held at Al-Yamamah Palace today.
