The alliance includes the family of the late Prince Ahmed bin Salman as well as a number of media organizations such as SRMG, Al-Yamamah Press Est., Al-Jazirah Organization for Press, Printing and Publication, Dar Al-Hayat Publishing Company, Al-Yaum Press, Printing and Publishing House, Al-Madinah Al-Munawwarah Organization for Press, Printing & Publication, Middle East Television Group and Dallah Media Production. The world-class institute is the first media training facility of its kind in the Arab world with technical, design and practical training for prospective members of the media. It is expected to open a new era in Saudi mass communication with its diverse international courses in print and broadcast media. Governor of Riyadh Province Prince Salman bin Abdulaziz formally inaugurated the Prince Ahmed bin Salman Applied Media Institute in Riyadh last night.

title: Saudi Antiquities to be Exhibited at the Louvre in 2010
content: The Louvre previously mounted an exhibit of Islamic artifacts at the Saudi Arabian National Museum in Riyadh. At the opening of the exhibit in March 2006, Former French President Jacques Chirac suggested the possibility of holding a Saudi exhibit in Paris, and Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz endorsed the idea. Secretary-General of the Supreme Tourism Commission (SCT) Prince Sultan bin Salman and Louvre Director Henri Loyrette signed an agreement on the exhibit in Riyadh today. A collection of Saudi antiquities will be exhibited at the Louvre Museum in Paris in the summer of 2010.

title: Prince Saud Calls for Dialogue Among Religions
content: The prince noted that the theme ???Ć?£Culture and Respect for Religions???Ć?was chosen because culture plays a critical role in the development of an individual???Ć?Ös identity, his views towards others as well as the role of religion as a spiritual element for nations. ???Ć?£We are living in an era of globalization which makes it necessary to easily conduct dialogue among us, so it is an important matter to exert further efforts to get acquainted with others in a better way,???Ć?Prince Saud said.?é?? In a speech read on his behalf by Madani, Prince Saud called for holding more dialogues among different religions and cultures in order enhance understanding among societies.?é?? Minister of State for Foreign Affairs Dr. Nizar Madani opened the forum on behalf of Minister of Foreign Affairs Prince Saud Al-Faisal.?é?? The theme of the three-day forum is ???Ć?£Culture and Respect for Religions.???Ć?It was organized by the Institute of Diplomatic Studies and the Ministry of Foreign Affairs Department of Islamic Affairs.?é?? The sixth forum for cultural dialogue between Japan and the Islamic world opened in Riyadh yesterday.?é??

title: King Abdullah Calls for Interfaith Meeting
content: During the reception, Japanese Vice-Minister of Foreign Affairs Osamu Onu told King Abdullah that there are more than 200,000 thousand Muslims and 40 mosques in Japan. The King expressed the hope that that the delegates attending the forum would take this message to their respective countries. He also said that Saudi Arabia would hold a number of conferences to gather viewpoints of Muslims on the issue.?é?? The King said that he presented the idea of holding such an interfaith meeting to Islamic scholars in Saudi Arabia and that they approved it. He also discussed the idea of dialogue among divine religions with Pope Benedict XVI at their November 2007 meeting at the Vatican.?é?? In his remarks, King Abdullah emphasized the need for people of all faiths to return to God and confront the spread of atheism, the disintegration of the family structure and an overall lack of ethics.?é?? ???Ć?£The idea is to request all religions to sit together with their brothers faithfully and sincerely as we all believe in the same God,???Ć?King Abdullah said. The dialogue would include Christianity, Judaism and Islam.?é?? King Abdullah made the remarks today in a reception for delegates of the sixth forum for cultural dialogue between Japan and the Islamic world.?é?? Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz has called for a meeting of monotheistic religions in order to solve the pressing problems facing humanity.?é??

title: New Media Institute to be Launched in April
content: The institute was established through an alliance that aims to develop human resources in the media sector. The alliance includes the family of the late Prince Ahmed bin Salman as well as a number of media organizations such as SRMG, Al-Yamamah Press Est., Al-Jazirah Organization for Press, Printing and Publication, Dar Al-Hayat Publishing Company, Al-Yaum Press, Printing and Publishing House, Al-Madinah Al-Munawwarah Organization for Press, Printing & Publication, Middle East Television Group and Dallah Media Production. ???Ć?£This institute will be the first step toward building a base for media training that can interact with the requirements of the media industry in the Kingdom in particular and the region in general,???Ć?Prince Faisal said. Prince Faisal said the institute would serve all sectors of the media. He also praised efforts to promote the media in Saudi Arabia, noting that the Saudi media has made tremendous progress in recent years. ???Ć?£The institute is first of its kind in the Kingdom, and is specialized in providing technical, design and practical training to prospective journalists and mediapersons,???Ć?Prince Faisal said, as quoted in the Arab News. Prince Faisal bin Salman, chairman of the Saudi Research & Marketing Group (SRMG), made the announcement yesterday. Governor of Riyadh Province Prince Salman bin Abdulaziz will open the Prince Ahmed bin Salman Institute for Applied Media on April 27, the Arab News reported today.

title: King Faisal International Prizes for 2008 Presented
content: For more information about the King Faisal Foundation International Prize and this year???Ć?Ös winners, please see the King Faisal Foundation website. Other winners include: Professor R?ā??diger Wehner of Germany (Science), Professors Donald D. Trunkey and Basil A. Pruitt, Jr. of the United States (Medicine), and Professor Ahmad Matloob Al-Nasiri of Iraq and Professor Muhammed Rashad Al-Hamzawi of Tunisia (Arabic Language and Literature). The prize for Islamic Studies was not awarded this year due to a lack of merit among nominees. ???Ć?£The Muslim scientist in his laboratory, the soldier defending his homeland, the preacher calling for moderation, the impartial employee who refuses inducements, the fair and equitable judge, the worker who works with his hands and masters his work and the student who perseveres in his study, all those are serving Islam,???Ć?King Abdullah said. In remarks on the occasion, King Abdullah said that he accepted the prize on behalf of every Muslim man and woman who served Islam out of the limelight without expectation of any reward. Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz won the prize for Service to Islam recognition of his service to Muslims both in Saudi Arabia and abroad. The King Faisal International Prizes for 2008 were presented at a ceremony in Riyadh last night. Prizes were awarded in four categories: Arabic Language and Literature, Science, Medicine and Service to Islam.?é??

title: King Abdullah Meets with Jenadriyah Guests
content: The weeklong Jenadriyah Heritage and Culture Festival showcases Saudi Arabia???Ć?Ös rich cultural heritage with exhibits including traditional arts and crafts, poetry, singing and dancing. It is organized each year by the National Guard. The guests included Muslim scholars, intellectuals and members of the media from Saudi Arabia and abroad. Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz met with distinguished guests of the National Guard who are attending the 23rd Jenadriyah Heritage and Culture Festival, which began this past week.

title: King Abdullah Opens 23rd Jenadriyah Heritage Festival
content: In related news, King Abdullah also met with Nelson Ford, Assistant Secretary of the US Army for Financial Management. Ford presented King Abdullah with a certificate marking the 35th anniversary of cooperation between the Saudi National Guard and the US Army. King Abdullah also met today with distinguished guests attending the festival, including King Hamad Al-Khalifa of Bahrain, the heir apparent of Abu Dhabi Sheikh Hamad Al-Nahayan, and Turkish Minister of Culture and Tourism Ertu?ä?¤rul G?ā??nay. The weeklong event showcases Saudi Arabia???Ć?Ös rich cultural heritage with exhibits including traditional arts and crafts, poetry, singing and dancing. It is organized each year by the National Guard. After recitations from the Holy Qur???Ć?Öan, the festival kicked off with a traditional camel race. Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz opened the 23rd Jenadriyah National Festival for Heritage and Culture today.?é??

title: King Faisal International Prize Winners
content: . For more information about the King Faisal Foundation and the International Prize, please see the  Professor Wehner from the University of Zurich, Switzerland won the Science prize for his work in the field of biology. An outstanding neuroethologist, Prof. Wehner???Ć?Ös research has revolved around the general question of how a 0.1-milligram brain of a 10-milligram animal solves complex computational tasks. He has focused on the extraordinary navigational skills of visually guided desert ants. His work has inspired many international research groups to use this elegant animal model for various conceptual and methodological approaches aiming at the questions of how nervous systems evolved, how they work and how they control behavior. The prize for medicine in the field of Trauma Management was shared by Professor Trunkey of Oregon Health & Science University and Professor Pruitt of the University of Texas Health Science Center in San Antonio. Prof. Trunkey, one of the most influential leaders in the field of trauma management, conceived and validated an organized trauma system that significantly improved the survival of injured patients. For his part, Prof. Pruitt is an internationally renowned clinical leader in burn surgery and a distinguished researcher in the science of improving the outcome of serious burn injury. Over the past 50 years, his work has significantly improved trauma care. Regarding the winners for Arabic Language and Literature, Professor Al-Nasiri of Iraq is recognized for his fundamental role in studying the evolution, organization and context of terminology in Arabic rhetoric and literary criticism. Al-Hamzawi is awarded the prize for his pioneering contributions to the field of terminology in classical and modern Arabic language, and his efforts to develop this field as an integral part of Arabic language scholarship. Other winners include: Professor R?ā??diger Wehner of Germany (Science), Professors Donald D. Trunkey and Basil A. Pruitt, Jr. of the United States (Medicine), and Professor Ahmad Matloob Al-Nasiri of Iraq and Professor Muhammed Rashad Al-Hamzawi of Tunisia (Arabic Language and Literature). The prize for Islamic Studies was not awarded this year due to a lack of merit among nominees. King Abdullah was awarded the prize in recognition of his service to Muslims both in Saudi Arabia and abroad. In the Kingdom, his achievements include the establishment of the King Abdullah Economic City, the creation of 10 universities, the expansion of the holy sites and the establishment of the Human Rights Commission as well as the Center for Dialogue. The King has also demonstrated a commitment to Arab and Islamic issues, particularly the situation in Palestine and Arab unity. The winners of the King Faisal International Prize for 2008 were announced this week. Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz won the prize for Service to Islam recognition of his service to Muslims both in Saudi Arabia and abroad.

title: Real Estate Development Fund Approved Over $650 Million in New Home Loans
content: The Director General of Real Estate Development Fund, Yousef bin Abdullah Al-Zughaibi, has approved a new batch of home loans totaling SR 2.44 billion ($650.67 million) in FY 1436-1437 H (October 2014-October 2015).

title: Foreign Minister: King Salman, President Hollande are Keen to Enhance Bilateral Relations
content: Regarding the aid provided for the Lebanese army, the minister said that there is an agreement between the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and France to provide the Lebanese army with weapons worth $3 billion. The agreement is valid and payments will be scheduled. Regarding the Geneva negotiations on Yemen, Minister Al-Jubeir said ???Ć?£It might be better to consider the negotiations as a first step where there would be additional steps that can be taken. The solution for Yemen is clear and should be based on the Gulf Cooperation Council Initiative, the outcome of Yemen national dialogue and UN Security Council Resolution 2216. The Geneva meeting was a first step that proved that Houthis were not serious about reaching a peaceful solution with the Yemeni legitimate government." Responding to a question regarding the recent visit to Russia by the Deputy Crown Prince, Minister Al-Jubeir said ???Ć?£We are meeting and consulting with our allies about the region's issues; and we are coordinating our stances towards these issues. The Deputy Crown Prince's visit to Russia was a bilateral visit where we discussed means of enhancing bilateral relations between the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and Russia. It was also an opportunity to consult with the Russian side on issues of interest in the region, including Syria, Iran's negative acts in the region, the situation in Yemen and other issues." The Foreign Minister confirmed that all agreements to be signed today between Saudi Arabia and France are between the two governments without any third party and without any mediator to ensure that the Kingdom obtains the best technology at the best price. Minister Al-Jubeir reiterated the keenness of the Kingdom to acquire the best technology in the world in all fields in the service of its people and economy, pointing out that the Kingdom and France have a long history in the field of armament. On the issues of regional security, Minister Al-Jubeir said that ???Ć?£there should be coordination and consultation between the two countries in the fields of economy, trade, investment, technology, armament, defense and military cooperation.???Ć?Minister Al-Jubeir said that a number of bilateral agreements will be signed later today. These agreements have been negotiated and finalized in a short period of less than six weeks, Minister Al-Jubeir said. He also added that both countries are looking forward to more agreements and understanding. Minister Al-Jubeir added that mechanism for coordination and consultation is established, including Joint Committee co-chaired by the foreign ministers and under supervision of Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense. Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir said at a joint press conference with French Foreign Minister Laurent Fabius in Paris today that Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz and French President Francois Hollande are keen on enhancing bilateral in all fields, including the political, security, military, economic and education fields.

title: Deputy Crown Prince Visits France Tuesday
content: The Royal Court announced today that Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Defense Minister, will be travelling to France on Tuesday, June 23. Prince Mohammed bin Salman is scheduled to meet with French President Francois Hollande and a number of French officials.

title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet Session
content: The Cabinet authorized president of King Abdulaziz City for Science and Technology, or his deputy, to discuss and sign a draft memorandum of joint intent with the Russian Space Agency in the field of exploration of outer space and its use for peaceful purposes. After reviewing the report submitted by the Minister Finance and after consideration of the Shura Council Resolution No. (48/28) dated 18/06/1436 H, the Cabinet approved the agreement the Kyrgyz Republic on the avoidance of double taxation and prevention of fiscal evasion with respect to taxes on income, signed in Riyadh on 10/02/1436 AH. The Cabinet expressed satisfaction with the strength of the Saudi national economy, as shown in the Saudi Arabian Monterey Agency???Ć?Ös 50th and 51st annual reports. The Cabinet noted that the national economy has achieved good results in the last year, where the real Gross Domestic Product (GDP) grew by 5.3 percent and is expected to grow. The Kingdom's economic policies have focused on vital areas such as the development of national human resources, modernization and maintenance of infrastructure projects, quest to diversify the production base, and enhancing the financial reserves of the State to cope with unforeseen developments in the global oil prices. The Cabinet commended the results of the 135th session of the Ministerial Council of the Gulf Cooperation Council for Arab States which was held in Riyadh, and the results of the 23rd meeting of Ministers of Information of the Gulf Cooperation Council for Arab States which was held in Doha. The Cabinet was then briefed on reports about local affairs and the latest regional and international developments and commended the United Nations' efforts to hold the Geneva conference on Yemen today. The Kingdom re-emphasizing its support for the demands of the legitimate Yemeni government by committing to the Riyadh Declaration, United Nations Security Council Resolution 2216, the Gulf Initiative, and the results of the Yemeni National Dialogue. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz chaired the Cabinet's session at Al-Salam Palace in Jeddah on this afternoon. King Salman began the meeting by congratulating the people of Saudi Arabia and Arab and Islamic peoples on the near advent of the blessed holy month of Ramadan, praying to Allah Almighty to help them in fasting and prayers and accept their good deeds in this holy month. The King then briefed the Cabinet on the message he received from the Emir of Kuwait Sheikh Sabah Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah, and the results of his talks with President Ali Bongo Ondimba of Gabon. King Salman also briefed the Cabinet on his meeting with the Executive Director of World Food Programme Ertharin Cousin, pointing to the Kingdom's keenness to support various efforts and provide assistance for the needy peoples around the world.

title: Saudi Food and Drug Authority Bans Poultry Meat from 13 US States and Bhutan
content: The Authority excluded from the temporary ban are thermally processed poultry meat and products or other internationally accepted treatments. Saudi Food and Drug Authority (SFDA) has temporarily banned the import of poultry meat, eggs, their products and equipment from five other US states and Thimphu, Bhutan because of a huge bird flu outbreak. The five US states are: Montana, North Dakota, South Dakota, Wisconsin and Iowa. Last year, SFDA banned the imports from Oregon, California, Washington, Idaho, Minnesota, Missouri, Kansas and Arkansas for the same reason.

title: French President Meets with Saudi Officials
content: French President Francois Hollande met separately at his residence in Riyadh today with the Director General of Saudi Arabian Airlines, Saleh Al-Jasser; President of King Abdullah City for Atomic and Renewable Energy (KACARE) Dr. Hashim Yamani; Minister of Transport Abdullah Al-Moqbil; Minister of Health Khalid Al-Faleh; Minister of Finance Dr. Ibrahim Al-Assaf; Minister of Municipal and Rural Affairs Abdullatif Al-AshSheikh; .and Minister of Commerce and Industry Tawfiq Al-Rabiah. The meetings were attended by the Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir.

title: King Appoints New Crown Prince, Deputy Crown Prince and Foreign Minister
content: Other key appointments also announced today included: Khaled bin Abdulaziz Al-Falih as minister of health and chairman of Saudi Aramco; Adel bin Mohammed Fakeih as minister of economy and planning; Mufrej bin Saad Al-Haqbani as minister of labor; and Hamad bin Abdulaziz Al-Suwailem as chief of the Royal Court at the rank of minister. Prince Saud Al-Faisal was relieved as foreign minister at his request for health reasons. He was appointed state minister, cabinet member, adviser and special envoy to King Salman, and supervisor on foreign affairs. Saudi Ambassador to the United States Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir was appointed as the new foreign minister Prince Mohammed bin Salman was appointed as deputy crown prince and second deputy prime minister. He will retain his positions as minister of defense and president of the Council of Economic and Development Affairs. Crown Prince and Deputy Prime Minister Prince Muqrin bin Abdulaziz was relieved of his post at his request. Prince Mohammad bin Naif was appointed crown prince and deputy prime minister. He will retain his positions as minister of interior and president of the Council of Political and Security Affairs. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz today issued a number royal orders, including the appointments of a new crown prince, deputy crown prince and foreign minister.

title: King Salman Calls for Economic Strength and Stability in Major Policy Address
content: King Salman urged the people of Saudi Arabia to stand united and said that Saudi Arabia would continue its efforts to achieve comprehensive, integrated and balanced development for all regions. ???Ć?£Ever since the Kingdom???Ć?Ös founding, the government has been making continuous efforts for development and modernization, without deviating from religious principles and social values while protecting the rights of all citizens,???Ć?said King Salman. King Salman also emphasized the need for the Kingdom to prioritize national security stating, ???Ć?£Security is everyone???Ć?Ös responsibility and will not allow anyone to tamper with our security and stability.???Ć?In response to the growing threat of extremism and violence in the region, King Salman condemned terrorism in all forms. He stated, ???Ć?£The Kingdom is focused on the fight against extremism and terrorism in all its forms and manifestations, whatever its sources, and in cooperation with brotherly and friendly countries and the international organizations in the fight against this detestable menace, and in seeking eradicating its roots and causes.???Ć?King Salman reinforced the Kingdom???Ć?Ös commitment to the teachings of Islam, stating that Saudi foreign policy would continue to defend Islamic causes globally. ???Ć?£Saudi foreign policy is always committed to the teachings of our religion, calling for love and peace in accordance with its principles,???Ć?said King Salman. King Salman addressed falling oil prices, assuring the Saudi people that the nation will remain economically stable. ???Ć?£We encourage and support medium and small enterprises to grow so that they become a strong economic area for a large segment of society,???Ć?King Salman stated. ???Ć?£The coming years will witness rich and great achievements that enhance industrial and services roles in the national economy.???Ć?Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz on today announced his domestic and foreign policies to a group of government officials, scholars and other dignitaries in Riyadh. King Salman emphasized his plan to build a more diversified economy and defend Islamic causes globally.

title: KSRelief Delivers 30 Tons of Dates to WFP in Cameroon
content: In the presence of Saudi Ambassador to Cameroon Mohammed bin Sulaiman Al-Mis-her, King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Works (KSRelief) delivered 30 tons of dates to the World Food Program (WFP) in Cameroon.?é?? The shipment is part of the Kingdom's donation to WFP for its distribution in Cameroon.

title: KSRelief Provides to Yemen With Over $600 Million
content: The Riyadh-based center was highly commended by sovereign states, community leaders and international and regional organizations working in the areas of relief and humanitarian services as well as visitors of the facility as provider of state-of-the-art organization. Since its inauguration, the King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Works (KSRelief) has provided a number of projects worth over $600 million to 127 governorates in Yemen in various fields including relief, humanitarian, shelter and environmental sanitation programs.

title: Saudi Arabia Pledges $150 Million in Humanitarian Aid to Yemen
content: Established in May 2015, KSRelief has provided health, nutrition and sanitation aid and relief to 17 million people impacted by the conflict in Yemen. Partnering with international humanitarian organizations, KSRelief has established more than 100 health facilities, provided emergency food aid and supplied more than 350,000 gallons of fuel to medical facilities. Dr. Al-Rabeeah explained that the new donation comes as part of the $8.2 billion committed by the Kingdom within its humanitarian and developmental assistance to Yemen since April 2015. Saudi Arabia remains the single largest donor of humanitarian assistance to Yemen. Dr. Abdullah Al-Rabeeah the supervisor general for KSRelief, issued the announcement during the Geneva aid conference, a day-long meeting of international donors focused on responding to Yemen???Ć?Ös humanitarian crisis. (WASHINGTON, DC) ???Ć?The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia pledged today a $150 million donation to support the King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Center (KSRelief) in Yemen.

title: King Salman Relief Center Delivers 150 Tons of Dates to Jordan
content: King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Aid (SKRelief) delivered 150 tons of dates as a gift from Saudi Arabia to Jordan.

title: King Salman Relief Center Distributes 1938 Metric Tons of Maize in Ethiopia
content: King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Aid (SKRelief), in cooperation with the World Food Program, has distributed 1938 metric tons of maize to assist 127,000 people in Jijiga, the capital of the Somali Regional State of Ethiopia.

title: King Salman Relief Center Provides Dates, Clothes in Yemen
content: King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Aid (KSRelief) provided 215 tons of dates and 3 tons of winter clothes to Yemenis in Shabwa governorate.

title: King Salman Center Dispatches Urgent Relief Supplies to Khan Shekhoun
content: In a statement, acting KSrelief spokesman the Dr. Sami Al-???Ć?ÄJutaili said that a truck convoy?é??laden with urgently needed food supplies?é??has ???Ć?Äleft last night heading for the Turkish border post of Bab Al-???Ć?ÄHawa on it its way to the southern rural areas of Idlib.???Ć?Ä?é?The supplies are expected to meet the urgent needs of over ???Ć?Ä???Ć?,200 families.???Ć?Upon directives of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques ???Ć?ÄKing Salman bin Abdulaziz to help needy Syrians, the King Salman ???Ć?ÄHumanitarian Aid & Relief Center (KSRelief) launched an urgent relief ???Ć?Äcampaign to help the people in the war-ravaged Syria city of ???Ć?ÄKhan Shekhoun.???Ć?

title: Saudi Clinics Treat some 380 Syrian Patients a Day at Al-Zaatari Camp in Jordan
content: Saudi Specialized Clinics at Al-Zaatari Camp in Amman, Jordan has treated 11,916 Syrian refugees ???Ć?Äin its specialized medical clinics during the month of March, medical director of clinics, Dr. Hamid ???Ć?ÄAl-Mufalani, announced today.???Ć?

title: Coalition Calls on UN to Ensure Delivery of Humanitarian Supplies to Yemeni Population
content: The coalition has called on the United Nations to exercise oversight of the Port of Hodeidah to safeguard the free flow of humanitarian deliveries. The coalition added that the other ports including Aden, Mukalla and other airports are open as routes for humanitarian supplies. The coalition accused that Houthi rebels of seizing and selling humanitarian supplies that pass through the Hodeidah.?é?? ???Ć?£The Coalition is deeply concerned about the humanitarian situation in Yemen. We are doing everything in our power to ensure that the Yemeni population receives the food and medicines it needs, the coalition said.?é?? ???Ć?£The Port of Hodeidah is an important entry point for humanitarian supplies. But it is currently in the hands of the Houthi rebels, who use it to smuggle arms and people. Houthi rebels also finance their ongoing violent campaign to overthrow the legitimate government by seizing humanitarian supplies and selling them for exorbitant sums. The Coalition Supporting Legitimacy in Yemen today called on the United Nations to ensure the free flow of humanitarian shipments through the Port of Hodeidah to alleviate the humanitarian crisis in Yemen.

title: King Salman Center Launches Anti-Dengue Fever Campaign in Yemen
content: King Salman Humanitarian Aid & Relief Center (KSRelief) has launched an anti-dengue fever campaign in six governorates in Yemen.?é?? KSRelief is working with the Yemen-based Al-Awn Foundation for Development to provide intravenous fluids and supportive treatment to functioning health facilities in affected areas, and to conduct spraying activities in Hodaidah, Aden, Hadramout, Taiz, Lahj, and Shabwah governorates.

title: KSRelief Anti-Cholera Medicine Arrives in Iraq
content: In implementation of the order of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz to assist the Iraqi people, and in response to an appeal by the World Health Organization to help combat an outbreak of cholera in Iraq, a King Salman Humanitarian Aid & Relief Center (KSrelief) aircraft laden with more than 10 tons of medical supplies arrived at Baghdad International Airport yesterday evening.

title: KSRelief Air Dropping Supplies to Besieged Yemeni City
content: The Riyadh-based King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Centre (KSRelief) announced today that it ???Ć?Äis air dropping humanitarian supplies to the besieged population in Taiz.?é?? KSRelief Director Dr. ???Ć?ÄAbdullah Al-Rabiah said that in collaboration with the Saudi Ministry of Defense and the Arab ???Ć?ÄCoalition to Support Legitimacy in Yemen, the humanitarian air drops broke the siege by Houthi militias ???Ć?Äand Ali Abdullah Saleh forces of Taiz. The air drops consists of food and medical supplies.?é?? Dr. Al-???Ć?ÄRabiah emphasize the Kingdom is committed to provide humanitarian assistance to all Yemeni ???Ć?Äpeople in all parts of Yemen.???Ć?

title: Saudi Campaign Distributes over 230,000 Winter Items to Syrians Refugees in Jordan
content: The Saudi National Campaign for the Support of Brothers in Syria has distributed 231,049 winter items to 73,401 Syrian refugees in Al-Zaatari Refugee Camp in Jordan as part of its winter campaign.

title: Kingdom Sets Up Camps for Displaced Yemenis in Horn of Africa
content: The center launched a comprehensive educational program and training support to serve the ???Ć?Äeducation and its staff in Yemen, based on the arms of electronic gate for transmitting the lectures ???Ć?Äwith Yemeni contents presented by Yemeni teachers, satellite broadcasting, and training for ???Ć?Äteachers and replacing the printed educational curricula with digital ones, as well as applications ???Ć?Äon smart devices.???Ć?KSRelief constructed 300 housing units, including schools and mosques in Djibouti.?é?? It also ???Ć?Äprovided accommodation, healthcare and education for displaced Yemenis.?é?? The center also ???Ć?Äcarried out a project to improve safe movement, protection and finding durable solutions for ???Ć?ÄSomali returnees and Yemeni refugees in Somalia in cooperation with the United Nations High ???Ć?ÄCommissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) and the International Organization for Migrations (IOM). ???Ć?ÄThe Mogadishu projects benefits nearly 20,000 beneficiaries.???Ć?In 2016, KSRelief carried out five projects targeting 39,000 people at a cost of $57 ???Ć?Ämillion, including the repatriation of over 3,000 Ethiopian refugees from Yemen to Ethiopia and ???Ć?Äthe transferring of 1,240 stranded Yemenis from several countries.???Ć?The projects included, educational services, two satellite channels broadcasting about 4,000 ???Ć?Älectures to more than 1,000,000 students, in addition to therapeutic projects, construction of ???Ć?Ähousing units and addressing the situation of refugees in Yemen.???Ć?The Riyadh-based King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Centre (KSRelief) has set up refuges camps ???Ć?Äin Somalia, Yemen and Djibouti to accommodate Yemenis escaping the war and other refugees ???Ć?Äfrom disaster-stricken countries.???Ć?

title: Saudi Arabia Steps Up Assistance to Care for Ill and Injured in Yemen
content: The organization recently transferred a group of injured Yemenis from Taiz for treatment in Sudan, paying for the full cost of their travel and treatment. KSRelief has executed 51 projects in health, nutrition, water and environmental sanitation in Yemen totaling more than $208 million and benefiting 24.5 million Yemeni citizens, in addition to programs providing medical care and treatment for people inside Yemeni territory. KSRelief has entered into a number of agreements with private hospitals in Aden and Taiz aimed at providing full medical care for more than 750 wounded in these provinces. KSRelief seeks to activate the role of government hospitals to treat the injured inside Yemeni territories by providing medical services and treatment devices. Saudi Arabia, working through KSRelief, is the largest donor of humanitarian assistance to the Yemeni people. KSRelief was established in May 2015 under the sponsorship of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz. (WASHINGTON, DC) ???Ć?The King Salman Humanitarian Aid & Relief Center (KSRelief) recently sent 58 trucks carrying medical supplies and equipment to a number of provinces in Yemen. Of these, 11 trucks delivered supplies to Republic Hospital in Aden and six trucks to Mareb Hospital.

title: Saudi Arabia Leads International Efforts to Save Children, Rehabilitate Yemen???Ć?Ös Health Sector
content: In collaboration with the World Health Organization (WHO), KSRELIEF provided basic services such as diabetes and cancer medicines, and support the operation of emergency rooms by providing 350,000 gallon of fuel to 88 hospitals and 19 health facilities in Yemen. To date, KSRELIEF provided 12,000,000 vaccines for Yemeni children against measles and rubella diseases and tetanus and whooping cough. KSRELIEF has sent 58 trucks carrying over 600 tons of medical and therapeutic supplies to all Yemeni provinces, starting with Aden and Marib.?é?? KSRELIEF also worked closely with the UN International Children???Ć?Ös Emergency Fund (UNICEF) in training 3,153 health workers on the social management of acute malnutrition and opened 854 centers for therapeutic nutrition program. The program also provided medical treatment and follow up cases of acute malnutrition.?é?? KSRELIEF mobile health teams have treated over a quarter of a million children suffering from acute malnutrition and provided immunization and postnatal care to over 1.2 million children throughout Yemen.?é?? The teams also provided services for pregnant women and post-delivery care for over 220,000 women. The Riyadh-based King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Centre (KSRELIEF) is leading international efforts to rehabilitate Yemen???Ć?Ös health sector which has nearly been destroyed since the Houthi militias and the forces loyal to deposed president Ali Abdullah Saleh usurped power in Sana???Ć?Öa.?é?? The United Nations detailed Houthi militias???Ć?gross violations of international humanitarian law, including attacks on hospitals and medical facilities.?é?? Houthi militias and Saleh loyalists have also prevented medical supplies from reached the needy.

title: Houthi Militias Mining Areas as They Retreat
content: The King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Aid (KSRELIEF) is carrying out numerous projects to alleviate the suffering of all civilians in Yemen, including supporting rehabilitation of injured civilians?é??and supporting International Committee of Red Cross's (ICRC) artificial limbs centers in Yemen. Houthi militias and forces loyal to deposed president Ali Abdullah Salah are minding areas liberate by the Yemeni national army in an effort to slow the advance of pro-government forces. The mines have already claimed the lives of many civilians and prevented the return of others.?é?? Former governor of Al-Jawf Governorate Hussein Awadi said that he believed that the Houthi militias and their allies?é??had planted over 30 thousand mines with varied sizes in the governorate.?é?? Saudi-led Arab Coalition to Support Legitimacy in Yemen are working with the Yemeni national army to clear landmines to help conflict-affected communities to return to their homes and rebuild their lives.

title: King Salman Relief Center Distributes 20,000 Food Baskets in Hodeidah
content: On January 9, KSRELIEF began the distribution of 300,000 food baskets in Hodeida Province to benefit 460,000 needy people. King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Centre (KSRELIEF) has recently distributed 20,000 food baskets in Hodeidah Province to meet the needs of 120,000 Yemeni civilians. The food baskets are being distributed through local partners.

title: KSRELIEF Distributes 30,000 Food Baskets in Somalia
content: The King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Assistance (Ksrelief) distributed 30,000 food baskets to the people affected by drought in Somalia, benefitting 180,000 persons.?é?? In a statement, Fahd Al-Osaimi, Director General of the Relief Department at the center, said that the food aid is estimated about 2,130 tons including sugar, flour, rice, dates, oil, and beans. The aid is expected to benefit 180,000 people.

title: KSRELIEF Distributes 300,000 Food Baskets in Yemen
content: The Riyadh-based King Salman bin Center for Relief and Humanitarian Works (KSRELIEF) is currently embarked on distributing 300,000 food baskets in Hodeida Province of Yemen.?é?? The Center has already has already executed phase one of 20,000 food baskets in 15 directorates through local partners as well as 150,000 food baskets benefiting 460,000 persons.

title: King Salman to Cover Cost of Jordanian With Special Needs Who Wish to Perform Umrah
content: ?é?? The Saudi Embassy in Amman today launched the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz?é??Umrah program for people with special needs.?é?? The program is in collaboration with the Handicapped Child Care Charity Association in Irbid, Jordan and the Medical Educational Center.?é??

title: King Salman Ordered a Relief Campaign for the Syrian People
content: King Salan kicked off the campaign by donating SR 20 million, Crown Prince Mohammed bin Naif donated SR 10 million, and Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman donated SR 8 million. King Salman dedicated SR 100 million ($27 million) for the campaign.?é?? The campaign will be coordinated by the King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Works which will set up collection centers throughout the Kingdom to collect monetary and in-kind donations. The Royal Court announced today that the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz has ordered the formation of a relief campaign to help the suffering Syrian brethren, especially those evacuated from Aleppo and other areas, who have been displaced and because of the difficult situation they face.?é?? The campaign will begin Tuesday, December 27, 2016.

title: Saudi Arabia Pays Share to Palestinian Authority's Budget for Eight Months
content: Saudi Development Fund has deposited $61.6 million to the Palestinian Ministry of Finance's account. Saudi Ambassador to Egypt, Ahmed Kattan explained that the amount represents the value of the Kingdom's monthly contributions to support the Palestinian National Authority's budget.?é?? The payment is for?é??April through?é??November?é??2016.

title: Saudi King Says Nation Is Committed to Regional Security and Economic Development
content: ???Ć?£I am optimistic about a better tomorrow,???Ć?King Salman said. ???Ć?£These conditions that we are now experiencing are not more difficult than their previous ones. We will overpass them to a better and brilliant future.???Ć?King Salman noted that economic reforms implemented to date have focused on diversifying the nation???Ć?Ös economy and re-distributing resources to expand job opportunities. Saudi Arabia has ???Ć?£come out of [the past three years] with a strong economy and continuous growth; we have set out our reforms today to look forward to the future,???Ć?he noted. The strength of these structural reforms fall under Vision 2030, Saudi Arabia???Ć?Ös economic transformation plan. The region???Ć?Ös stability directly impacts the Kingdom???Ć?Ös economic development, and ensuring both remains fundamental, King Salman stressed. ???Ć?£Our domestic policy is based on pillars of maintaining security, stability and prosperity to achieve the development that meets the needs of the present and preserves the right of future generations.???Ć????Ć?£We in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia believe that the security of our dear neighbor Yemen is the security of the Kingdom, and will not accept any interference in its internal affairs,???Ć?he stated. King Salman pledged to uphold Yemen???Ć?Ös right to security. Yemen, he said, cannot be the ???Ć?£corridor???Ć?for countries trying to undermine the security of the Kingdom, nor can Saudi Arabia allow other nations to undermine its sovereignty and security. Among the core foreign policy priorities, King Salman vowed to continue cooperating with the international community in combatting the extremism that threatens its borders and the region at large. Political solutions to international crises can safeguard values of tolerance and coexistence, paving the way for nations to ensure peace and achieve development, King Salman said. In his annual address to the Consultative Council, King Salman presented an optimistic outlook for the nation???Ć?Ös foreign and domestic policies. He said that Saudi Arabia has played an active and constructive role in the region and in important global undertakings with the international community is proceeding with ???Ć?£firm steps???Ć?to maintain its active role in the region and around the world. title: KSRELIEF Kingdom Provides Humanitarian Assistance to All Parts of Yemen
content: The King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Aid (KSRELIEF) reiterated today that it is ???Ć?Äcoordinating with the Coalition Forces and supported by the Kingdom???Ć?Ös ???Ć?ÄMinistry of Defense to gain access to all the governorates of Yemen and ???Ć?Äprovide medical assistance to the needy, provide financial assistance to ???Ć?Ähospitals and health centers inside Yemen, and providing them with all?é??forms ???Ć?Äof treatments, medicines and medical supplies, especially for children, ???Ć?Ämothers and chronically ill people.???Ć?KSRELIEF said that it has not hesitated to provide ???Ć?Ähumanitarian aid to all the needy across all of Yemen.???Ć?

title: Saudi Arabia Contributes $59 Million to UNRWA
content: Saudi Arabia has contributed more than $500 million to UNRWA to date. Over the past three years, Saudi Arabia has become UNRWA???Ć?Ös third largest donor. It has also been a valued member of the UNRWA Advisory Commission, which advises and assists the Commissioner-General in carrying out UNRWA???Ć?Ös mandate, since 2005. SFD Vice-Chairman and Managing Director Yousef Al-Bassam a

title: Kingdom Distributes over 42,000 School Bags to Syrian Children
content: The Saudi National Campaign to Support Syrian Brothers distributed 42,272 school bags containing school supplies to Syrian students in Turkey and Syria. The campaign distributed approximately 8,454 school bags in Turkey???Ć?Ös Antakya region and 33,818 inside Syria.

title: King Salman Center Delivers Aid to Al-Jawaf in Yemen
content: The King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Works has provided aid to more than 3,000 families in Al-Jawf Governorate in Yemen. The Center distributed over 6,000 food baskets and blankets and 500 tents.

title: Saudi Arabia Continues Distribution of Winter Clothes to Syrian
content: The Saudi National Campaign to Support Syrian Brothers distributed 6,686 pieces of winter clothes and related relief materials to 314 Syrian families in Mafraq Governorate as part of its 28th stage of its relief project ???Ć?£My Brother, Your Warmth, My Goal 3???Ć?

title: Half a Million School Bags Distributed to Syrian Children
content: The director of the campaign office in Lebanon Walid Al- Jalal, said ???Ć?£The campaign has given the educational side great importance, where it gave scholarships in the amount of $1.5 million to Syrian refugees. The Saudi National Campaign to Support the Syrian Brothers has distributed over 450,000 school bags, included stationery materials and school supplies, to displaced Syrian children in Jordan, Turkey and Lebanon.

title: Winter Clothes Distribute do Syrian Refugees
content: The Saudi National Campaign to Support the Syrian Brothers has dispatched 11 relief convoys to Syria included 377,370 pieces of winter clothes that have been specially made Turkey for the campaign to be distributed displaced Syrian in Syria.

title: Saudi Campaign Distributes over 257,000 Winter Clothes to Syrian Refugees
content: Saudi National Campaign to Support Syrian Brothers has continued its humanitarian efforts by distributing 257,312 winter clothe items to displaced Syrians in Lebanon, Jordan, and Turkey, as well as to displaced Syrians inside Syrians. The campaign goal is to distribute two million winter pieces of clothes, including 420,000 blankets, 360,000 jackets, one million sweaters, and 150,000 women's head scarves, in addition to 130,000 various pieces of other winter clothes. Dr. Saed Al-Orabi Al-Harthi, an adviser to the Minister of Interior and chairman of campaign, praised the great role of Saudi citizens and their generous contributions to help Syrian which exceeded SR 852 million ($227.2 million).

title: Saudi Clinics Treated 1479 Patients at Al-Zaatari Camp in Jordan
content: Saudi Specialist Clinics provided medical services to 1,479 displaced Syrians at Al-Zaatari Camp in Jordan last week. The clinics treats an average of 210 patients a day. The regional director of the National Saudi Campaign to Support Syrian Brothers, Dr. Badr Al-Samhan, said that more than 83,700 Syrian patients benefited from the Saudi Specialist Clinics 2015.

title: King Salman Center to Provide Yemen with 100,000 Foodstuff Baskets
content: In line with the directives of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques, The King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Works endorsed providing 100,000 baskets of foodstuff to Taiz, Yemeni. The action also came in the light of conclusions reached by participants of the peace talks, sponsored by the UN in Switzerland, in which humanitarian assistance was to be provided immediately to Taiz.

title: Saudi National Campaign Distributes 14,000 Winter Clothes to Syrian Families
content: The Saudi National Campaign to Support Brothers in Syria distributed 14,000 pieces of winter clothes to Syrian refugees in Mafraq Governorate, Jordan during the 14th station of its project My brother, Your warmth is My Goal 3. Through this station, the campaign provided winter clothes necessary for 3,500 refugees and included winter items such as blankets, jackets, sweaters and caps.

title: Kingdom Distributes Winter Clothes to Syrian Refugees
content: The Saudi National Campaign to Support Brothers in Syria is to begin the distribution of winter clothing to Syrian refugees in Jordan, Lebanon and Turkey, in addition to displaced Syrians inside Syria.

title: King Salman Center Begins Distribution of Aid to Cyclone Chapala Victims in Yemen
content: The King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Workings has begun the distribution relief supplies to the victims of Cyclone Chapala in Radhum Province, Yemen. The aid is part of the Kingdom???Ć?Ös support of the Yemeni people.

title: Food Distribution Continues in Yemen
content: The 5000 food baskets are part of a 130,000 food basket allotted for Taiz Province, in addition to the overall food security program implemented by King Salman Center to assist all the needy in all parts of Yemen in collaboration with the World Food Program at a total amount of more than $140 million. The King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Aid distributed 5000 food baskets to the needy in Yemen's Taiz Province. The distribution was carried out by the Islamic Relief Commission, the Islamic Relief Coalition, and local organizations.?é??

title: 5th Relief Plane Arrives in Yemen
content: The 5th relief airplane from the King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Works arrived at Yemen's Socotra Airport.  The aircraft was loaded with 10 tons of relief aid to be distributed in Socotra under supervision of the Center's specialized team.

title: Kingdom Distributes Winter Supplies to Syria Refugees in Lebanon
content: The director of the campaigns Lebanon operation, Walid Jalal, said that the distribution in other regions will be done sequentially according to the plan prepared for this purpose and priorities in provided the most needed areas. The Saudi National Campaign to Support the Brothers in Syria has distributed more than 18,500 pieces of winter clothes to Syrian families in Baalbek, Lebanon.

title: Kingdom Distributes Winter Clothing to Syrian Refugees
content: The Saudi National Campaign to Support Brothers in Syria has continued its winter project by distributing winter clothing to 428 Syrian families in northern Lebanon.

title: Saudi Campaign Launches New Winter Program for Syria Refuges in Jordan
content: The campaign part of seasonal projects being carried out by the Saudi National Campaign in Jordan, Turkey, Lebanon and inside Syria. The Saudi National Campaign to Support the Syrian Brothers has launched the third stage of its winter campaign distribute 13,000 winter clothes to more than 500 Syrian families at refugees in Ajlun Region in northern Jordan.

title: King Salman Center for Relief Provides Extra Aid for Yemeni Refugees in Djibouti
content: A Saudi aircraft dispatched by King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Aid arrived at today, carrying 10 tons of food and medical supplies for the mobile clinics for Yemeni refugees at the Obouk Camp.

title: Saudi Fund for Development to Guarantee $170 Million in Export Credit for Sudan
content: The Saudi Fund for Development (SDF) will guarantee $170.5 million in export credit to Sudan. The Vice President and Managing Director of the SDF Yousef Al-Bassam said that since the Saudi Exports Program was launched in 2002, the Fund guaranteed over $12.54 billion in export credit to developing countries.

title: Fourteenth Saudi Relief Plane Arrives in Aden
content: The fourteenth Saudi relief plane arrived today at Aden International Airport carrying 8 tons of children???Ć?Ös???Ć?supplies, including toys.

title: Eleventh Saudi Relief Plane Arrives in Yemen
content: The eleventh Saudi relief plane arrived today at Aden International Airport carrying 11 tons of medical supplies. The relief aid comes from the King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Center.

title: Tenth Saudi Relief Plane Arrives in Yemen
content: The tenth Saudi relief plane arrived today at Aden International Airport carrying 10 tons of medical equipment and supplies. The relief aid comes from the King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Center.

title: King Salman Center Signs Sgreement with UNDP
content: The King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Center signed a cooperation and partnership arrangement with the UN Development Program. Under the agreement, the King Salman Center will help promote small farmers, herders and food producers in Yemen.

title: Kingdom Delivers 100 Tons of Dates to Niger
content: Saudi Ambassador to Niger Turki bin Nagi Al-Ali handed over 100 tons of dates to Niger???Ć?Ös Food Crisis.

title: Ninth Saudi Relief Plane Arrives in Yemen
content: The ninth Saudi relief plane arrived today at Aden International Airport, carrying 10 tons of medical equipment and supplies. The relief aid comes from the King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Center.

title: Eighth Saudi Relief Plane Arrives in Yemen
content: The eighth Saudi relief plane arrived today at Aden International Airport, carrying 8 tons of infant formula. The relief aid comes from King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Center.

title: Seventh Saudi Relief Plane Arrives in Yemen
content: The seventh Saudi relief plane arrived today at Aden International Airport, carrying 10 tons of medical supplies. The relief aid comes from the King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Center.

title: Sixth Saudi Relief Plane Arrives in Aden
content: The sixth Saudi relief plane arrived in Aden carrying 11 tons of foodstuffs, accompanied by a team from the King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Center.

title: Saudi Aid Arrives in Albania
content: Saudi plane loaded with 180 tons of aid arrived today in Albania for victims of the recent floods. The aid consists of food, tents, blankets and floor mats.

title: King Salman Center Launches Food Distribution Campaign in Yemen
content: King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Center today began a food aid packages distribution campaign to needy and displaced people in the Yemeni governorate of Hadramout. The Center aims to distribute 100,000 food baskets in cooperation with the International Organization for Human Development of Muslim World League.

title: King Salman Center Supervisor Receives WHO Official
content: Dr. Abdullah Al-Rabeeah, advisor at the Royal Court and General Supervisor King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Center, met today with the Director of the Eastern Mediterranean Region of the World Health Organization (WHO) Dr. Alaa Al-Alwan. During their meeting, the two sides reviewed issues on the aid delivery's mechanism to Yemen and discussed the joint cooperation between the two sides in humanitarian and relief works. Dr. Al-Rabeeah and Dr. Al-Alwan also discussed the programs that would be implemented by the WHO in Yemen through a strategic partnership with King Salman Center.

title: Fourth Saudi Relief Plane Arrives in Yemen
content: The fourth Saudi relief plane arrived in Aden International Airport carrying 10 tons of medical supplies for patients with kidney failure. The medical supplies were accompanied by a team from the King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Center and Doctors Without Borders.

title: Saudi Health Centers Provide Psychological Services to some 1,400 Patients a Week in Zaatari Camp
content: The Vice Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Prince Mohammad bin Naif instructed that cases that cannot be treated at the Specialized Clinics, should be considered for treatment outside the camp and paid for by the campaign. The Psychological Support Unit of the Saudi Specialized Clinics treats 1,400 patients suffering from depression, after shock, phobia, and all other types of psychological problems. In addition, more than 2700 refugees benefitted from the Saudi Vaccine Program during the first half of 2015. The Saudi National Campaign to Support Syrian Brothers funded medical centers in Zaatari refugee camp in Jordan provided psychological services to about 1,400 Syrian patients per week.

title: Saudi Relief Ship Arrives in Aden Port
content: The Saudi relief ship ???Ć?śPath of the Goodness???Ć?arrived in the Yemeni port of Aden carrying 3,540 tons (7.8 million lbs.) of food and medical supplies. The aid ship arrived under the supervision of The King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Center and in coordination with the evacuation and humanitarian operations section at the Saudi Arabia Ministry of Defense.

title: Saudi Arabia Donates 134 Tons of Dates for Refugees in Eastern Cameroon
content: Saudi Arabia has provided 134 metric tones of dates to refugees in the east of the of Cameroon. The donation was handed over by Saudi Ambassador to Cameroon Mohammed Al-Msohr last Tuesday to the United Nations World Food Programme (WFP).

title: UN Special Envoy for Yemen Praises Kingdom???Ć?Ös World-Wide Humanitarian Work
content: The Special Envoy praised the leading role of Saudi Arabia in supporting relief and humanitarian works as well as UN activities worldwide, hailing the efforts exerted in this regard by King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Center. General Supervisor the King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Center Dr. Abdullah Al-Rabeeahin met today with Special Envoy of UN Secretary General for Yemen Ismail Ould Cheikh Ahmed in Riyadh.

title: Foreign Minister Receives UN Special Envoy for Yemen
content: Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir received today U.N. Secretary-General???Ć?Ös Special Envoy for Yemen Ismail Ould Cheikh Ahmed. During the meeting, they discussed the latest developments in Yemen.

title: King Salman Center for Relief Distributes Ramadan Food Baskets to 7,500 Families in Yemen
content: King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Center has distributed Ramadan food baskets to as many as 7,500 Yemeni families. The Center's spokesman, Rafat Al-Sabbagh, said that Ramadan food baskets were distributed yesterday in Yemen through the World Food Program in the framework of the agreement between the two sides.

title: Crown Prince Approves Fasting Program to Syrians and Somalis
content: Upon directives of Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz, Crown Prince Mohammed bin Naif bin Abdulaziz, Second Deputy Prime Minister, Minister of Interior and Director General of Saudi Relief Campaigns, approved the program of fasting for the Syrian and Somali peoples during the Holy Month of Ramadan at a cost of over $3.31 million.

title: Saudi National Campaign Signs Contract to Supply Medicines in Al-Zaatari Camp in Jordan
content: Saudi National Campaign to Support Brothers in Syria has signed a $32,000 contract to supply the 12th batch of medical supplies to Saudi Specialized Clinics in Al-Zaatari Camp in Jordan. The campaign's regional director, Dr. Badr bin Abdulrahman Al-Samhan, clinics provide health care to more than 83,000 Syrian refugees in Al-Zaatari Camp in Jordan.

title: Islamic Relief Organization Opens the Largest Station to Provide Water in West Sudan
content: The Jeddah-based International Islamic Relief has opened a new station to provide drinking and irrigation water in Ghabsha Badein area in western Sudan. The station will provide enough water for 20,000 people.

title: Kingdom Resume Aid Flight to Nepal, Second Relief Plane Arives in Kathmandu
content: Saudi Ambassador to Nepal Abdulnasser Al-Harthy said that the Saudi relief air lift was suspended for a brief time because of high volume of traffic and the limits imposed on large aircraft that carry more of 120 tons. Upon directive of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz, a second plane carrying 28 tons of humanitarian supplies for the people of Nepal arrived in Kathmandu yesterday. The aid consists of tent, blankets, food and medical supplies.

title: Saudi Relief Aid Arrives in Comoros
content: In implementation of the directives of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz, the Saudi relief plane carrying 100 tons of supplies, including 50 tons of foodstuffs and 50 tons of other supplies, including tents and blankets, arrived in the Comoros.

title: Saudi Committee for Relief of Afghan People Complete Winter Relief Project
content: Dr. Saed Al-Orabi Al-Harthi, an adviser to the Minister of Interior and chairman of the committee, said that the committee???Ć?Ös humanitarian and relief services will continue in a number of areas, including education and health services. Dr. Al-Harthi explained that SR 137 million ($36.5 million) has been approved for educational and health projects by the general supervisor of the committee, Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif bin Abdulaziz, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior. The Committee distributed 55,000 food baskets and 50,000 blankets to 55,000 needy families affected by the natural disasters across Afghanistan.?é?? Saudi Committee for the Relief of the Afghan People ended the third phase of the winter relief project. The $3.2 million project includes the distribution of food baskets and blankets. The relief supplies were distributed in cooperation with Afghan authorities, including the Afghan Red Crescent Society, and the Saudi Embassy in Kabul.

title: Aid ships Arrive in Yemen
content: Official spokesman for the King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Works Raafat Al-Sabagh said that several ships laden with fuel and humanitarian relief supplies, including medical supplies, have been allowed to enter Yemeni ports. Al-Sabagh said a UN ship loaded with diesel, a Liberian ship carrying 23 million metric tons of gasoline and liquid cargo, and an Indian and Djibouti cargo ships docked at Yemeni ports.

title: King Salman to Attend Opening of Humanitarian Relief Center
content: Dr. Abdullah bin Abdulaziz Al-Rabiah, an advisor at the Royal Court and the general supervisor of the center, said that the center reflects the role the Kingdom plays in humanitarian relief efforts. Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz on Wednesday will attend the laying of the cornerstone of the headquarters of the King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Works. This was announced by advisor at the Royal Court and General Supervisor of the center

title: Saudi National Campaign Sends 41 Tons of Relief Materials to Southern Syria
content: Saudi National Campaign to Support Brothers in Syria has dispatched its 28th convoy for displaced civilians in southern Syria. The Campaign's Regional Director Dr. Badr Al-Samhan said that the aid convoy consists of 10 trucks containing 41.6 tons of relief supplies valued at $512,000. The aid convoy is expected to enter Syria across the Jordanian border under the umbrella of U.N. Security Council Resolution 2191.

title: Saudi Arabia, UNHCR Sign MoU to Assist Syrians
content: The funded projects by Saudi Arabia aim to rebuild and rehabilitate a total of 4,650 homes in Damascus, Homs, Hama, Daraa and Idlib, in addition to providing public facilities and services, which will benefit a total of 23,250 displaced Syrians. Saudi Arabia and the High Commissioner of the United Nations for Refugees (UNHCR) signed a cooperation agreement under which the Kingdom will contribute $5 million to help the displaced Syrians. This came on the sidelines of the 3rd International Pledging Humanitarian Conference for Syria held in Kuwait today in the presence of the Minister of Finance Ibrahim bin Abdulaziz Al-Assaf. The agreement was signed by Saudi Fund for Development representing the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia.

title: Saudi Distributes Bread to Displaced Syrians
content: Saudi National Campaign to Support Brothers in Syria continued providing bread for the Syrian displaced people in Aleppo and Idlib through mobile bakeries. Since the program began about two years ago to now, over 60,000 loaves of bread have been distributed daily to nearly 4,000 Syrian families for a total of some 33 million loaves at a cost about $544,000.

title: Foreign Minister: There Is No Blockade of Qatar
content: ???Ć?£We have exercised our sovereign right to prohibit them from using our airspace. We have allowed the movement of families between the two countries to happen so that we don???Ć?Öt divide families, and we are prepared to provide Qatar with food and medical supplies, should they need this, through the King Salman Center.???Ć????Ć?£There is no blockade of Qatar. Qatar is free to go ???Ć?the ports are open, the airports are open. What we have done is we have denied them use of our airspace, and this is our sovereign right. The limitation on the use of Saudi airspace is only limited to Qatari airways or Qatari-owned aircraft, not anybody else. The seaports of Qatar are open; there is no blockade on them. Qatar can move goods in and out whenever they want; they just cannot use our territorial waters,???Ć?the Foreign Minister said. Saudi Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir met today with U.S. Secretary of State Rex Tillerson in Washington, D.C. Before the meeting, Minister Al-Jubeir commented on the situation in Qatar, emphasizing that there is no blockade in place.

title: Deputy Crown Prince Receives Phone Call From US Secretary of State
content: During the conversation, they reviewed bilateral relations, latest developments in the region, and the joint efforts of the two countries in combating terrorism, extremism and financing terrorist organizations to achieve security and stability in the region. The Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense received today a telephone call from US Secretary of State Rex Tillerson.

title: Deputy Crown Prince Receives Telephone Call from US Secretary of Defense
content: The two sides looked forward to more work for unifying international efforts in order to achieve security and stability in the region. The US Secretary of Defense lauded Saudi Arabia's active role in combating terrorism and countering extremism. Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense, received today a telephone call from US Secretary of Defense James Mattis.?é?? During the telephone conversation, they discussed the current situations and latest developments in the region.

title: King Salman Receives Telephone Call from U.S. President Trump
content: President Trump lauded the leading role of the Kingdom in combating terrorism and its strenuous efforts to dry up its sources as well as the United States of America's gearing forward to enhance joint action between the two countries to fight extremism and exert efforts for achieving security and stability in the region. The Custodian of Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz received a telephone call today from U.S. President Donald Trump.?é?? During the conversation, the historical ties between the two friendly countries as well as the developments of situations in the region and the world at large were discussed.

title: King Salman Chairs Weekly Cabinet's Session
content:
The Cabinet stressed the Kingdom's strong condemnation of the bombings in the Iraqi capital, Baghdad, the series of bombings in the Afghan capital, Kabul, the terrorist attacks that took place in London, expressing condolence to the governments and peoples of Iraq, Afghanistan and Britain on the victims of bombings and terrorist incidents, wishing the wounded a speedy recovery. 
?é?? In line with a noble directive, the Cabinet was briefed by Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense, on the outcomes of his visit to Russia, meeting with the Russian President Vladimir Putin, discussion of reinforcing Saudi-Russian understanding on the oil accord, led by the Kingdom, which guarantee for the first time, in the history of the region the cooperation of OPEC member states and non-member states, including Russia, in addition to review bilateral relations, in the field of investments and various oil and petrochemical industries and uniting efforts to combat terrorism and extremist thought. 
The Cabinet pointed out that the decision of various sisterly countries to severe diplomatic relations with the state of Qatar was an expression of keenness to preserve the unity of the Arab nation and take stance against Qatari acts, aimed at destabilizing security, in the region, contravening accords, charters, principles of international law and good neighborliness. 
?é?? The Cabinet confirmed that the decision to cut diplomatic and consulate relations with Qatar came emanating from the Kingdom's observing its sovereign rights guaranteed by international law and in protection of its national security against the dangers of terrorism and extremism as the Kingdom has taken this decisive decision due to the grave violations practiced by the authorities in Doha secretly and publicly throughout previous years. This aimed to strike a wedge in the Saudi domestic ranks, instigating people to disobey the State and breach its sovereignty through securing safe havens for numerous terrorist and sectarian groups targeting the stability of the region. The Cabinet reiterated that the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia will remain supportive for the Qatari sisterly people and their security and stability regardless of the hostile practice committed by the authorities in Doha. King Salman began the meeting by briefing the Cabinet on the outcome of his audience with King Abdullah Al-Hussein of Jordan, the contents of a verbal message he sent to Sheikh Sabah Al-Ahmed Al-Jaber Al-Sabah, Emir of Kuwait; his meeting with Sheikh Mohammed bin Zayed Al Nahyan, Crown Prince of Abu-Dhabi, the contents of the telephone call the monarch received from President Emmanuel Macron of France, as well as his meeting with the Secretary General of the GCC Dr. Abdullatif bin Rashed Al-Zayani. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz chaired the weekly Cabinet meeting at Al-Salam Palace in Jeddah this evening.

title: Kingdom of Saudi Arabia Cuts Off Diplomatic and Consular Relations With the State of Qatar
content: The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia affirms that it has long been patient, despite the fact that the authorities in Doha have continued to evade their commitments and conspire against the Kingdom, out of concern for the people of Qatar, who remain a natural and genuine extension of their brethren in the Kingdom and an integral part of its history and family. The Kingdom will continue to support the people of Qatar, its security and stability regardless of the hostile practices the authorities in Doha continue to carry out. In accordance with the decision to cut off diplomatic and consular relations, Saudi citizens are prohibited from traveling to, residing in or passing through Qatar. Saudi residents and visitors in Qatar are advised to leave its territories within no more than 14 days. Unfortunately, for security reasons, this prevents Qatari citizens' entry to or transit through the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, and Qatari residents and visitors must leave Saudi territories within 14 days. All the while, the Kingdom confirms its commitment and care to provide all the services and facilities for Qatari Haj and Umrah pilgrims. Since 1995, the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and its sister nations have made strenuous and continued efforts to urge the authorities in Doha to abide by its commitments and agreements, yet, they have repeatedly violated their international obligations and the agreements they signed under the umbrella of the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) for Arab States to cease the hostilities against the Kingdom and stand against terrorist groups and activities of which the latest was their failure to implement the Riyadh Agreement. This decisive decision was taken by the government of Saudi Arabia as a result of grave violations that the authorities in Doha continued to commit over the past years, covertly and publicly, with the aim of dividing internal Saudi ranks, inciting against the State, infringing on its sovereignty, embracing various terrorist and sectarian groups aimed at destabilizing the region including the Muslim Brotherhood Group, Daesh (ISIS) and Al-Qaeda, constantly promoting the literature and plans of these groups through its media, supporting the activities of the Iranian-backed terrorist groups in the governorate of Qatif in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and our sister nation the Kingdom of Bahrain, its continued financing, adopting and sheltering of extremists who seek to undermine the stability and unity at home and abroad, and using the media to fuel the internal strife. Additionally the support of the authorities in Doha for the Houthi militia led coup was made clear to the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, even after the announcement of the Coalition to Support the Legitimacy in Yemen. This decision by the Kingdom was also taken in solidarity with the sister Kingdom of?é??Bahrain, which is subjected to terrorist campings and operations supported by the authorities in Doha. An official source stated that the Government of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, emanating from exercising its sovereign rights guaranteed by international law, to protect its national security from the dangers of terrorism and extremism, has decided to cut off diplomatic and consular relations with the State of Qatar. It also decided to close all land, sea and air ports, to prevent crossing into Saudi territories, airspace and territorial waters, and begin immediate legal procedures to create an understanding with our sister nations, allies and partners, and international companies to implement the same procedure as soon as possible for all means of transport to and from the State of Qatar, for reasons relating to Saudi national security.

title: Saudi Arabia Condemns Series of Attacks in Kabul
content: An official source at the Saudi Ministry of Foreign Affairs expressed the Kingdom's strong condemnation of the series of attacks in Afghanistan's capital, Kabul, killing and wounding a number of people.?é?? The source offered the Kingdom's condolences to the families of the victims and to the Afghan Government and people, wishing a speedy recovery to the wounded.

title: King Salman Condoles UK Prime Minister o London Terror Attack
content: The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz sent a cable of condolences to British Prime Minister Theresa May following the terrorist attack that took place in London.?é?? In his cable, King Salman strongly condemn the heinous criminal act and offered his deepest condolences.

title: King Salman Consoles British Prime Minister on Manchester Bombing
content: Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz has sent a cable of condolences to British Prime Minister Theresa May following the terrorist bombing in Manchester. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques strongly condemned this criminal act, wished speedy recovery to the injured and stressed the importance of international efforts to confront and eradicate terrorism.

title: King Salman Calls British Prime Minister To Express Support Following Manchester Terror Attack
content: During the conversation, King Salman confirmed the stance of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia with all its might with Britain in the war against terrorism. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz expressed the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia's strong denunciation of the Manchester terrorist attack, noting that it contradicts the principles and ethics of Islamic religion. In a telephone call to the British Prime Minister Theresa May today, King Salman extended sincere condolence and sympathy to the families of the victims, wishing the injured a quick recovery.

title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet Session
content: The Cabinet also lauded the exchange of the memorandum of understanding between the GCC countries and the US to establish a center to dry-up the sources of funding of terrorism. The Cabinet asserted that the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques' announcing and launching of the Global Center for Combating Extremist Ideology "Etidal", in the presence of U.S. President Donald Trump and world leaders. The King also drew the attention to the importance of achieving peace between Palestinians and Israelis, intensifying efforts to resolve the Syrian crisis, refusing to harm the Islamic countries' relations with friendly countries or exploiting Islam as a cover for political purposes fueling hatred, extremism and terrorism as well as religious and sectarian conflicts, citing acts done by the Iranian regime, its affiliated groups and organizations and other terrorist organizations. The Cabinet highlighted the valuable contents of the speech delivered by the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques pointing to the King's emphasis on the responsibility of the Arab and Islamic countries to be united in the fight against the forces of evil and extremism and terrorism in all its forms. The King also reviewed historical relations between the two countries and ways of enhancing them in various fields and discussed the latest world developments, They also exchanged of a number of trade agreements and investment opportunities exceeding $280 billion. King Salman said that the historic agreement signed by the GCC with the United States take firm measures against the financing of terrorism; and the announcement of the launch of the Global Center for Combating Extremist Ideology aimed at spreading the principles of moderation. King Salman began by expressing his appreciation to US President Donald J. Trump and leaders of Arab and Islamic countries who participated in the 17th Consultative Meeting of Leaders of Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC), the U.S.-GCC Summit, and the Arab-Islamic-American Summit hosted by the Kingdom yesterday. title: Saudi Foreign Minister and US Secretary of State Confirm Strategic Partnership

content: "This visit will enhance, we believe, cooperation between the US and Arab and Islamic countries in combating terrorism and extremism. He described the US President's visit to the Kingdom as historic by all standards. ???Ć?£The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia is the first partner for the United States of America in the Arab and Islamic nations.?é?? title: GCC Ministerial Council Concludes 142nd Session in Riyadh
content:
24. The Ministerial Council congratulated the Republic of Somalia on the completion of the electoral process and the emphasis on support in the efforts to rebuild and meet the economic and development challenges and fight terrorism. On Somalia: 
23. The Ministerial Council affirmed the positions and resolutions of GCC on Lebanon and welcomed the visit made by President Michel Aoun to the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and the State of Qatar in January 2017.?é?? It stressed the importance of relations between the GCC States and Lebanon at all levels, In order to achieve security and stability and prosperity. On Lebanon: 
22. The Ministerial Council affirmed the positions and decisions of the GCC on the Libyan crisis, stressing the need to implement the Al-Sukhairat Agreement signed between the Libyan parties in December 2015 as a framework for ending the Libyan crisis. The Council reiterated the keenness of the GCC States on the security, stability and unity of the Libyan territories. On Libya: 21. The Ministerial Council reaffirmed its support for Security Council Resolution 2107 (2013), which unanimously decided to refer the file of prisoners of war, missing persons, Kuwaiti property and the National Archives to the United Nations Mission to Monitor this issue. The Council also regrets that no progress has been made in any. The Council calls upon the Iraqi Government and the United Nations Assistance Mission for Iraq (UNAMI) to make every effort to reach a solution to this humanitarian and other related issues. 20.
