The Ministerial Council expressed its rejection of the statements made by Iraqi Vice President Nuri al-Maliki and the official spokesman of the Iraqi Foreign Ministry on the fair judicial sentences that were committed against those convicted of terrorist cases in the Kingdom of Bahrain, stressing the rejection by the GCC States of any form of interference in their internal affairs. 19. The Ministerial Council reaffirmed the Permanent Council's positions and resolutions on Iraq and expressed its support for the Government of Iraq in taking measures to combat terrorism aimed at achieving security and stability in Iraq. 
On Iraq : The Ministerial Council condemned Houthi and Saleh's forces for targeting of the population centers in Saudi Arabia and Yemen by ballistic missiles. The Ministerial Council lauded the meeting of the Quartet with the participation of the Sultanate of Oman on Yemen held in Bonn on 16 February, 2017, calling upon the Houthi and Saleh group to commit to peace and resume consultations under the auspices of the United Nations in accordance with the Gulf Initiative and its executive mechanism, outputs of the comprehensive national dialogue conference, the Security Council Resolution 2216, and the framework presented by the Special Envoy of the UN Secretary General which includes security and political measures to end the crisis. 18. The Ministerial Council confirmed the GCC firm stances and decisions on the crisis in Yemen and welcomed the issuance of UN Security Council Resolution 2342 under Chapter VII of the Charter of the United Nations which stressed the national, regional and international key points to reach a political solution to the crisis. 17. The Ministerial Council welcomed the final communiqu?ā?? issued by the 9th Coordination Meeting of the Group of Senior Donors to Support the Humanitarian Situation in Syria held in Kuwait on 16 January, 2017. 16. The Ministerial Council welcomed the US President Donald Trump's commitment to sign an executive decision to develop a plan for the establishment of safe regions in Syria, hoping that this will contribute to maintaining the safety of civilians in accordance with the relevant international resolutions. 15. The Ministerial Council stressed the GCC positions and resolutions on the Syrian crisis and followed up the results of the meetings of the Astana Conference between the Syrian opposition delegations and the regime in January and February 2017 and the Geneva Conference of 23 February, 2017.?é?? The Council stressed the need to stabilize the cease-fire and establish an effective and practical monitoring mechanism to achieve a political solution in accordance with the Geneva Declaration 1 (June 2012). 14. The Ministerial Council confirmed the GCC stances and decisions in this regard that Iran abides by the agreement reached with the the P5+1 (the permanent members of the United Nations Security Council plus Germany) in July 2015, on its nuclear program and to implement the Security Council Resolution 2231 (July 2015) on the nuclear agreement. 13. The Ministerial Council expressed its condemnation of the Iranian regime's irresponsible provocative statements and acts of aggression against the Kingdom of Bahrain, calling upon the Iranian regime to stop the policies that feed sectarian conflicts. 12. The Ministerial Council stressed the GCC stances and resolutions regarding the GCC-Iranian relations and confirmed the necessity of fully abiding by the basic principles based on the Charter of the United Nations, the international law, the principles of good neighborliness, respecting the sovereignty of States, non-interference in internal affairs, peaceful settlement of disputes, and rejection of sectarianism.?é?? The Council expressed its full rejection of the continuation of Iranian interference in the internal affairs of the GCC States and the region. The Council calls upon Iran to respond to the UAE's efforts to resolve the issue through direct negotiations or go to the International Court of Justice. 11. The Ministerial Council confirmed the GCC permanent positions and resolutions which were confirmed by all previous statements rejecting Iranian continued occupation of the three islands of Greater Tunb, Lesser Tunb and Abu Musa of the United Arab Emirates.?é?? The Council stressed its support of the UAE sovereign right over its three islands: Greater Tunb, Lesser Tunb and Abu Musa and the territorial water, airspace, continental shelf and exclusive economic zone of the three islands as an integral part of the territory of the United Arab Emirates.?é?? The Council considered that any decisions, practices or acts carried out by Iran on the three islands are null and void as well as do not change any historical and legal facts. Iranian occupation of the three islands belonging to the United Arab Emirates: 10. The Ministerial Council welcomed the outcomes of the Peace Conference held in Paris on January 15, 2017, to revive the Palestinian-Israeli peace negotiations and its statement which calls to achieve a lasting and just peace in the region and calls upon the international community to carry out its responsibilities. 9. The Ministerial Council condemned the actions taken by the Israeli occupation authorities in Al-Quds (Jerusalem) to change its identity.?é?? The Council welcomed the Security Council Resolution 2334 which denounced the Israeli settlement.?é?? The Council strongly condemned the decision of the Israeli Parliament to allow settlement gatherings on the Palestinian private lands, which is a flagrant violation of the rights of the Palestinian people and the international law, calling for pressure on the Israeli government to stop its illegal practices in the Palestinian occupied territories. 8. The Ministerial Council stressed the GCC firm and permanent stances and resolutions towards the Palestinian Cause, stressing that a comprehensive, just and lasting peace can be achieved only with the full withdrawal of Israel from all the Arab territories occupied in 1967 and with establishment of an independent Palestinian state with East Jerusalem as its capital in accordance with the Arab Peace Initiative and the international relevant resolutions. The situation in the Palestinian territories and developments of the Arab-Israeli conflict: 7. The Ministerial Council expressed its deep condemnation of the statement made by the representative of the Swiss Confederation before the United Nations Human Rights Council in its 34th session in Geneva on human rights in Bahrain. The council expressed its categorical rejection of the allegations in the statement which ignores the efforts exerted by Bahrain in protecting and enhancing human rights. The Council expressed hope that the Swiss government is to review its position and take information on human rights from the reliable sources. The Council stressed that these rejected positions do not help to develop and strengthen the common relations between the GCC and the Swiss Union. 6. The Ministerial Council welcomed the statement issued by the US Department of State, which included placing of two affiliates of the Saraya al-Ashtar terrorist organization on its lists of Specially Designated Global Terrorists.?é?? The Councils stressed that this position reflects the determination of the United States to address all forms of terrorism at the regional and international levels. The council stressed that this stance represents a tangible support for the efforts of the Kingdom of Bahrain to promote security and peace in Bahrain. 5. The Ministerial Council lauded the opening of the NATO Regional Center for the Istanbul Cooperation Initiative countries in Kuwait to enhance aspects of joint coordination and cooperation in the security fields. 4. The Ministerial Council condemned the terrorist attack on the guest house of Kandahar Governor which resulted in the martyrdom of the United Arab Emirates ambassador to Afghanistan and five diplomats during a humanitarian mission within the assistance program provided by the United Arab Emirates in Afghanistan. 3. The Ministerial Council lauded the efforts exerted by the security services in the Kingdom of Bahrain and its high efficiency in maintaining the security and stability of the Kingdom of Bahrain and its territorial integrity, praising its efforts to foil smuggling of a number of wanted fugitives in terrorist cases. The Council supported all efforts taken by Bahrain in maintaining its security and stability. 2. The Ministerial Council hailed an award received by Crown Prince Mohammed bin Naif bin Abdulaziz, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of the Interior, from the Central Intelligence Agency (CIA) for the distinguished intelligence work in the field of combating terrorism, which is an international recognition of the Kingdom's outstanding work in the fight against terrorism. 1. The Ministerial Council affirmed the GCC constant stances and resolutions against terrorism and extremism.?é?? The council reviewed the developments in this regard. It also confirmed a continuation of the GCC support regarding the international coalition to fight the Daesh (ISIS) terrorist organization in Syria and Iraq, stressing the need to protect civilians and return of the displaced people to their cities and villages.?é?? The council welcomed outcomes of the international conference to promote efforts of the Islamic and friendly countries against ISIS terrorist organization held in Riyadh on January 15, 2017. The council considered this conference as a commitment of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia to support all international efforts to combat terrorism. Combating Terrorism: The Ministerial Council reiterated its concern over the continued abduction of a number of Qatari citizens in Iraq, calling upon the Iraqi government to assume its responsibility to ensure the safety of the kidnapped citizens and release them. The Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) Ministerial Council welcomed the outcomes of the 28th Arab Summit held Jordan on 29 March 2017 and its constructive resolutions that serve the Arab issues and contribute to the achievement of the objectives and aspirations of the Arab States.

title: King Salman Hold Sideline Meetings During Arab League Summit in Jordan
content: On the sidelines of the 28th Arab League summit on the banks of the Dead Sea in Jordan, the Custodian of Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz met separately with King Hamad bin Isa Al Khalifa of Bahrain, President Mohamed Beji Caid Essebsi of Tunisia, Sheikh Sabah Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah, the Emir of Kuwait, President Abdel Fattah El-Sisi of Egypt, Prime Minister Dr. Haider Al-Abadi of Iraq, and Fayez Mustafa al-Sarraj, the Chairman of the Presidential Council of Libya.?é?? During the meetings, they reviewed bilateral relations and the agenda of the summit.

title: King Salman Arrives in Amman for Arab League Summit
content: The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz arrived in Amman, Jordan for an official visit to Jordan and attend the 28th Arab League summit.?é?? King Salman was received by King Abdullah II bin Al-Hussain of Jordan.

title: Kingdom condemns London Terrorist Attack
content: Saudi Arabia today condemed terror attack in London.?é?? In a statement, the Foreign Ministry has expressed the Kingdom's solidarity and support for the United Kingdom's war on terrorism and extremism, and offered its condolences to the families of the victims, the government and people of the United Kingdom.

title: Crown Prince Meets Director of US National Security Agency
content: Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior met at the Interior Ministry today with Adm. Michael S. Rogers, director of the US National Security Agency. During the meeting, they discussed a number of security issues of mutual interest, including enhancing joint cooperation to combat extremism and terrorism.

title: Foreign Minister Meets With Secretary Tillerson
content: Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir met this afternoon with US Secretary of State Rex Tillerson at the State Department.?é?? During their talks, they discussed bilateral relations following the the recently visit by Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman to Washington.

title: King Telephones British Prime Minister to Express Condolances
content: During the call, King Salman reiterated the Kingdom's strong denunciation of such criminal acts, which contravenes with humanitarian and ethical principles, pointing out that the Kingdom, fully, takes the side of Britain, with all potentials and concluding that terrorist acts underscore the need and importance of a world concerted collaboration to eradicate terrorism phenomenon and dry up its sources. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz Saud called British Prime Minister Theresa May today to offer his condolences to the victims of the London terror attack.

title: King Salman Condoles British Prime Minister on Victims of Terrorist
content: Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of the Interior, and Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Defense Minister, sent simular cables condolences and condemnations of terrorist attacks. The King said in his cable, "We have received the news of the terrorist attack that took place in front of the British Parliament which resulted in deaths and injuries. As we strongly condemn this terrorist act, we, on behalf of the people and Government of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, offer our deepest condolences to Your Excellency, the families of the victims and friendly British people. We re-confirm the firm position of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia on rejecting such terrorist acts in all their forms and manifestations, re-emphasizing the importance of international efforts to confront and eradicate them". The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz has sent a cable of condolences to British Prime Minister Theresa May following the terror attack in London.

title: Houth Ballistic Missiles Intercepted by Saudi Air Defense Forces
content: The?é??United Nations has?é??confirmed that Houthi militias and ousted Saleh forces have been using ballistic missiles against the Kingdom and Yemeni cities since June 16, 2015.?é?? The report concluded that due to the inaccuracy of these missiles which do not distinguish between military and civilian targets, their use is a violation of the international human law. Inside Yemen, the militias have continuously launched ballistic missiles at Marib and Taiz Provinces to break the resistance to the Houthi and ousted Saleh militias. All missiles were successfully destroyed by Royal Saudi Air Defense Forces without causing any casualties or material damages. Houthi militias have launched dozens of ballistic missiles at populated areas in a number of Saudi cities along the border, including Khamis Mushait, Jazan, Dhahran Al-Janoub, Najran, Taif, and Makkah.?é?? All missiles were successfully destroyed by Royal Saudi Air Defense Forces before the reached their intended targets and without causing any casualties or material damages. The Command of the Coalition Forces Supporting the ???Ć?ÄLegitimate Government of Yemen announced that Royal Saudi Air Defense Forces intercepted a number of ballistic missile launched by Iranian-backed Houthi militias and forces loyal to former president Ali Abdullah Saleh towards Saudi Arabia and liberated Yemeni cities since 2015.

title: Foreign Minister Meets With Iraqi Prime Minister in Washington
content: Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir met today in Washington with Iraqi Prime Minister Dr. Haider Al-Abadi on the sidelines of the Ministerial Plenary for the Global Coalition Working to Defeat ISIS.?é???é??During the meeting, they discussed the topics on the agenda of the conference, bilateral relations and means of enhancing them in all fields in addition to regional and international developments.

title: Foreign Minister Participates in Paris Group Meeting on Syria in Washington
content: Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir and Ambassador Abdullah Al-Saud participated in a meeting of the Paris Group on Syria at the French Embassy in Washington on Tuesday to discussed developments in the Syrian arena.

title: King Salman Arrives Back in Riyadh
content: The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz arrived in Riyadh tonight, following trips to Malaysia, Indonesia, Brunei, Japan and China.

title: Coalition Navy Intercepts Three Armed Houthi Boats
content: The coalition navy destroyed one of the boats and captured another, and arrested its crew.?é?? A third boat managed to escape to the high seas.?é?? In January,?é??armed Houthi movement attacked a Saudi warship off the western coast of Yemen, causing an explosion that killed two crew members and injured three others. The Saudi-led Arab Coalition to Support Legitimacy in Yemen has intercepted three small Houthi boats off Port Midi in Yemen yesterday.?é?? The Coalition announced that the Houthis were planning to use the boats to attack coalition ships patrolling the off the coast of Yemen.

title: Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman Concludes US Visit
content: On March 16, Prince Mohammed met with Secretary of Defense Jim Mattis at the Pentagon to review progress in the bilateral defense relationship, including the two nations???Ć?efforts to confront the most pressing security challenges to the region, such as Iran's destabilizing regional activities and the ongoing campaign to defeat Daesh (ISIS) and other terrorist organizations. Saudi Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, the Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense, concluded his visit to Washington, DC.?é?? Prince Mohammed met with President Donald Trump and Vice President Mike Pence on Tuesday at the White House. During the meeting, they reaffirmed the importance of the Saudi-U.S. strategic partnership.?é?? The leaders stressed their determination to strengthen their cooperation in the economic, commercial, investment, and energy fields.

title: King Salman Holds Talks With Chinese President
content: King Salman is paying a state visit to China from March 15 to 18 as a guest of President Xi. Following their talks, King Salman and President Xi attended the signing of a number of memoranda of understanding and cooperative agreements covering a wide range of areas, including trade and investment, space exploration, educational, labor, the peaceful use of nuclear technology, drone manufacturing, energy, and broadcasting. King Salman expressed his pleasure at the level of the bilateral relationship, especially the level of the strategic partnership. King Salman said the world today is witnessing many challenges that threaten security and stability topped by the phenomenon of terrorism, interference in internal affairs of states, the arms race, and the clash of cultures. The king said that these challenges necessitate that the international community exert more efforts and work together to confront these challenges and enhance international peace and security.?é?? The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz and President Xi Jinping of the People's Republic of China held talks at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing today. During their meeting, they reviewed the bilateral relationships and prospects of joint cooperation in various fields, and the latest developments at the international arena.

title: King Salman Arrives in China
content: The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud arrived in the People???Ć?Ös Republic of China today on an official visit. The King was received at Beijing International Airport by State Councilor Yang Jiechi.

title: Kingdom, Toyota sign MoU on Vehicles and Parts Development in Saudi Arabia
content: Abdul Latif Jameel as a local distributor for Toyota will be also taking part in the joint feasibility study.?é?? Toyota remains the leader in Saudi and other Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) car markets with more than 500,000 units sold in the GCC in 2016. The study would take into account the evaluation of development of a local supply base using materials produced by major Saudi companies like Sabic, Maaden, Petro Rabigh, and other major industrial companies in the Kingdom.?é?? It would also be looking into the development and attraction of talented Saudi workforce and put in place the adequate training programs. The MoU is part of NICDP???Ć?Ös plans for the development of a globally competitive automotive industry in Saudi Arabia, and confirming the intent of both parties to continue with the evaluation of the project as part of the Kingdom???Ć?Ös 2030 vision. The MoU was signed in Tokyo on the sidelines of the visit by the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz to Japan. The Saudi National Industrial Cluster Development Program (NICDP) and Toyota Motor Corporation today signed a memorandum of understanding for the feasibility study of an industrial project to produce vehicles and parts Saudi Arabia.

title: King Salman Holds Talks with Japanese Prime Minister
content: The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz held today talks with the Prime Minister of Japan Shinzo Abe.?é?? King Salman arrived in Tokyo yesterday from Indonesia.

title: Minister Al-Jubeir Meets Secretary of State Tillerson
content: Foreign Minster Adel al-Jubeir met today with Secretary of State Rex Tillerson at the State Department.?é?? During their meeting, they reviewed bilateral relations the latest regional and international developments, including the situations in Syria, Yemen, Libya and Iraq. Minister Al-Jubeir and Secretary Tillerson also discussed the efforts being exerted by both counties to fight terrorism and combat extremism.

title: Deputy Crown Prince Arrives In United States for a Meeting With President Trump
content: Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, the Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defence, arrived this morning in Washington for a working visit starting Thursday.?é?? During the visit, he will meet US President Donald Trump and a number of officials to discuss the strengthening of bilateral relations between the two countries and regional issues of mutual interest.

title: Vice Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Chairs Cabinet Session
content: The Cabinet reiterated the condemnation of terrorist attacks which took place at the military hospital in Kabul, Afghanistan and a military base in Mali. The Cabinet stressed the decisions of the session, confirming the full commitment and adherence to the Arab Peace Initiative without change, and the need to enable the Palestinian people to exercise their inalienable rights. The Cabinet was then briefed on the latest developments on the Arab and Islamic arenas. The Cabinet lauded the decisions adopted by the 147th session of the Council of Arab Foreign Ministers held in Cairo. In this context, the Cabinet expressed appreciation to the Arab foreign ministers for thanking the Saudi-led Arab Coalition to Support Legitimacy in Yemen in its support of Yemen's legitimacy and people, the resumption of the peace process, and endeavors to stop war and restore the Yemeni state.?é?? The Cabinet also appreciated the humanitarian efforts of the King Salman Humanitarian Aid & Relief Center (KSRelief) in providing assistance to the Yemeni people.?é?? The Cabinet also welcomed the Council of Arab Foreign Ministers???Ć?Ös statements unilaterally condemning the coup leaders in Yemen, their violations against the people of Yemen as well as the Iran???Ć?Ös continual interventions that violate the security and stability of Yemen. Crown Prince Mohammed briefed the Cabinet on the contents of the message sent by the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz to Sheikh Sabah Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah of Kuwait, the telephone call King Salman had with President Joko Widodo of Indonesia, and his meetings in Indonesian.?é?? Crown Prince Mohammad also briefed the cabinet on his meeting with New Zealand???Ć?Ös Foreign Minister Murray MuCully. Vice Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Prince Mohammad bin Naif chaired the Cabinet's weekly session at Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh this afternoon.

title: King Salman Leaves Indonesia on His Way to Japan
content: Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz today left Indonesia on his way to Japan on an official visit.

title: King Salman Arrives in Japan
content: The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz arrived in Japan today on an official visit.?é?? King Salman was received at Haneda International Airport in Tokyo by Crown Prince Naruhito of Japan,

title: Royal Court: Deputy Crown Prince Leaves to United States for a Meeting With President Trump
content: The Royal Court?é?? announced today that Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, the Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defence, left this morning for?é??the United States on an?é??working visit starting?é??Thursday.?é?? During the visit, he will meet US President Donald Trump and a number of officials to discuss the strengthening of bilateral relations between the two countries and regional issues of mutual interest.

title: King Salman Expressed Condolences to Afghan President on Terrorist Attack
content: Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz expressed his condolences to ???Ć?ÄPresident Dr. Ashraf Ghani Ahmadzai of Afghanistan on the victims of the terrorist attack at the ???Ć?Ämilitary hospital in Kabul.?é?? During the telephone conversation, King Salman pray to Allah ???Ć?ÄAlmighty to grant the victims rest in peace, wishing a speedy recovery to the injured.???Ć?

title: Foreign Minister receives UN Envoy to Yemen
content: Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir received in Riyadh today Special Envoy of UN Secretary-General to Yemen Ismail Ould Cheikh Ahmed.?é?? During the meeting, they discussed the latest developments on the Yemeni arena.

title: Kingdom Dismisses as Baseless and Unfounded Reports It Is Buying an Atoll in the Maldives
content: The Saudi Embassy in the Maldives today dismissing as baseless and unfounded allegations that Saudi Arabia is purchasing an atoll from the Maldives. In a statement, the embassy said that ???Ć?£the Government of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia neither invest in such project nor has an intention doing so or buying a land/island or an atoll, in the Republic of Maldives.???Ć?

title: Saudi Arabia Steps Up Assistance to Care for Ill and Injured in Yemen
content: The organization recently transferred a group of injured Yemenis from Taiz for treatment in Sudan, paying for the full cost of their travel and treatment. KSRelief has executed 51 projects in health, nutrition, water and environmental sanitation in Yemen totaling more than $208 million and benefiting 24.5 million Yemeni citizens, in addition to programs providing medical care and treatment for people inside Yemeni territory. KSRelief has entered into a number of agreements with private hospitals in Aden and Taiz aimed at providing full medical care for more than 750 wounded in these provinces. KSRelief seeks to activate the role of government hospitals to treat the injured inside Yemeni territories by providing medical services and treatment devices. Saudi Arabia, working through KSRelief, is the largest donor of humanitarian assistance to the Yemeni people. KSRelief was established in May 2015 under the sponsorship of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz. (WASHINGTON, DC) ???Ć?The King Salman Humanitarian Aid & Relief Center (KSRelief) recently sent 58 trucks carrying medical supplies and equipment to a number of provinces in Yemen. Of these, 11 trucks delivered supplies to Republic Hospital in Aden and six trucks to Mareb Hospital.

title: Kingdom Condemns Terror Attack on Military Base in Mali
content: The Foreign Ministry today condemnation yesterday???Ć?Ös terrorist attack on a military base in Mali that killed a number of soldiers.?é?? In a statement, the foreign ministry reiterated the Kingdom???Ć?Ös support for Mali and offered its condolences to the families of the victims and Malian government and people.

title: Vice Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Chairs Cabinet Session
content: Turing to domestic issues, the Cabinet approved a number of memoranda of understanding and cooperation, including a draft MOU with the Chinese National Space Administration on cooperation regarding Chang E-4 to explore the moon. Following a review of King Salman???Ć?Ös ongoing tour of Asia, the Council reviewed recent regional and international developments. The Vice Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Prince Mohammad bin Naif chaired the Cabinet???Ć?Ös weekly session at Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh this afternoon.?é?? At the outset of the session, Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif reviewed the success the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz???Ć?Ös visit to Malaysia, Indonesia and Brunei Darussalam.

title: King Salman Arrives in Brunei Darussalam
content: The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz arrived here today on an official visit to Brunei Darussalam.?é?? King Salman was received at Brunei International Airport by Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah.

title: King Salman Concludes Visit to Indonesia
content: ?é?? The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz left Indonesia today after a four-day visit during which he met with?é??President Joko Widodo and senior Indonesian officials, business and religious leaders.

title: King Salman Holds Talks With Sultan of Brunei Darussalam
content: The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz and Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah of Brunei Darussalam held talks today at Istana Nurul Iman Palace in Brunei.?é?? During the session, they reviewed bilateral relations and aspects of cooperation between the two brotherly countries in all fields.

title: King Salman Holds Talks with Indonesian President
content: King Salman arrived in Jakarta earlier in the day after concluding a four-day visit to Malaysia. Developing a stronger relationship build on a firm bases would positively contribute to tackling the regional and international crises, and enhance cooperation among Islamic countries based on good neighborhood, non-interference in the domestic affairs of other countries and solving of disputes through peaceful means according to the Organization of Islamic Cooperation Charter, UN Charter and principles of international law. During the meeting, King Salman cited the historic close cooperation between the Kingdom and Indonesia and said that the challenges facing the world today necessitate to deepen dialogue, and intensify efforts to confront those challenges.?é?? The King appreciated the position of Indonesia in supporting Islamic solidarity as well as the fair cause of Palestinian people. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz and Indonesian President Joko Widodo held talks at Astana Presidential Palace in Jakarta today.

title: King Salman Holds Talks With Malaysian Prime Minister
content: King Salman arrived in Kuala Lumpur on yesterday as part of a multi-nation tour. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz held in Kuala Lumpur today talks with Malaysian Prime Minister Mohammad Najib Abdul Razak.?é?? During the talks, they reviewed bilateral relations and ways of enhancing them in various fields to serve the interests of the two countries and their brotherly peoples.

title: Crown Prince Chairs Cabinet Session
content: The Cabinet also approved a scientific cooperation agreement with Belarus; authorized King Saud University to discuss a MoU for academic cooperation with Shanghai University in China.?é?? The Cabinet also authorized the Minister of Culture and Information, or his designate, to discuss with Indonesia and Malaysia a program to exchange news between the Saudi Press Agency and Indonesian and Malaysian news agencies. The Cabinet finally adjourned after discussing a number of topics including cooperation agreements between the Kingdom and each of Kyrgyzstan and Russia in combating crime and protection of classified information; approved the memorandum of understanding with Afghanistan on cooperation in judiciary and legal affairs and the memorandum of understanding with Japan in the field of social development. The Cabinet reviewed the latest developments at the regional and international arenas including the resumption of Syrian negotiations in Geneva under the auspices of the United Nations. The Cabinet expressed hope that the negotiations would bring about a comprehensive ceasefire and a political solution to the Syrian crisis based on Geneva Declaration I and the UN Security Council Resolution 2254. The Cabinet was briefed on the outcomes of the visit of Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir to Iraq, where he held talks with a number of Iraqi officials, reiterating the affirmation of the ties binding the two countries. Crown Prince Mohammad briefed the Cabinet on the outcomes of the talks that the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman held in Riyadh during the week with President Mohammed Abdullah Farmajo of Somalia and President Prokopios Pavlopoulos of Greece. Vice Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Prince Mohammed bin Naif Saud chaired the Cabinet session Monday afternoon at Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh.

title: King Salman Arrives in Malaysia
content: King Salman was accorded a state welcome at Parliament Square by Sultan Mohammed, King of Malaysia. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz arrived in Kuala Lumpur today on a four-day state visit to Malaysia.?é?? King Salman was received at Kuala Lumpur International Airport by Malaysian Prime Minister Mohamed Najib Abdul Razak, Defense Minister Hishamuddin Hussein, a number of Malaysian officials and Saudi Ambassador to Malaysia Fahd bin Abdullah Al-Rashid.

title: King Salman Leaves Riyadh on Multi-Nation Tour
content: Prior to his departure, King Salman issued a royal decree deputizing Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif to steer the state's affairs. In line with the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz's willingness to promote and consolidate the Kingdom???Ć?Ös relations in all fields with friendly countries and in response to invitations received by the King; the King Salman left Riyadh today on a tour that includes Malaysia, Indonesia, Brunei Daralsalam, Japan, China, the Maldives and Jordan.

title: Foreign Minister in Iraq for Talks Prime Minister Al-Abadi
content: The foreign minister congratulated Iraq on its achievements in countering terrorism, stressing the desire of both countries to eliminate the scourge of terrorism. Minister Al-Jubeir later met with Iraqi Foreign Minister Ibrahim Al-Jaafari. Following the meeting, Minister Al-Jubeir expressed his pleasure to visit Iraq for the first time, stressing that the Kingdom stands equidistant from all parties in Iraq and described his meeting with Prime Minister Al-Abadi as positive and fruitful. Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir met today with Iraqi Prime Minister Haider Al-Abadi in Baghdad during an official visit to Iraq.?é?? During the meeting, they discussed issues of common interest in addition to means of enhancing bilateral relations.

title: Saudi Minister of Hajj and Umrah Meets Iranian Hajj Officials
content: Minister of Hajj and Umrah Dr. Mohammad Saleh Bin Taher Benten met in Riyadh today Chairman of Iranian Hajj and Visit Organization Dr. Hameid Mohammadi to discuss Iranian pilgrims affairs arrangements for the this year???Ć?Ös Hajj season.

title: Deputy Crown Prince Holds Talks With Somali President
content: The meeting was attended by the Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir and other senior officials as well as the accompanying Somali delegation. The meeting began by congratulating the newly elected Somali president on assuming the presidency. They also discussed bilateral relations binding the two countries in all fields, means to extend promote them, in addition to latest regional developments. Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense met in Riyadh today, with Somali President Mohammed Abdullah Farmago.

title: Foreign Minister Meets Somali Counterpart
content: Dr. Omer is in the Kingdom part of Somali President Mohammed Abdullah Farmago???Ć?Ös delegation. Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir met in Riyadh today with Somali Minister of Foreign Affairs and Investment Promotion, Dr. Abdusalam Omer.?é?? During the meeting, they discussed bilateral relations and means to promote them, in addition to overall regional developments.

title: Foreign Minister Holds Meeting With Visiting Somali President
content: During the meeting, they discussed bilateral relations as well as the regional developments. Somali President Mohammed Abdullah Farmago, who is currently on a visit to the Kingdom, received in Riyadh today the Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir.

title: Saudi Foreign Minister: Iran Is the Biggest State Sponsor of Terrorism
content: ???Ć?£When the Iranians send weapons in violation of UN Security Council resolutions to the Houthis in Yemen, that???Ć?Ös an action,???Ć?he said.?é?? ???Ć?£When the Iranians send Shiite militias to fight in support of Bashar al-Assad in Syria, that???Ć?Ös action. When the Iranians plant terror cells in Bahrain and in Saudi Arabia and in other places, that???Ć?Ös action. The action is more important than the words.???Ć?Minister Al-Jubeir said Saudi Arabia is hopeful that Iran will change, but that Iran ultimately will be judged by its actions, not its words. ???Ć?£We look at the region and we see terrorism and we see a state sponsor of terrorism that is determined to upend the order in the Middle East,???Ć?he said. ???Ć?£The Iranians are the only country in the region that has not been attacked by either Daesh or Al-Qaeda. And this begs the question why? If Daesh and Al-Qaeda are extremist Sunni organizations, you would think they would be attacking Iran, a Shiite state. But they have not. Could it be that there???Ć?Ös a deal between them that prevents them or causes them not to attack the Iranians? This is a question that we keep asking ourselves.???Ć?The foreign minister noted that Iran was the only country in the region that has not been attacked by either Al-Qaeda or Daesh. Minister Al-Jubeir said that Iran is a destabilizing force in the region and ???Ć?£remains the biggest state sponsor of terrorism in the world.???Ć?He said, ???Ć?£The Iranians do not believe in the principle of good neighborliness or non-interference in the affairs of others. This is manifested in their interference in Lebanon, Syria, Iraq, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, Bahrain, Yemen, Pakistan, Afghanistan.???Ć????Ć?£One of the biggest factors I think that will help to resolve many of these challenges is the new American administration,???Ć?said Minister Al-Jubeir. ???Ć?£We expect to see America engaged in the world. And we expect to see a realistic American foreign policy, and we look forward to working with this administration very, very closely.???Ć?He also outlined the Kingdom???Ć?Ös view of the Arab-Israeli conflict and the crises in Syria and Yemen and said Saudi Arabia was optimistic about working with the U.S. administration to resolve many of the crises in the region today. (WASHINGTON, DC) ???Ć?Saudi Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir said that Iran was the ???Ć?£biggest state sponsor of terrorism in the world.???Ć?He made the remarks today in an address before the Munich Security Conference. Minister Al-Jubeir cited several covert, political and military actions carried out by Iran to buttress his argument.

title: Foreign Minister Holds Meetings With British, Singaporean Counterparts
content: On the sidelines of the foreign ministers meeting of the 20-Group summit held in Bonn, Germany, Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir held two separate bilateral meetings with the British Foreign Minister Boris Johnson and Singaporean Foreign Minister Dr. Vivian Balakrishnan,?é?? During the two meetings, they discussed issues listed on the agenda of the meeting, as well as regional and international issues of common interests.

title: Coalition to Investigate Houthi Claims Civilians Killed in Air Raid
content: The Coalition Supporting Legitimacy in Yemen announced today that it will investigate Houthi claims that civilians were killed during an air raid on a target near the Yemeni capital Sana???Ć?Öa.?é?? In a statement issued in Riyadh, the Coalition said ???Ć?£We are aware of media reports that Houthi militias are claiming that Yemeni civilians were killed in an air raid overnight near Sana'a. There has been fighting between Yemeni armed forces and rebels in this area in recent days. We are investigating the reports and will release further information when we have it.???Ć?

title: Foreign Minister Meets with US Secretary of State
content: Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmad Al-Jubeir met in Bonn, Germany, today with U.S. Secretary of State Rex Tillerson on the sidelines of the meeting of G20 foreign ministers.?é?? During the meeting, they reviewed bilateral relations between the two friendly countries. They also discussed the latest developments at regional and international arenas, including the situations in Syrian and Yemen.

title: Deputy Islamic Affairs Minister Receives Visiting USCIRF Delegation
content: At the end of the meeting, the members of USCIRF delegation praised the efforts being exerted by the Ministry of Islamic Affairs in the fight against extremism and terrorism, wishing to strengthen bilateral relations between the Commission and the Ministry to serve the principle of freedom. During the meeting, Dr. Al-Sudairy explained the overall rights and freedoms being guaranteed by Islam, the tolerance of Islam and its great values in all aspects of life, including the guarantee of freedom and peaceful coexistence among the followers of other religions. Deputy Minister of Islamic Affairs, Call and Guidance Dr. Tawfiq Abdulaziz Al-Sudairy received in Riyadh today a delegation from United States Commission on International Religious Freedom (USCIRF).

title: Prince Mutib Receives ARCENT Commander
content: Prince Mutib bin Abdullah, Minister of National Guard, received today in Riyadh Lt. Gen. Michael Garrett, U.S. Army Central Commanding General (ARCENT). During the meeting, they discussed issues of common interest.

title: Deputy Crown Prince Meets with Turkish President
content: During the meeting, they reviewed bilateral relations between the two countries and ways of enhancing them. They also discussed the latest developments in the region and the exerted efforts towards them in order to achieve security and stability in the region, including combating terrorism. Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense, met in Riyadh today with President Recep Tayyip Erdogan of Republic of Turkey.

title: King Salman, Crown Prince Mohammad Hold Talks with Turkish President
content: The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz and Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior held separate talks today in Riyadh with visiting Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan.?é?? During their talks, they reviewed bilateral relations and aspects of cooperation between the two brotherly countries. They also discussed the latest developments at the regional and international arenas.

title: Minister Al-Jubeir Meets with His Turkish Counterpart
content: Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmad Al-Jubeir met in Riyadh today with Turkish Foreign Minister Mevlut Cavusoglu.?é?? During the meeting, they reviewed bilateral relations between the two countries and works of the first session of the Saudi-Turkish Coordination Council which was held in Ankara Turkey. They also discussed the latest regional and international developments.

title: President Erdogan Arrives in Riyadh
content: President of Turkey Rajab Tayyeb Erdogan arrived in Riyadh tonight, on an official visit to the Kingdom.?é?? He was was received at King Khalid International Airport by the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosque King Salman bin Abdulaziz.

title: Foreign Minister Meets with UN Secretary General
content: Minister of Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir met with UN Secretary General Antonio Guterres at the United Nations in New York.?é?? During the meeting, Minister Al-Jubeir stressed the Kingdom's keenness on working with the UN to meet the challenges facing the region and the world, including the Middle East peace process and the crisis in Yemen and Syria.

title: Saudi Navy Frigate Attacked by Houthi Suicide Boats
content: ?é?? The command declares that the continuation of the Houthi militias' use of the port of Hodeida as a launching pad for terrorist operations is a serious development that would affect international navigation and the flow of humanitarian and medical assistance into the port for Yemeni population. The coalition said that the Saudi frigate dealt with the boats as necessary. However, one of the suicide boats managed to collide and detonate explosives against the rear of the frigate, killing two sailors and wounding three others.?é?? The explosion also resulted in a fire which the crew extinguished.?é?? The command added that the frigate continued its patrol in the area of operations while coalition air forces and the naval forces continued to chase the fleeing boats. The Command of the Coalition to Support Legitimacy in Yemen announced today that two Saudi navy sailors were killed in Houthi suicide boat attack on a Saudi frigate operating in the Red Sea.?é?? The command said that a Royal Saudi Naval frigate came under a terrorist attack by three Houthi suicide boats while on patrol west of the port of Hodeida.

title: Kingdom Condemns Terrorist Attack on Quebec Mosque
content: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs strongly condemned yesterday???Ć?Ös attack at the Quebec Islamic Cultural Center in Quebec City whick killed six innocent people during evening prayers.?é?? The Foreign Ministry reiterated the Kingdom's position calling for the need for respect of religions and beliefs, extended its condolences to the relatives of the victims, the people and government of Canada.

title: King Salman, President Trump Speak by Phone
content: King Salman extended an invitation to President Trump to visit the Kingdom and the US President also invited the King to visit the US. The two leaders agreed to schedule the visits in the coming period so as to promote cooperation and joint action and activate the strategic partnership between the two countries further. The views of the two leaders were identical on the files that were discussed during the call, including the fight against terrorism, extremism, their finance, formulating the appropriate mechanisms for that, and confronting those who seek to undermine security and stability in the region and interfere in the internal affairs of other states. During the call, they discussed the latest regional and world developments, and the historic relations between their two friendly countries and the strategic partnership as well as the importance of improving the economic, security and military cooperation between them. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz discussed with US President Donald Trump several topics in a telephone call on Sunday.

title: King Receives Serbian and Bosnian Deputy Premiers
content: ?é?? During the reception, the existing relations between the Kingdom and the Republic of Serbia, and the Republic of Bosnia and Herzegovina as well as the prospects of enhancing them, particularly in trade and economic fields were reviewed. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz received at Yamamah Palace in Riyadh today, Rasim Ljajic, Serbian Deputy Premier, Minister of Trade and Tourism and Communications and Chairman of the Serbian economic delegation and Mirko Markovic, Deputy Premier of Bosnia and Herzegovina, Minister of Foreign Trade and Economic Relations and Chairman of the Bosnian economic delegation, who are currently on a visit to the Kingdom.

title: Foreign Minister Underscored the Strength of Saudi-US Relations, Addressing Continued and Expanded Areas of Cooperation
content: ???Ć?ØOur hope is that wisdom will prevail and the Congress will do the right thing and make the adjustments,???Ć?the foreign minister added. ???Ć?£The country that has the most to lose from dilution of sovereign immunities is the United States itself. And American officials know this. Because America has the largest footprint in the world, they operate all over the world. They???Ć?Öre fighting wars all over the world. They provide weapons to countries all over the world. If that principle is eroded, then the US could be sued in virtually every country in the world.???Ć?Minister Al-Jubeir said that the Justice Against Sponsors of Terrorism Act (JASTA) legislation passed by the U.S. Congress last year has undercut the foundation of sovereign immunity, turning ???Ć?£the international order into the law of the jungle. On the Israeli-Palestinian peace talks, Minister Al-Jubeir stressed that the Arab Peace Initiative remains on the table. ???Ć?£Our hope is that there will be enough courage and enough political will on both sides to be able to move to settling this longest conflict in our region,???Ć?he said. Minister Al-Jubeir said that Iran must be held accountable for its support of terrorism, violation of ballistic missile arrangements and for its interference is the affairs of the countries in the region. ???Ć?£Iran has been single-handedly the most important supporter of terrorism in the region. Iran has supported, created and supported Hezbollah. Iran has harbored terrorists,???Ć?said the foreign minister. Speaking at the World Economic Forum in Davos, Foreign Minister Al-Jubeir said "the U.S. and Saudi Arabia have had a historic strategic partnership, if not alliance, since World War II; that relationship continues. We expect it to broaden and deepen and grow stronger. Our interests are aligned when it comes to the issues of the region.?é?? Minister Al-Jubeir added that the Kigdom and the U.S. "have a very, very strong people-to-people relationships with the U.S., and I expect that this will continue.???Ć?

title: King Salman holds talks with Lebanese President in Riyadh
content: On the Lebanese side, the talks were attended by Minister of Foreign Affairs and Expatriates Jibran Bassil; Minister of Education and Higher Education Marwan Hamadeh; Minister of Finance Ali Hassan Khalil; Minister of National Defense Yaqoub Al-Sarraf; Minister of Interior and Municipalities Nihad Al-Mashnouq; Minister of State for Presidential Affairs Pierre Raffoul; Minister of Information Melhem Riashy; Minister of Economy and Trade Raed Khoury; and Lebanese Ambassador to the Kingdom Abdul-Sattar Issa. The meetings was attended by Prince Faisal bin Bandar bin Abdulaziz, Governor of Riyadh Province; Minister of State and Royal Advisor Prince Dr. Mansour bin Miteb bin Abdulaziz; Prince Miteb bin Abdullah bin Abdulaziz, Minister of National Guard; Minister of State Dr. Musaed bin Mohammed Al-Aiban; Minister of State Dr. Ibrahim bin Abdulaziz Al-Assaf; Minister of Culture and Information Dr. Adel bin Zaid Altoraifi; Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir; Minister of Finance Mohammed bin Abdullah Al-Jadaan; Minister of State for Arab Gulf Affairs Thamer bin Sabhan Al-Sabhan and Saudi Charge' D'Affaires to Lebanon Walid bin Abdullah Bukhari. During the meeting, they reviewed bilateral relations between the two countries, ways of enhancing them in various fields, and latest developments at Arab and international arenas. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz held at Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh today official talks with President Michel Aoun of Lebanon.

title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet's Session
content: The Cabinet authorized the General Commission for Statistics to conduct the population general census for 2020. The Cabinet approved the incentives and privileges offered to donors who purchase antiquities or masterpieces or special collections displayed in national museums; approved the regulatory provisions of the General Authority for Sports; The Cabinet approved the security cooperation agreement with Djibouti signed in Riyadh on 10/07/1437 AH; approved the memorandum of understanding (MoU) between the Ministry of Interior???Ć?Ös Saudi Financial Investigations Administration (SAFA) and the Financial Investigation Unit (FIU) of India as regards cooperation on the exchange of the investigations related to money laundering, terror financing and related crimes, signed in Riyadh on 25/06/1437 AH; approved MoU for cooperation in the field of renewable energy between King Abdullah City for Atomic and Renewable Energy and China???Ć?Ös National Power Management, signed in Riyadh on 09/04/1437H. The Cabinet finally adjourned after discussing a number of domestic issues. The Council strong condemnation of the murder of Russia's Ambassador to Turkey, Andrey Karlov and the terror attack in Berlin, Germany, that resulted in the death of 12 people.?é?? The Cabinet also the double terrorist bombing of a market in Baghdad and the attack in Istanbul.?é?? The Cabinet offered its condolences to the families of the victims. The Cabinet also reiterated the Kingdom's appreciation to U.S. Secretary of State John Kerry???Ć?Ös statement on the final solution of the Palestinian-Israeli conflict, as well as the adoption by Security Council Resolution 2334, which condemns Israeli settlement and calls for stopping it in the occupied Palestinian territories. The Cabinet also discussed the outcome of the 97th OPEC meeting in Cairo and commitment by OPEC members to honor the November 2016 agreement on reducing oil production. The Cabinet welcomed Security Council Resolution 2328 on the deployment of international observers in Aleppo to supervise the evacuation of civilians. The Cabinet was briefed on a number of reports including the emergency meeting of the Executive Committee of Foreign Ministers of Organization of Islamic Cooperation that the massacres being committed in Aleppo, Syrian, can be classified as a war crime and crimes against humanity. King Salman briefed the Cabinet on the results of his two phone calls with King Abdullah II bin Al-Hussein of Jordan and President Abed Rabbo Hadi of Yemen, as well as his two meetings with Palestinian President Mahmud Abbas and Chairman of the Board of Directors of Al-Quds Fund Muneeb Al-Masri, in addition to the messages received from King of Jordan; Emir of Kuwait and President of Comoros. The Cabinet appreciated King Salman???Ć?Ös order to organize a popular campaign for the Syrian people relief of the Syrian people.?é?? King Salman???Ć?Ös directive to allocate SR 100 million ($26.67 million) for the campaign and his personal SR 20 million ($5.33 million) donation.?é?? The Cabinet also express its appreciation to the SR 10 million ($2.67 million) donation by Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif and the SR 10 million ($2.67 million) Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman???Ć?Ös SR 8 million ($2.13 million) to alleviate the sufferings of Syrian people, especially the displaced from Aleppo. The Cabinet began by congratulating King Salman on the second anniversary of his ascension to the throne.?é?? The Cabinet then commended the State???Ć?Ös general budget for the fiscal year 2017; for its sufficient strength to cope with economic and financial challenges. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz chaired the Cabinet's session at Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh on Monday afternoon.

title: Kingdom Condemns Terrorist Attack On Prison in Bahrain
content: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs strongly condemned the yesterday???Ć?Ös terrorist attack on a prison in Bahrain which resulted in the death of a security officer. The Foreign Ministry reiterated the Kingdom's solidarity with Bahrain and its firm stance against terrorism and extended deep condolences to the relatives of the martyred security man, the government and people of Bahrain.

title: King Salman Condoles Turkish President on Victims of Terrorist Attack in Istanbul
content: The King said, "We have received with deep sorrow the news of the armed attack in Istanbul which resulted in deaths and injuries.?é?? We strongly condemn this cowardly terrorist act and stress the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia's standing with the Republic of Turkey and its people against anyone who tries to undermine its security and stability. We, in my name and on behalf of the Government and the people of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, offer our condolences to Your Excellency, the brotherly people of the Republic of Turkey and the families of the victims, wishing the injured a speedy recovery." The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz sent a cable of condolences to Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan following the terrorist attack in Istanbul which resulted in deaths and injuries.

title: King Salman Receives Phone Call From Yemeni President
content: The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz received on Sunday a telephone call from Yemeni President Abed Rabbo Hadi.?é?? During the call, President Hadi congratulated the King Salman the second anniversary of his accession to the throne and praised the King Salman in support of the Yemeni people in restoring legitimacy to Yemen.

title: Kingdom Condemns Terror Attack in Istanbul
content: In a statement, the Foreign Ministry reiterated the Kingdom???Ć?Ös support and solidarity with Turkey against terrorism and expressed condolences to the families of the victims as well as the Turkish government and people, wishing a speedy recovery for the injured. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs today condemned the terrorist attack in Istanbul, Turkey, in which dozens of people were killed and injured.

title: Kingdom Welcomes US Secretary of State Kerry's Proposals on Final Settlement of Palestinian-Israel Conflict
content: Saudi Arabia today welcomed the proposals put forth by U.S. Secretary of State John Kerry on a final settlement of the Palestinian-Israeli conflict. In a statement, the Saudi Foreign Ministry said the Kingdom views the proposals as being in accord with the majority of the resolutions of international legality and most of the elements of the Arab Peace Initiative adopted at the Arab Summit in Beirut in 2002 and by the Islamic Summit in Makkah in 2005, and that the proposals represent an appropriate basis for achieving a final settlement of the Palestinian-Israeli conflict.

title: Deputy Crown Prince Telephones US Secretary of State
content: Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, the Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense, telephoned US Secretary of State John Kerry on Monday.?é???é?? During the call, they discussed the latest developments in the region and a number of issues of common interest.

title: Foreign Minister Receives Ambassadors of the U.S., Finland, Burkina Faso, and Bosnia and Herzegovina.
content: Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmad Al-Jubeir received in Riyadh Ambassador of the United States Dr. Joseph Westphal as he approaches the end of his tour in the Kingdom.?é?? Minister Al-Jubeir also received separately Ambassadors Pekka Votileinin of Finland Pekka Votileinin; Ambassador Mansa Oontana of Burkina Faso, and Ambassador Snahd Bristeric of Bosnia and Herzegovina.?é?? During the meetings, bilateral relations and issues of common interest were discussed.

title: Foreign Ministry Welcomes UN Security Council Resolution Condemning Israeli Settlements
content: The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia hoped that the UN resolution will contribute to reviving the peace process in the region on the basis of relevant UN resolutions and Arab Peace Initiative, which will result in the establishment of an independent Palestinian State with East Jerusalem as its capital. In a statement, the Foreign Ministry said that the UN resolution confirms beyond any doubt that Israeli settlements on Palestinian territory, including East Jerusalem, have "no legal validity". The Ministry of Foreign Affairs today commended United Nations Security Council Resolution 2334 condemning the construction of Israeli settlements and demanding a halt of Israeli settlements on occupied Palestinian territory.

title: Kingdom Condemns Terror Attacks in Ankara, Berlin
content: Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of the Interior, and Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Defense Minister, sent simular cables condolences and condemnations of terrorist attacks. King Salman reiterated Saudi Arabia???Ć?Ös firm position in rejecting terrorism in all its forms and manifestations, and emphasized the need for stepping up global efforts to stamp it out. In separate cables sent to Russian President Vladimir Putin and German President Joachim Gauck, King Salman offered condolences to the families of the victims as well as to the peoples and governments of Russia and Germany. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz strongly condemned the terror attacks in Ankara that claimed the lives of Russia???Ć?Ös ambassador to Turkey Andrey G. Karlov and Berlin that resulted in the death of 12 people in Germany.

title: Kingdom Condemns Killing of Russian Ambassador to Turkey
content: The Foreign Ministry offered its condolences to the family of Ambassador Karlov and the Russian government and people. In a statement issued this evening, the Foreign Ministry said that the murder is contrary to the international laws, the principles of protection of diplomats and international envoys and humanitarian principles and ethics. The Foreign Ministry today expressed the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia's strong condemnation of the murder of Russia's Ambassador to Turkey, Andrey Karlov.

title: Foreign Minister Al-Jubeir Call For Greater Pressure on Syrian Regime
content: The Arab foreign ministers said in their emergency meeting on Syria that the continuation of the tragic situation in Syria poses a threat to Arab national security and global stability, stressing the need to impose the necessary pressure on the Syrian regime to respond to the will of the international community represented in the UN resolutions. Addressed the extraordinary session of the Foreign Ministers of the Arab League in Cairo, Minister Al-Jubeir called on putting pressure on the Syrian regime to reach a political solution. Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir said today?é?? that the ???Ć?£Syrian regime is the one who refused to enter into any serious negotiations, continued the killing of his own people and brought terrorist organizations and sectarian militias into Syria, including the Iranian Revolutionary Guard."

title: Saudi Arabia Pays Share to Palestinian Authority's Budget for Eight Months
content: Saudi Development Fund has deposited $61.6 million to the Palestinian Ministry of Finance's account. Saudi Ambassador to Egypt, Ahmed Kattan explained that the amount represents the value of the Kingdom's monthly contributions to support the Palestinian National Authority's budget.?é?? The payment is for?é??April through?é??November?é??2016.

title: Ambassador to Egypt Conveys Kingdom's Condolences to Pope Tawadros II on Terrorist Attack
content: Ambassador Kattan stressed the Kingdom's denunciation and condemnation of this cowardly terrorist act, confirming the Kingdom's solidarity with Egypt and the Egyptian people against anyone who tries to undermine its security and stability. Ahmad bin Abdulaziz Kattan, Saudi Ambassador to Egypt and Permanent Representative of the Kingdom to the Arab League has visited Pope Tawadros II, Pope of Alexandria and Patriarch, at St. Mark Cathedral to conveyed condolences of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz; Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior, and Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense, to Pope Tawadros II on the victims of the terrorist bombing targeting St. Peter Church in Cairo.

title: Saudi King Says Nation Is Committed to Regional Security and Economic Development
content: ???Ć?£I am optimistic about a better tomorrow,???Ć?King Salman said. ???Ć?£These conditions that we are now experiencing are not more difficult than their previous ones. We will overpass them to a better and brilliant future.???Ć?King Salman noted that economic reforms implemented to date have focused on diversifying the nation???Ć?Ös economy and re-distributing resources to expand job opportunities. Saudi Arabia has ???Ć?£come out of [the past three years] with a strong economy and continuous growth; we have set out our reforms today to look forward to the future,???Ć?he noted. The strength of these structural reforms fall under Vision 2030, Saudi Arabia???Ć?Ös economic transformation plan. The region???Ć?Ös stability directly impacts the Kingdom???Ć?Ös economic development, and ensuring both remains fundamental, King Salman stressed. ???Ć?£Our domestic policy is based on pillars of maintaining security, stability and prosperity to achieve the development that meets the needs of the present and preserves the right of future generations.???Ć????Ć?£We in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia believe that the security of our dear neighbor Yemen is the security of the Kingdom, and will not accept any interference in its internal affairs,???Ć?he stated. King Salman pledged to uphold Yemen???Ć?Ös right to security. Yemen, he said, cannot be the ???Ć?£corridor???Ć?for countries trying to undermine the security of the Kingdom, nor can Saudi Arabia allow other nations to undermine its sovereignty and security. Among the core foreign policy priorities, King Salman vowed to continue cooperating with the international community in combatting the extremism that threatens its borders and the region at large. Political solutions to international crises can safeguard values of tolerance and coexistence, paving the way for nations to ensure peace and achieve development, King Salman said. In his annual address to the Consultative Council, King Salman presented an optimistic outlook for the nation???Ć?Ös foreign and domestic policies. He said that Saudi Arabia has played an active and constructive role in the region and in important global undertakings with the international community is proceeding with ???Ć?£firm steps???Ć?to maintain its active role in the region and around the world. (WASHINGTON, DC) ???Ć?The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz reinforced today that Saudi Arabia remains fully committed to acting vigorously to ensure its security and the security of the region while moving forward on the Kingdom???Ć?Ös economic development and reforms.

title: Foreign Minister and British Foreign Secretary Hold Press Conference
content: Foreign Minister Al-Jubeir explained that he discussed with Minister Johnson ways of enhancing the current cooperation between the two countries in fighting terrorism and combating extremism, confirming that the talks, in this context, covered the Iranian interference in the affairs of the region, a move rejected by all countries of the region.?é?? Minister Al-Jubeir lauded the good relations between the two countries, also reviewed during the meeting, describing them as historical, strategic and deep-rooted, and confirming that the gains enjoyed by the two countries as a result of these distinguished and firm ties are appreciated by the two sides as both of them are seeking to further develop and enhance them in all fields. On the Libyan affairs, Saudi Arabia and Britain agree on the importance of the implementation of the Sukhairat declaration which officially ended the political and military infighting in Libya as well as on the exertion of more effort to narrow the gaps among the different Libyan factions and to form a government that would include all rivals in Libya and observe the country's security and stability, Minister Al-Jubeir said. On the situations in Yemen, Minister Al-Jubeir drew the attention to the arrangement reached between Saudi Arabia and Britain on the importance of extending support for the UN Special Envoy for Yemen to enable him orchestrate a peaceful solution in Yemen, gearing forward to achieving progress in this field. He said today's meeting dealt with the situations in Syria, and discussed the best way to reach a peaceful solution there, to be based on the UN Security Council Resolution 2254, while the view points of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and the United Kingdom were identical as regards Iraq where the two sides suggest that implementation of reforms reached in 2014 offering each Iraqi sect its rights, is suffice to guarantee Iraq's unity and integrity of territories. Casting light on the issues discussed during the meeting, Al-Jubeir said "we discussed regional situations of concern to the peace process, the importance of reaching a comprehensive and fair solution to the Palestinian-Israeli struggle through the Arab Peace Initiative. We spoke about Lebanon, expressing hope that the Lebanese would form a government that guarantees political stability in the country". Minister Al-Jubeir said that remarks attributed to Foreign Secretary Johnson during a conference in Italy were incorrectly interpreted as defaming Saudi Arabia and underestimates the deep-rooted and strong ties between the two countries. Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir affirmed the depth of the Saudi-British relations and its historic dimension which still enjoys growth and development in all fields and at all levels during a?é??joint press conference with British Foreign Secretary Boris Johnson in Riyadh in which the two ministers briefed the press on their meeting which focused on regional and international issues of mutual concern and to best enhance relations between the two countries.

title: Kingdom Denounces Terror Attacks in Istanbul and Mogadishu
content: The ministry reiterated the solidarity of the Kingdom with sisterly states in confrontation of terrorism in all its forms and manifestations, extending condolence and sympathy to the families of the victims and the governments and people of the two republics of Turkey and Somalia, wishing the injured a quick recovery. The Foreign Ministry today strongly denounced the terrorist attacks which took place Istanbul, Turkish and Mogadishu, Somalia, which killed 38 people and wounded 155 others in Istanbul, and killed at least 16 people in Mogadishu.

title: Crown Prince Receives British Foreign Secretary
content: During the meeting, they discussed ways for strengthening cooperation between the two countries in various fields, especially security cooperation and fighting against extremism and terrorism, and reviewed the latest developments in the Middle East, efforts being exerted towards them and the positions of the two countries in this regard. Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior received at the Interior Ministry in Riyadh today British Foreign Secretary Boris Johnson.?é??

title: King Salman Receives British Foreign Secretary
content: The meeting was attended by Prince Mohammed bin Nawaf bin Abdulaziz, Saudi Ambassador to the United Kingdom; Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior; Minister of State and Cabinet's Member Dr. Musaed bin Mohammed Al-Aiban; Minister of Culture and Information Dr. Adel bin Zaid Altoraifi; Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir; and British Ambassador to the Kingdom Simon Collis. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz received at Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh today British Foreign Secretary Boris Johnson.?é?? During the meeting, they discussed bilateral relations between the two countries and a number of issues of common interest.

title: GCC Leaders Conclude 37th Summit in Bahrain
content: The Leaders of Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) concluded their 37th Summit of the GCC Supreme Council in Manama, Bahrian, this afternoon.?é?? The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz headed the Kingdom???Ć?Ös delegation to the summit.

title: GCC Leaders Hold Joint Meeting With British Prime Minister
content: Leaders of the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) held at today a joint meeting with the British Prime Minister Theresa May in Manama in the framework of the 37th Summit of the GCC Supreme Council.?é?? The Saudi Arabia???Ć?Ös delegation was headed by the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz.

title: Joint Incidents Assessment Team (JIAT) Response to Doctors Without Borders, Amnesty International
content: The JIST spokesman also refuted allegations by the Amnesty International that a cattle market in the village of Fayyoh, in Lahaj Province, was subject to shelling by Coalition Forces on July 6, 2015, resulted in the death of 40 people and injuring dozens.?é?? The spokesman confirmed that JIST has investigated the facts and circumstances of the incident, and found that the mentioned market was not bombed.?é?? He added that Coalition Forces bombed two legitimate military targets on July 6, a gathering of armed Houthi militia about 7 km northwest of the cattle market, and a armed Houthi military command building 10 km north of the market.?é?? The spokesman said JIST found that the cattle market was not bombed by Coalition Forces. Regarding the allegation of Doctors Without Borders (MSF) that Al-Fazel School in Saada Province was subject to shelling on Aug. 13, 2016, which resulted in death 10 children and injuring at least nine people, the spokesman said that JIST investigated the facts and circumstances of the incident, and based on the confirmed information were dispatched by MSF on the site of incident, and found clearly that the school was not targeted.?é?? Coalition Forces targeted weapons depots belonging to Houthi armed group, a distanced 10 km from the school.?é?? The Coalition Forces, therefore, rejects the MSF allegations. With regard of the allegation contained by the Swedish Embassy that a food factory owned by Mr. Abdullah Ahmad Al-Agel, Honorary Swedish Consul in Sana'a was subject to aerial shelling on Aug. 9, 2016, which resulted in death of 16 workers, JIAT spokesman said that the investigation in the circumstances of the incident, found that the Coalition Forces bombed two sites on Aug. 9, an antennae connections used for military purposes in the Ayban Mount west of Sana???Ć?Öa (7 km from the factory in question), and a cave in Eastern Altahedan Mount south of Sana???Ć?Öa (10 km from the factory in question) and used for military purposes.?é?? Al-Mansour indicated that the two mentioned sites were legitimate military targets in accordance to the rules of engagement and international humanitarian law and that JIAT did not find any evidence to support allegations that that Coalition Forces bombed the factory. The Coalition therefore the allegations by the Swedish Embassy. Accordingly, and based on the rules of the international humanitarian law, the legal protection supposed to be enjoyed by civilian sites is dropped off this site due to its being used as a tool within the military effort, taking into consideration that the classes of the school have gone at the time on an open vacation in view of the surrounding circumstances in addition to that investigations have not revealed the occurrence of any human losses or injuries, with the building sustaining partial damage estimated at no more than 25%.?é?? JIAT has, accordingly, concluded that the procedures followed by the Coalition Forces targeting the questionable site were intact and correct. Al-Mansour refuting allegations by Amnesty International that Asmaa School in the city of Al-Mansouriya in the Yemeni province of Al-Hodeida was subject to air shelling on Aug. 24, 2015. The spokesman said that it was revealed that the Coalition Forces have targeted the Asmaa School based on surveillance information that the armed Houthi militias and forces loyal to former president Abdullah Salah were using the site as a headquarters and a weapons storage and distribution center for weapons smuggled through Al-Hodeida port, thus making it a legitimate high value target that achieves certified military advantage. In a press conference held today at King Salman Airbase for Middle Sector, Al-Mansour said that ???Ć?£in light of what has been viewed from the facts of the incident, the team found that damages of the originally-untargeted building were due to targeting of the vehicle, a legitimate military target, which was next to the building, disclosing that the joint team confirmed that the coalition forces are to apologize for the unintentional error, provide appropriate assistance to those affected, and launch investigation with the persons in charge of the incident to look into whether they have violated the accredited rules of engagement and take appropriate action as regards the incident. The Coalition Forces targeted the location of that gathering. As a result, the air crew monitored a moving vehicle from the same targeted site heading southwards, pursued and shelled it immediately. The vehicle was next to the building which had no signs of a hospital before the bombing.?é?? It was later identified as Abs Hospital. Spokesman of Joint Incidents Assessment Team (JIAT), Mansour Ahmad Al-Mansour,?é?? stressed that with regard to an announcement issued by Doctors Without Borders on the alleged bombing carried out by the coalition forces on Abs Hospital in the city of Abs in Yemen???Ć?Ös Hjjah Province on Aug. 15, 2016, which resulted in death seven people and wounding 13 others, the JIAT verified facts and circumstances of the incident based on confirmed intelligence information about existence of a gathering of Houthi armed leaders in the north of the city of Abs.

title: King Salman Address 37th GCC Summit in Manama
content:
Walsalamu Alaykum Warahmatu Allahi Wabarakatu (May the peace, mercy, and blessings of Allah be with you) Finally, I wish my brother King Hamad bin Isa Al-Khalifa every success during his current chairmanship of this session. 
Despite the important achievements implemented by the GCC, we gear forward to better future that guarantees the aspirations of the GCC citizens towards more prosperity and dignified living, enhances the march of the GCC at the regional and international arenas through active foreign policies to achieve security and stability in the region and supports regional and international peace. Dear Brothers, As of the situation in Syria, we are all saddened to see what is happening there, including killings, displacement and suffering of the Syrian people, mounting pressure on the world community to intensify efforts to stop bloodshed and find a political solution guaranteeing the achievement of security and stability as well as preserving the unity and integrity of the Syrian territories.
