As for the situation in Yemen, efforts are being exerted to end the struggle there in what could achieve security and stability under the leadership of its legitimate government and according to the GCC initiative, outcomes of the national dialogue and UN Security Council Resolution 2216, thanks to the endeavors offered by the UN Special Envoy to Yemen. The sad situation being suffered by some Arab countries, characterized by terrorism, domestic struggle and bloodshed, is an imperative result of the alliance of terrorism, sectarianism and flagrant interference, leading to their destabilization and breach of security. 
The current highly complicated circumstances in our region are obvious to everyone as well as the crises it was facing, requiring all of us to work together to confront and deal with them in a spirit of responsibility and determination and intensify efforts in order to consolidate the foundations of security and stability of our region as well as development and prosperity for our countries and peoples. Dear brothers: I am pleased, at the outset of this summit, to express appreciation of King Hamad bin Isa Al- Khalifa, the government and people of the Kingdom of Bahrain for the warm hospitality and good reception. I wish all success for the King of Bahrain during his chairmanship of this session. I expressed satisfaction for what has been achieved by the competent agencies of the GCC council over the past year in accordance with mechanisms adopted by the council which aim to raise the level of cooperation and coordination, including the decision of establishing the Economic and Development Affairs Commission which began its work recently to promote joint action in the economic and development fields. 
Peace, Mercy and Blessing of Allah be Upon You, 
Leaders of the Gulf Cooperation Council Member States, King Hamad bin Isa Al-Khalifa, King of the Kingdom of Bahrain, 
Praise to Allah, prayer and peace be upon the Messenger of Allah, on his family and companions, "In the Name of Allah, the Most Gracious, Most Merciful, The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz today addressed the 37th Summit for the Supreme Council of the Gulf Cooperation Council hosted by King Hamad bin Isa Al-Khalifa of Bahrain.

title: King Salman Hold Meeting With British Prime Minister in Manama
content: The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz received at his guest residence in Manama today British Prime Minister Teresa May.?é?? During the meeting, they discussed relations and fields of cooperation between the two countries and ways of enhancing them in addition to the developments of situations in the region.

title: Deputy Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Chairs Cabinet's Session
content: The Cabinet has also reviewed a number of issues on its agenda, including the annual report of the general organization for social insurance on a previous fiscal year. The Cabinet issued relevant instructions towards them. The cabinet also approved the appointment of Jamal bin Ali Al-Kishi and Eng. Omar bin Nabil Al-Khudairi as two members of the private sector in the Board of Directors of the Saline Water Conversion Corporation for a period of three years. The Cabinet approved an amendment to Table (2) regarding the joint committees list attached to the Cabinet's decision No. 2 dated 03/01/1428H, so that the Saudi side at the Saudi-Indonesian Joint Committee is chaired by the Minister of Foreign Affairs, and that the Committee shall meet every six months. The Cabinet concluded by approving a memorandum of understanding the Peoples Republic of China on enhancing joint cooperation on the silk road economic belt and the initiative of naval silk road for the 21st century and cooperation on productive energy which was signed in Riyadh on 9/4/1437 H;?é?? authorized the Minister of Islamic Affairs, Call and Guidance, or his deputy, to negotiate a memorandum of understanding in the field of Islamic Affairs with Mauritania, sign it, and forward the final version for taking further procedures; approved a general cooperation agreement Mauritius which was signed in New York on 12/12/1436H; authorized the Minister of Foreign Affairs, or his deputy, to negotiate with Norway and New Zealand draft memoranda of understanding on political consultations, sign them, and submit the final versions for taking further authentication;?é?? authorized the Acting Secretary General of King Abdulaziz Foundation for Research and Archives, or his deputy, to discuss with the United Arab Emirate a draft memorandum of cooperation between King Abdulaziz Foundation for Research and Archives Saudi Arabia and the National Archive of the Ministry of Presidential Affairs of the United Arab Emirates, sign it, and submit the signed final version. Also praised was the Kingdom's participation in the international conference on the preservation of endangered cultural heritage, held in Abu-Dhabi in the attendance of Prince Khalid Al-Faisal, Advisor to the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosque and Governor of Makkah Region, who led the Kingdom's delegation on behalf of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosque. The Cabinet lauded the holding of a number of cultural activities, citing the opening by the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques of Trade Roads in the Arab peninsula exhibition, the masterpieces and antiquities of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia across history, held at King Abdulaziz international cultural center in Dhahran, and the king's okay of the exhibition's tour of east Asia following its successful nine tours of Europe and America. To mark the occasion of the International Day for Solidarity with the Palestinian people, the Cabinet reiterated the Kingdom's call for the world community to remain committed towards the rights of Palestinians and retard Israel from its procrastinating policies against them. The Cabinet praised the 7th Bosphorus Summit.?é?? The Cabinet hailed the cooperation agreements in the fields of investment signed by Saudi Arabia and Turkey during the Summit. The Cabinet also praised the cooperation and contribution of the main oil producing countries outside OPEC to the restoration of stability and balance of international petroleum market. The Cabinet was briefed on the outcome of OPEC ministerial meeting which was held last week in Vienna. The Cabinet praised the outcome of the meeting, the decisions that were taken as well as the clear cooperation among all OPEC countries to restore stability in the international oil market by reducing excess supply. The Cabinet commended the outcome of the 35th Meeting of GCC Interior Ministers, expressing hope that the outcome of the meeting will contribute to enhancing the efforts of GCC countries in the field of security. The Cabinet reviewed a number of reports on regional, Arab and international developments, and looked forward to the outcome of meetings the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques will hold with leaders of the United Arab Emirates, the State of Qatar, the Kingdom of Bahrain and the State of Kuwait during his tour that emanates from his keenness to communicate with Gulf Cooperation Council leaders to serve the interests of their peoples and to enhance ties of brotherhood among GCC countries. Crown Prince Mohammad welcomed Minister of Labour and Social Development Dr. Ali bin Nasser Al-Ghafis in his new portfolio, wishing him success. He also thanked the outgoing officials and members for their efforts. The Deputy Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques congratulated the Kingdom's Grand Mufti; members of the Senior Scholars Commission; full-time sheikhs, members of the Standing Committee for Fatwa (affiliated to the Senior Scholars Commission); and Speaker and members of Majlis Al-Shura on the royal trust bestowed on them through renewing their memberships to their agencies, wishing them all success. Deputy Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Prince Mohammad bin Naif bin Abdulaziz Al Saud chaired the Cabinet's session which was held at Al-Yamamah Palace today.

title: Saudi Arabia Responds to Inaccurate Story on Yemen
content: Media reports that import restrictions put in place in Yemen by the coalition to support the legitimate government have affected supplies in dialysis treatment centers in Yemen are not correct. There are no restrictions on the entry of humanitarian assistance, including medical supplies and equipment into Yemen. There is an inspection mechanism to ensure that no weapons are smuggled into Yemen but this mechanism does not impede the delivery of humanitarian assistance to Yemen. The coalition is the largest provider of humanitarian assistance to Yemen, including medical supplies and equipment. Saudi Arabia alone has provided more than $500 million in humanitarian aid, making it the largest single provider of humanitarian assistance to Yemen. title: Vice Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Chairs Cabinet's Session
content: The Cabinet approved the accession of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia to the membership of The Hague Conference on Private International Law; and approved the organization of the Council of Family Affairs. The Cabinet approved an agreement Tunisia on cooperation in the field of defense, which was signed in Riyadh on 11/3/1437 H.; approved a cooperation arrangement with Brazil in the field of sports, which was signed in Brasilia on 25/6/1436H.; approved the organizational arrangements of the Center for Achievement and Rapid Intervention; and approved a number of arrangements described in detail in Resolution No. 2770 dated 03/06/1436H regarding the rearrangement of services supervised by the Ministry of Finance. The Cabinet reiterated the Kingdom's strong denunciation and condemnation of the terror explosion that ripped the Afghani capital of Kabul, and the terror armed attack which took place in the German city of Munich, leaving tens of innocents killed or injured, expressing the condolence and sympathy of the Kingdom to each of the Republic of Afghanistan and Federal Republic of Germany on the terror victims, wishing the injured quick recovery and calling for concerted international efforts to fight this dangerous plague which is categorically rejected by all divine religions and international norms. The Cabinet welcomed the statement issued by the 25th session of the joint ministerial council between the Gulf Cooperation Council and the European Union in Brussels, underscoring the importance of continuing enhancing relations between them amid the regional challenges to develop into firm and effective bases for regional and international stability and security as well as the strategic importance of close coordination between the two sides. The Cabinet reviewed a number of reports on the developments of issues regionally and internationally, as well as the international efforts regarding a number of issues at the international arena and terror combating. The Cabinet welcomed the convening of the 2nd meeting of the International Coalition for Fighting Daesh held at Andrews Airbase, near the Washington, DC.?é?? Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense, led the Saudi delegation to the meeting. At the outset of the session, the Vice Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques briefed the Cabinet on the outcome of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques audience held for King Hamad bin Isa Al Khalifa, King of Bahrain. Vice Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Prince Mohammad bin Naif chaired the Cabinet's session held today at Al-Salam Palace in Jeddah.

title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet Session
content: The Cabinet was finally briefed on the topics on its agenda including the Memorandum of Understanding between the Kingdom???Ć?Ös Ministry of Defense and the Ministry of defense in the Finnish Republic for cooperation in the field of defense. The Cabinet also discussed a number of local issues. The Cabinet welcomed the statement issued by Foreign Ministers of International Alliance against ISIS terrorist organization at the conclusion of their meeting in Rome, which confirmed the determination to confront and defeat this organization and curb its ambitions. The Cabinet expressed the Kingdom's appreciation to the United Kingdom, countries and bodies that organized the Fourth Conference of Donors to Syria in London, stressing that the Kingdom's provision of an amount of one hundred million dollars additional to the amount pledged by the Kingdom in Kuwait Conference, in addition to $90 million from the previous obligations available for disbursement in the coming period embody the belief of the Kingdom's government and people in the need to provide assistance to Syria, where the total assistance provided by the Kingdom exceeded $780 million. The Cabinet expressed its thanks to the President of Sudan for his commending the significant role of the Kingdom in Africa, its benevolent initiatives in all African countries and its roles in strengthening Arab-African relations, and its efforts to clear the air between Sudan, Egypt and Ethiopia. The Cabinet appreciated the King's speech during receiving guests of Janadriah Festival in which he confirmed the Kingdom's keenness that security and peace prevail in the region; his call for cooperation to address poverty, ignorance and promotion of science, thought and culture; unification of ranks; and enlightening the nation; as well as his emphasis that Islam is a religion of moderation, justice and mercy, and what is seen today of terrorism from those who claim to be Muslims have no relation to Islam. At the outset of the session, King Salman briefed the Cabinet on the message sent to Emir of Kuwait Sheikh Sabah Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah; the messages received from King Mohammed VI of Kingdom of Morocco and Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani, Emir of Qatar; and the results of his meetings with Foreign Minister of Germany Frank-Walter Steinmeier and Chairman of Foreign Affairs Committee of European Parliament Elmar Brok. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques chaired the Cabinet's session at Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh this Monday afternoon.

content: Military Adviser to the Minister of Defense Brig Gen. title: Foreign Minister Arrives in Rome to Attend Anti-ISIS International Conference
content: Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir arrived in Rome to attend anti-ISIS international alliance conference to be held today. The conference will include the participation of 23 foreign ministers along with a representative of the European Union. The United States Secretary of State John Kerry and Italian Foreign Minister Paulo Jntelaun will chair the sessions.

title: Minister Al-Jubeir Meets with Turkish Prime Minister
content: Turkish Prime Minister Ahmet Davutoglu met at his residence in Riyadh today with Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir. During the meeting, they discussed issues of common interest and means of enhancing cooperation between the two countries.

title: King Salman Receives Turkish Prime Minister
content: Following the meeting, King Salman hosted a luncheon in honor of the Prime Minister. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz received in Riyadh today Turkish Prime Minister Ahmet Davutoglu. During the meeting, they reviewed bilateral relations and discussed aspects of cooperation between the two brotherly countries. They also discussed the latest developments at regional and international arenas.

title: King Salman Receives Asian Defense Officials
content: The audiences were attended by Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, Second Deputy Premier and Minister of Defense; Minister of State and Cabinet's Member Dr. Musaed bin Mohammed Al-Aiban; Minister of Culture and Information Dr. Adel bin Zaid Altoraifi; and Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz received at Al-Yamamah Palace today Indonesian Minister of Defense Ryamizard Ryacudu; Malaysian Minister of Defense Hishammuddin Hussein and Deputy Minister of Defense of the Sultanate of Brunei Lieutenant General Dato Abdulaziz Mohammed Tameet. The meeting discussed the mutual cooperation in defense fields between the countries.

title: Saudi Arabia provides share of Palestinian Authority budget for 2015
content: The Saudi Fund for Development transferred its share of support for the Palestinian Authority budget. The Kingdom transferred its payments for October, November and December 2015, totaling $60 million, to the Palestinian National Authority.

title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet Session
content: The Cabinet was then briefed on reports on the latest developments including the Palestinian issue, Syrian, and the latest developments in Yemen. The Cabinet expressed its strong condemnation of the terrorist attacks on a University in northwest Pakistan, and the center of the Afghan capital, Kabul. The Cabinet commended the statement of the Foreign Ministers on the rejection of Iran's interference in Bahrain, Yemen, Syria, Somalia, and its continuing support for terrorism. The Cabinet praised the statement issued by the Egyptian-Saudi Coordination Council in its fourth meeting in Cairo, its completion of the discussion of the topics set on its agenda and confirmation of the importance of completing the rest of the tasks. The Cabinet also commended the statement of the Foreign Ministers of the Organization of Islamic Cooperation which condemned the attacks on the Kingdom's missions in Tehran and Mashhad and described them as clear violations of Vienna Convention on Diplomatic Relations 1961, Vienna Convention on Consular Relations 1963 and the Organization of Islamic Cooperation and the United Nations Charters. The Cabinet commended the outcomes of the visit of Prime Minister of Pakistan Nawaz Sharif in enhancing closer brotherly relations between the two countries. The Cabinet considered the inauguration of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques for the project of Sinopec for Refining YASREF' and the opening of the King Abdullah Petroleum Studies and Research Center in Riyadh as an extension of the support and attention being provided to institutions and citizens. The Cabinet highlighted the outcomes of the Chinese President's visit to the Kingdom in signing 14 agreements and memorandums of understanding to enhance cooperation between the two countries in all fields and to upgrade relations to a comprehensive strategic partnership. King Salman began by briefing the Cabinet on his consultations and results of talks with President Xi Jinping of China, the Prime Minister of Pakistan Nawaz Sharif, US Secretary of State John Kerry, and NATO President Lord Jopling. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz chaired the Cabinet's session at Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh this afternoon.

title: King Salman Holds Bilateral Talks with Palestinian President Abbas
content: During their talks, the two leaders discussed developments in the occupied Palestinian territories. Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz held talks today with visiting Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas. At the outset of their meeting, King Salman was awarded the Medal of Honor, the highest honor granted by Palestine, in recognition of King Salman???Ć?Ös stances towards the Palestinian cause.

title: Saudi, Qatari, and Turkish Foreign Ministers Meet in New York
content: Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir met today with Qatari Foreign Ministers Khalid bin Mohammed Al-Atiyyah and Turkish Foreign Minister Molud Jawish Oglu in New York to discuss the latest developments of the Syrian crisis and the agenda of Vienna Group meeting to be held in New York.

title: Foreign Minister Arrives in Rome for Ministerial Meeting on Libya
content: The Saudi Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir arrived in Rome today to participate in the ministerial meeting on Libya. The meeting was chaired by Italian Foreign Minister Paulo Jantelaun, US Secretary of State John Kerry and UN envoy to Libya Martin Kobler.

title: Gulf Cooperation Council Supreme Council Concludes its 36th Session in Riyadh
content: GCC Secretary General Dr. Abdullatif Al-Zayani concluded by saying that the two-day summit is being held while the region is witnessing serious challenges that require vigilance and hard work in order to preserve GCC achievements and to seek further integration and solidarity to achieve the hopes and aspirations of the GCC citizens.???Ć?In his speech, Qatari Emir Sheikh Tamim, chairman of the 35th session of the Supreme Council, underscored the need for exerting great efforts in light of the critical circumstances, rapid changes and serious challenges facing the world in general and the Middle East in particular. King Salman called for completing the procedures to build a joint GCC defense and security system to safeguard the interests of the Gulf states and their people. On Yemen, the King expressed keenness of the coalition states to achieve security and stability under the leadership of its legitimate government. On Syria, King Salman said that the Kingdom is hosting a meeting of the Syrian opposition as support to them to find a political solution that guarantees the Syrian territorial unity and integrity in accordance with the decisions of the Geneva 1 conference. The King also called on all states in the world to shoulder their responsibility to fight extremism and terrorism and eradicate it whatever may be its sources, stressing the fact that Islam is a moderate religion that rejects terrorism. On the occasion of the 35th anniversary of the formation of the GCC, the King called for proper evaluation of the bloc???Ć?Ös achievements saying ???Ć?£It is an appropriate time to assess the achievements and look forward to the future.???Ć?The GCC leaders and heads of delegations included Sayyid Fahd bin Mahmoud Al-Said, Deputy Prime Minister for the Cabinet's Affairs of the Sultanate of Oman; Sheikh Sabah Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah, Emir of the State of Kuwait; King Hamad bin Isa Al Khalifa of Kingdom of Bahrain; Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani, Emir of the State of Qatar; and Sheikh Mohammed bin Rashid Al Maktoum, Vice-President of the United Arab Emirates, Prime Minister and Ruler of Dubai. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz and leaders and heads of delegations of Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) for Arab States concluded its two-day session of the 36th session of the GCC Supreme Council at Diriyah Palace in Riyadh today.

title: GCC Concludes 36th Session in Riyadh
content: GCC Secretary General Dr. Abdullatif Al-Zayani concluded by saying that the two-day summit is being held while the region is witnessing serious challenges that require vigilance and hard work in order to preserve GCC achievements and to seek further integration and solidarity to achieve the hopes and aspirations of the GCC citizens. In his speech, Qatari Emir Sheikh Tamim, chairman of the 35th session of the Supreme Council, underscored the need for exerting great efforts in light of the critical circumstances, rapid changes and serious challenges facing the world in general and the Middle East in particular. On Yemen, the King expressed keenness of the coalition states to achieve security and stability under the leadership of its legitimate government. On Syria, King Salman said that the Kingdom is hosting a meeting of the Syrian opposition as support to them to find a political solution that guarantees the Syrian territorial unity and integrity in accordance with the decisions of the Geneva 1 conference. The King also called on all states in the world to shoulder their responsibility to fight extremism and terrorism and eradicate it whatever may be its sources, stressing the fact that Islam is a moderate religion that rejects terrorism. On the occasion of the 35th anniversary of the formation of the GCC, the King called for proper evaluation of the bloc???Ć?Ös achievements saying ???Ć?£It is an appropriate time to assess the achievements and look forward to the future.???Ć?King Salman called for completing the procedures to build a joint GCC defense and security system to safeguard the interests of the Gulf states and their people. The GCC leaders and heads of delegations included Sayyid Fahd bin Mahmoud Al Said, Deputy Prime Minister for the Cabinet's Affairs of the Sultanate of Oman; Sheikh Sabah Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah, Emir of the State of Kuwait; King Hamad bin Isa Al Khalifa of Kingdom of Bahrain; Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani, Emir of the State of Qatar; and Sheikh Mohammed bin Rashid Al Maktoum, Vice-President of the United Arab Emirates, Prime Minister and Ruler of Dubai. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz and leaders and heads of delegations of Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) concluded its two-day session of the 36th session of the GCC Supreme Council at Diriyah Palace in Riyadh today.

title: King Salman holds Meeting with Syrian Opposition Leaders
content: ???Ć?£We wish that the Almighty Allah to guide you, in order that you may bring about security, stability and justice, in Syria.???Ć?King Salman said. He reiterated that his wish that the Syrian people close ranks wants Syria to be restored, as it was. Syria, he said, was used to provide the Kingdom with advisors, some of them have become ministers, ambassadors and physicians. ???Ć?£Since I was young, and I knew them, since the time of my late father, may Allah???Ć?Ös mercy be upon him.???Ć?At a meeting with leaders of the Syrian opposition, King Salman welcomed Syrian delegations and said that Syria, the cradle of Pan-Arabism, ???Ć?£is dear to us and our relation with Syria is historical and the Syrian and the Syrian brethren???Ć?Ös steadfast, is important to us.???Ć?Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz reaffirmed the Kingdom???Ć?Ös commitment to bring security, stability and justice to Syria.?é??

title: Foreign Minister Holds Press Conference after 36th session of the GCC Supreme Council
content: Al-Jubeir also confirmed that the GCC states are looking forward in establishing better relations with Iran, as an Islamic state and a neighbor, pointing out the GCC???Ć?Ös refusal of the negative role played by Iran and its interventions in the internal affairs of countries in the region, whether in Syria, Iraq, or Yemen, as well as its support for terrorism. Al-Jubeir wished that Iran could change its policies to have a positive role in the region. Regarding Qatar's host of World Cup 2022, Al-Jubeir and Al-Zayani stressed that the final communique of the 36th GCC supreme council supported this occasion, pointing out Qatar???Ć?Ös transparency in its efforts to host the World Cup. Regarding the Syrian file, Al-Jubeir talked about the Syrian Opposition Conference being held in Riyadh, stressing the Kingdom's commitment in supporting the legitimate rights of the Syrian people. He added that the conference aims to unify the Syrian opposition to enter into negotiations for a peaceful solution based on principles of 'Geneva1'. In a joint press conference with Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmad Al-Jubeir and GCC Secretary General Dr. Abdullatif bin Rashid Al-Zaynai held after 36th session of the GCC Supreme Council; Al-Jubeir stressed that the session represents a chance to review and assess the Council after 35 years of its establishment, stressing the fact the GCC leaders are keen to enhance cooperation in political, economic, social, military, security and other fields of joint cooperation.

title: King Salman Orders Investments in Egypt Exceed $8 billion
content: The second meeting of the council was held in Cairo yesterday in the presence of members of both sides. The Egyptian side was led Prime Minister Engineer Sherif Ismail Mohammed, and the Saudi side by Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, the Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense. This came as a result of the joint statement for second meeting of Saudi-Egyptian Coordination Council for the implementation of the Cairo Declaration, signed in Riyadh on 11/11/2015 calling in item (iii) of it for the council to meet periodically and alternately between the two countries. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King ordered the Kingdom to help meet Egypt???Ć?Ös petroleum needs for the next five years. He also ordered that Saudi investments in Egypt be more than SR 30 billion Riyals ($8 billion).

title: Saudi Arabia and Costa Rica Establish Diplomatic Relations
content: Saudi Permanent Representative to the U.N. Ambassador Abdullah bin Yahya Al-Mualami and Costa Rica???Ć?Ös Permanent Representative to the U.N. Ambassador Juan Carlos Mendoza Garcia signed an agreement in New York today on the establishment of diplomatic relations.

title: Saudi Embassy in U.K. Warns Citizens Against Suspicious Places
content: In a cautionary statement released today, the embassy said, ???Ć?£In view of the security situation in the United Kingdom following the unfortunate incident that took place in the subway in east London and security threats in Europe in general, the embassy hopes that all Saudi citizens who are in the United Kingdom and Northern Ireland to be cautious, stay away of suspicious places, be careful not to enter into controversial discussions and not respond to any possible provocations.???Ć?The Saudi Embassy in the United Kingdom urged citizens to exercise caution following the stabbing at an east London subway station.

title: GCC Foreign Ministers Hold Supplementary Meeting of 137th Preparatory Session of the GCC Summit
content: Al-Jubeir said that GCC participants are very keen that the summit would achieve tangible outcomes in all fields. Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir, who is also chairman of the current session, led the Kingdom's delegation to the meeting with presence of the GCC foreign ministers and GCC Secretary General Dr. Abdulatif Al-Zayani. The foreign ministers of the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) Member States held in Riyadh today a supplementary meeting of the 137th preparatory session of the 36th GCC Summit to be held in Riyadh on Wednesday.

title: Foreign Minister Meets President of Iraq Kurdistan Region
content: The Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir met the President of Iraq Kurdistan Region Massoud Barzani in Riyadh today. During the meeting, aspects of joint cooperation between the Kingdom and Iraq Kurdistan Region as well as issues of mutual concern were discussed. The meeting was attended by Deputy Chief of General Intelligence General Yousef bin Ali Al-Edreesi and Saudi Ambassador to Iraq Thamer Al-Sabhan.

title: Former Spanish King Leaves Riyadh
content: Former King Juan Carlos of Kingdom of Spain left Riyadh today. He was seen off at King Salman Air Base Airport by Prince Faisal bin Bandar bin Abdulaziz, Governor of Riyadh region, and a number of officials. King Juan arrived at King Salman Airbase Airport near Riyadh on Tuesday where he met by the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz. During his one-day visit, King Salman hosted a dinner in honor of the former Spanish monarch.

title: King, Deputy Crown Prince Meet with President of Iraq Kurdistan Region
content: Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques and Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman met separately today with the president of Iraq Kurdistan Region Massoud Barzani, in Riyadh. During the meetings, aspects of bilateral cooperation in addition to a number of issues of mutual concern were discussed.

title: Saudi Fund for Development to Fund Development Project in Jordan
content: The total grants and loans provided by the Kingdom to Jordan amounted to SR 6.98 billion ($1.86 billion). The first agreement is a $65 million grant to finance the reconstruction and rehabilitation Highway 15 (also known as the Desert Highway) which runs from Aqaba in the south to Ma???Ć?Öan in the north. The second agreement is a framework agreement to grant $100 million as contribution by Saudi Arabia to support priority development projects in the Jordanian communities hosting Syrian refugees. The third agreement is a $53.3 million soft loan guarantee to finance the fourth steam-unit project for the Samra power plant. The Saudi Fund for Development and Jordan today singed three agreements in Amman to finance development projects in the Hashemite Kingdom.

title: Kingdom Welcomes British Air Campaign Against Daesh (ISIS)
content: The Kingdom today welcomed the United Kingdom's decision to participate in the air campaign against Daesh (ISIS). A senior official at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs announced today that Saudi Arabia was one of the first countries to join in the formation of the international core coalition to fight Daesh in Syria and Iraq and was also the first to actively participate in military actions against the terrorist group in Syria.

title: Kingdom Designates Hezbollah Individuals and Entities as Terrorists
content:
Mohammed Salman Fawaz. 
Mohamed Najib Karim. 
Abdel Nour Al-Shaalan. 
Fouad Shukr. 
Ibrahim Aqil. 
Mustafa Badr Eddin. 
Hussein Ali Faour, and car care center. 
Qasim Hajij. Adham Tabaja and his company and the group of development for business tourism and its subsidiaries. : Bint Jubail, Lebanon. 
 : September 1970, 1 January 1974, 1980, 
 : Muhammad Yusuf Ahmed Mansour (better knows as Sami Hani Shehab, Salem Basim Sami, Jamal Hani Halawi, Mohammed Yusuf Mansour Sami Shehab, Sami Shehab and Hani Halawi,) : Najaf, Iraq. 
 : 1945.1959, 1961 
 : Lebanese and Iraqi, 
 : Mohammed Kawtharani (also known as Mohammed AlKawtharani and Jafar Al-Kawtharani : Beirut, Lebanon; Al-Karrada, Baghdad, Iraq. 
 : September 1, 1969; December 31, 1971; August 9, 1971, September 9, 1970, August 9, 1969, 5 March 1972 
 : Lebanese 
 : Ali Mousa Daqduq Al-Moussawi (as known as Ali Mousa Daqduq, Hamid Mohammed Jabr Al-Lami, Hamid Mohammed Al-Lami, Hussein Mohammed Jabr Al-Moussawi, Hamid Mohammed Jabr al-Moussawi Hamid Mohammed Daqdug Al-Moussawi and Hamid Majed Abdul Younis) The Interior Ministry added that today???Ć?Ös designation is pursuant to Royal Decree A/44 on the Regulation of Crimes of Terrorism and Terror Financing, which targets terrorists, their supporters and those working with them or on their behalf, where any assets belonging to those classified names shall be frozen in accordance with regulations in the Kingdom and Saudi citizens be banned from any dealing with them. The Ministry of Interior added that as Hezbollah is spreading chaos and instability, launching terrorist attacks and carrying-out criminal activities around the world, Saudi Arabia will continue to designate activists, leaders and entities belonging to Hezbollah and sanction them. The Ministry of Interior said in announcing the designation said that the Kingdom will continue to fight Hezbollah???Ć?Ös terrorist activities by all available means and will continue to work with partners around to make it unequivocally clear that its militias and its extremist activities will not be tolerated by any country. Saudi Arabia today designated a number of Hezbollah individuals and entities as terrorists for their activities throughout the Middle East, including providing arms to groups outside of Lebanon.

title: Saudi-Iraqi Border Officials Hold Third Round of Talks
content: Director General of the Border Guards Rear Admiral Awad bin Eid al-Balawi met today in Jeddah with the commander of the Iraqi border guards Vice Admiral Hamed bin Abdullah Ibrahim. and his accompanying delegation on Sunday at King Abdulaziz International Airport in Jeddah. The discussed boarder security and the creation of a joint mechanism to counter terrorist operations in the border area.

title: Kingdom Condemns Terrorist Attack on Hotel in Mali
content: Saudi Arabia today condemned the terrorist attack at the Radisson Blu Hotel in Bamako, Mali and expressed its condolences to the families of the victims and the government of Mali and its people, and wished the injured a speedy recovery.

title: Colors of French Flag on Kingdom Tower in Riyadh
content: As an expression of condemnation by the Kingdom of the Paris terrorist attacks on Friday, both the Kingdom and Alfaisaliah Towers in Riyadh were decorated with the lights of the colors of the French flag. The lights will continue for three days.

title: King Salman Holds Bilateral Meeting at G-20
content: Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz held bilateral on the sideline of the G-20 Summit in Antalya with German Chancellor Angela Merkel, Turkish Prime Minister Ahmet Davutoglu and U.S. President Barack Obama.

title: King Salman Attends G-20 Summit in Turkey
content: The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz headed the Kingdom's delegation to the summit of leaders of the Group of Twenty, which started today in Antalya, Turkish.

title: King Salman Express Condolences to French President
content: Earlier, King Salman sent a cable to the French president in which he condemned the terrorist attack and called for concerted international efforts to fight this serious scourge which is not sanctioned by all divine religions and international norms and conventions, and to rid the international community of its evils. Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz today expressed his condolences to French President Francois Hollande on the Paris terrorist attacks. In a telephone call King Salman and President Hollande agreed on enhancing coordination and cooperation to combat the scourge of terrorism in all its forms and manifestations which target security and stability all over the world.

title: Saudi Arabia Condemns Paris Terrorist Attack
content: Saudi Arabia underscores the importance of the world community to stand united to double its efforts to root out this dangerous and destructive plague, which targets the security and stability of the entire world. The source added that an effective means for joint international action was needed to fight those who sought to harm global security under any pretext. A source at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs expressed condolences of the Saudi government and people to the families of the victims, the government and people of the France, and wished the injured a quick recovery. Saudi Arabia today strongly denounces the terror attacks in Paris and urges for global solidarity to defeat terrorism.

title: Deputy Crown Prince Meets Leaders of Bahrain, Jordan, Lebanon, and Egypt
content: Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense, met this evening separately with King Hamad bin Isa Al Khalifa of Bahrain, King Abdullah II bin Al-Hussein of Jordan, Prime Minister of Lebanon Tammam Salam and President Abdel Fattah Al-Sisi of Egypt individually on the sidelines the 4th Summit of South American-Arab Countries.

title: King Salman Arrives in Turkey for G-20 Summit
content: The official Saudi delegation accompanying King Salman includes Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense; Minister of State Dr. Musaed bin Mohammed Al-Aiban; Minister of Finance Dr. Ibrahim bin Abdulaziz Al-Assaf; Minister of Economy and Planning Engineer Adel bin Mohammed Faqih; Minister of Commerce and Industry Dr. Tawfiq bin Fawzan Al-Rabiah; Minister of Culture and Information Dr. Adel bin Zaid Altoraifi; Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir; Minister of State, Acting Chief of the Royal Court Khaled bin Abdulrahman Al-Essa; and Governor of Saudi Arabian Monetary Agency (SAMA) Dr. Fahd bin Abdullah Al-Mubarak.

title: Kingdom, Egypt Form Council to Implement Cairo Declaration
content: The agreement was signed by Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir and his Egyptian counterpart Sameh Shoukry. King Salman bin Abdulaziz and Egyptian President Abdel Fattah A-Sisi agreed to establish a Saudi-Egyptian Coordination Council tasked to implement the Cairo Declaration. Meeting at the sideline of the 4th Summit of South American-Arab Countries, King Salman and President Al-Sisi agreed to set up a committee to implement the Cairo Declaration which was signed in Cairo July 30 by Deputy Crown Prince and Defense Minister Mohammed bin Salman and President Al-Sisi and seeks to boost economic and military ties.

title: King Salman Inaugurates 4th Summit of South American-Arab Countries
content: At the sideline of the summit, King Salman held bilateral talks with a number of leaders at the summit, inducing Egyptian President Abdel Fattah Al-Sissy, U.N. Secretary General Ban Ki-Moon; King Abdullah II bin Al-Hussein of Jordan, Tunisian Prime Minister Habib Essid, Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro Moros, President of Guyana David Arthur Granger. President Rafael Correa Delgado of Ecuador, and President Mohammed Fuad Masum of Iraq. Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz last evening inaugurated the 4th Summit of South American-Arab Countries in Riyadh.

title: Saudi Arabia, Serbia Sign MoU on Heritage and Tourism
content: Saudi Arabia and Serbia today signed a memorandum of understanding on heritage and tourism in Belgrade. The MoU was signed by Prince Sultan bin Salman, President of the Saudi Commission for Tourism and National Heritage and Rasim Ljajic, Serbia???Ć?Ös Deputy Premier and Minister of Commerce, Tourism and Communications.

title: Foreign Minister Addresses Manama Dialogue Forum
content: As for the visit of Omani Foreign Minister to Syria, he said, ???Ć?£we do not question the intentions of the Sultanate of Oman and we are confident that it came for approaching the views with Syria.???Ć?The foreign minister expressed no objection to the promotion of relations between Saudi Arabia and Iran, noting that the two countries had good relations before the Iranian revolution. Minister Al-Jubeir said, ???Ć?£the Gulf countries seek to enhance security and stability in the region, and have extended a friendly hand to Iran, which for its part, has made attempts to smuggle weapons to the Kingdom of Bahrain and the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, and now the ball is in Iran's court to prove its good intentions in the next phase and to desist from interfering in the internal affairs of neighboring countries.???Ć?Addressing Manama Dialogue Forum yesterday, he said that Syrian President Bashar Al-Assad should depart so that Syria returns to security and stability and that that the Vienna meeting reviewed the gradual establishment of a transitional government ensuring security non-looseness, hoping that Russia persuades Al-Assad to depart. Minister Al-Jubeir renewed his welcome of Iran's nuclear agreement, considering it as an appropriate opportunity for Tehran to prove its good intentions for reintegration and play an active role in the region, asking whether Iran intends to change its policy carried out over the past years towards the countries of the region. The Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir said that the meeting held recently in Vienna on Syria was characterized by frank discussions among all parties.

title: Joint Saudi-Pakistani Military Exercise Concluded
content: The drill, which was held in Islamabad, focused on counter terrorism training of the special operation forces of Pakistan and Saudi Arabia and included cordon and search operations, airdropping and heli lifting of special forces. The Saudi-Pakistani Joint Training Military Drill (Al-Shihab) concluded today within framework of the military cooperation between the two countries.

title: Foreign Minister Attends Paris Talk on Syria
content: Minister Al-Jubeir said that the meeting discussed developments in Syria, and the importance of uniting efforts on the principles of the Geneva I based on maintaining the civilian and military state institutions and the formation of a transitional body for government with no place for Bashar Al-Assad. The meeting also discussed the importance of the departure of foreign troops and militias from Syria to advance the political process in Syria, according to these principles and frameworks. Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir represented the Kingdom yesterday at the Syria talks in Paris. The meeting, which was held at the French Foreign Ministry, included Saudi Arabia, United Arab Emirates, Jordan, Qatar, Turkey, France, Germany, the United States, Italy and Britain.

title: King Salman Receives British Foreign Secretary
content: During the meeting, they reviewed bilateral relations between the two friendly countries, ways of enhancing them, and the latest developments at regional and international arenas. Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz received today British Foreign Secretary Philip Hammond at Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh.

title: King Salman Receives Call from Russian President
content: Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz received a telephone call today from Russian President Vladimir Putin. During their conversation, they discussed bilateral relations in addition to the latest regional and international developments.

title: King Salman Receives German Foreign Minister
content: The meeting was attended by Crown Prince Mohammed bin Naif bin Abdulaziz. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques received at Al-Yamamah Palace today German Foreign Minister Dr. Frank-Walter Steinmeier. The meeting discussed bilateral relations between the two countries, ways of enhancing them in various fields, and the latest developments on regional and international arenas.

title: Crown Prince Receives UN Envoy to Yemen
content: Crown Prince Mohammed bin Naif bin Abdulaziz, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior, received in Riyadh yesterday UN Secretary General's Envoy to Yemen Ismail Ould Al-Sheikh Ahmed. During the meeting, they discussed latest developments related to Yemeni affairs, UN efforts to find a peaceful solution and the implementation of UN Security Council Resolution 2216 to ensure the security and stability in Yemen.

title: Foreign Minister Holds Meeting with UN Envoy to Yemen
content: Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir received today UN Secretary General Special Envoy to Yemen Ismail Ould Al-Sheikh Ahmed. The meeting discussed the latest developments in Yemen, in the context of the political endeavors to implement UN Resolution 2216. The meeting was attended by a senior official and the Yemeni Ambassador to the Kingdom.

title: Kingdom's Position on Syria Unchanged
content: ???Ć?£I believe that the matter of the end of hostilities lies entirely with the Houthis and with Saleh," the minister said. "They are the ones who started this, and they are the ones who continue this.???Ć?Minister Al-Jubeir dismissed comments by former president Ali Abdullah Salah denying the presence of Iranians in Yemen, saying that the former president has a history of making statements devoid of reality. The minister stressed that ???Ć?£everyone knows that the Iranians are present in Yemen, provide support for the Houthis, supply them with weapons, and provide them with military experts.???Ć?The Foreign Minister also discussed the ongoing Saudi-led campaign in Yemen, saying the coalition has achieved success in reversing the gains by Houthi forces and now is the time for a political process to begin. ???Ć?£There is no change. From the beginning of the crisis, the position of the Kingdom is that Al-Assad is the problem in the Syria crisis,???Ć?said the Foreign Minister. ???Ć?£He killed hundreds of thousands of his people, displaced millions and destroyed the country as a whole???Ć??he has no future in Syria.???Ć?Minister Al-Jubeir said Saudi Arabia is working to convince Russia to push for a political solution to the crisis that does not include the presence of Al-Assad. Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir, at a joint news conference with French Foreign Minister Laurent Fabius in Riyadh today, reiterated the Kingdom???Ć?Ös position towards the crisis in Syria, maintaining that President Bashar al-Assad is the cause of the ongoing conflict and has no future in Syria.

title: King Salman Meets with French Prime Minister
content: Earlier in the day, the prime minister met with Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior. The meeting was attended by Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Defense Minister. Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz held bilateral meetings with visiting French Prime Minister Manuel Valls. During their meeting, they discussed bilateral relations and ways to enhance them in various fields, as well as the latest regional developments.

title: Saudi Arabia Provides Share of Palestinian Authority Budget
content: The Saudi Fund for Development transferred its share of support for the Palestinian Authority budget. Saudi Ambassador to Egypt Ahmed Abdulaziz Kattan reported today that the Kingdom transferred its payments for July, August and September 2015, totaling $60 million, to the Palestinian National Authority. Since January 2013, the Kingdom increased its assistance to the Palestinian Authority's budget from $14 million to $20 million a month.

title: Kingdom Condemns Terrorist Bombing in Ankara
content: In a statement issued following the terrorist attack, the ministry expressed the Kingdom???Ć?Ös solidarity with Turkey in its war against terrorism and offered the condolences of the Government and people of Saudi Arabia to the families of the victims and to the government and people of Turkey, wishing the injured a speedy recovery. The Foreign Ministry today strongly condemned and denounced the criminal terrorist attack in Ankara, Turkey.

title: Al-Moa'allami: Kingdom is Honored to Stand with Palestinian People
content: In an Ad Hoc Committee for Voluntary Contributions to the UN Relief and Works Agency for Palestine Refugees (UNRWA) in New York yesterday, Permanent Representative of the Kingdom to the UN, Ambassador Abdallah Y. Al-Moa'allami, stressed that the Kingdom is honored to stand with the Palestinian people to achieve their legitimate rights to live in freedom and dignity, noting that the Kingdom provided more than $350 million to UNRWA since 2013. He added that the Kingdom has also recently contributed more than $30 million to cover UNRWA's deficit budget and to ensure that the schools it was sponsoring would not be closed, in response to appeals of the United Nations and the Government of Jordan.

title: Foreign Minister Receives UN Deputy Secretary-General
content: Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir received at his office Jeddah today Deputy Secretary General of the United Nations Jan Eliasson. During the meeting, they discussed regional and international developments.

title: Foreign Minister Receives Russian Ambassador
content: Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir received at his office Jeddah today Russian Ambassador Oleg Ozerov. During the meeting, they discussed regional and international developments and issues of mutual interest between the two countries.

title: Foreign Minister Receives British Ambassador
content: Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmad Al-Jubeir, received at his office in Jeddah today British ambassador to Saudi Arabia Simon Collis. During the meeting, they reviewed bilateral relations and discussed issues of common interest.

title: King Salman Holds Talks with Mauritania's President
content: The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques, King Salman bin Abdulaziz, held official talks with visiting Mauritania's President Mohamed Ould Abdel Aziz at Al-Salam Palace in Jeddah today. The talks focused on ways of enhance bilateral relations in various fields and latest regional and international developments. The meeting was also attended by Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman.

title: Foreign Minister of Saudi Arabia, Qatar and UAE Met with Aid Agencies on Yemen
content: Following the meeting, the Kingdom???Ć?Ös UN Ambassador Abdullah bin Yahya Al-Mouallimi said at a press conference that assistance being provided by the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) to the Yemen will continue for years to come. Ambassador Al-Mouallimi added that Saudi aid to Yemen has reached over $500 million. On the sidelines of the 70th session of the United Nations General Assembly, Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir, Foreign Minister Dr. Khalid Al-Attiyah of Qatar, and Foreign Minister Sheikh Abdullah bin Zayed Al-Nahyan of the United Arab Emirate, met this evening in Washington with the heads of relief organizations operating in Yemen. During the meeting, they discussed the humanitarian situation in Yemen as well as the assistance provided so far.

title: Kingdom Addresses 70th UN General Assembly
content: The foreign minister added that the Kingdom welcomes all efforts to make the Middle East a zone free of nuclear weapons and all weapons of mass destruction, including the P5+1 Group agreement with Iran, which prohibits Iran from obtaining a nuclear weapon. Al-Jubeir also confirmed that there are parties are trying to escalate the crisis in Yemen through incitement and attempt to smuggle arms to the rebels. For example, on Saturday, Sept. 26, an Iranian ship carrying weapons destined for the Houthis rebels, was stop under UN Security Council Resolution 2216. The minister noted that this was not the only case of the weapons smuggling to the Houthis by Iran. On Yemen, the Minister explained that coalition forces to support the legitimacy in Yemen has made great achievements in the liberation of many areas from the rebels' grip, including Aden, which allowed for the legitimate government to return. He added that meanwhile, the Kingdom and the coalition countries are working to intensify coordination with international organizations to provide humanitarian aid to Yemen, and the King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Center played a major role in providing humanitarian assistance to the Yemeni people. Regarding Syria, Al-Jubeir pointed out that the Syrian crisis is entering its fifth year, and the international community is still unable to take crucial decisions to save the Syrian people. So far, more than 300,000 of Syrians have been killed and twelve million displaced, as well as the destruction of Syria in one of the biggest humanitarian catastrophe of the contemporary history. Minister Al-Jubeir added ???Ć?£We see there is no way to end Syrian crisis but only through a political solution, based on the Declaration of Geneva 1,???Ć? pointing out that the Kingdom will continue to push for this solution to end Al-Assad regime. Al-Jubeir added that the Palestinian issue will continue to be a permanent topic on the agenda of the General Assembly until Palestinians achieve their right to establish a Palestinian independent state with Al-Quds (Jerusalem) as its capital and called to resolve the Arab-Israeli conflict, based on Security Council resolutions. He added, ???Ć?£In this regard, we have to renew our condemnation of the continuing Israeli violations of Al-Aqsa Mosque's sanctity, via policies aimed at dividing it at time and space levels, a matter poses a serious escalation in the conflict, and feeds the violence and extremism in the world.???Ć?In his address, Minister Al-Jubeir renewed the Kingdom???Ć?Ös determination to eradicate the phenomenon of terrorism from its roots and to dry its financial resources. He also strongly denounced the terrorists' attempts to connection their crimes to Islam, which calls for love, peace, tolerance and moderation. Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir this evening addressed the 70th General Assembly of the United Nations in New York, expressed keenness of the Kingdom to achieve the purposes of the UN Charter in maintaining the security and safety of the peoples of the world.

title: Foreign Minister Hold Sideline Meetings During the UN General Assembly
content:
Foreign Minister Al Jubeir also met Khalid Khoja, the President of the Syrian National Coalition at the Kingdom's Permanent Mission to the UN. During the meeting, they discussed the Syrian crisis and its latest developments in addition to ways to support the legitimate rights of the Syrian people and the preservation of Syrian unity and sovereignty. 
French Foreign Minister Laurent Fabius, and Sudanese foreign minister Ibrahim Ghandoor. In other separate meetings, Minister Al-Jubeir met U.S. Secretary of State John Kerry. Discussions included bilateral relations, regional and international issues as well as topics on the agenda of the General Assembly. Meetings with Al-Jubeir???Ć?Ös counterparts included; Spain's Foreign Minister Jose Manuel Garcia, Albania's Foreign Minister Ditmir Bushati, Foreign Minister of Afghanistan Salahuddin Rabbani, Foreign Minister of Venezuela Delcy Rodriguez, Somali Foreign Minister Abdulsalam bin Omer, Tunisian Foreign Minister Taieb Baccouche, Minister Al-Jubeir met Secretary-General of the United Nations Ban Ki-moon, the High Commissioner for Foreign Policy of the European Union Federica Mogherini and President of the International Atomic Energy Agency Yukiya Amano. Discussions reviewed regional and international issues as well as topics on the General Assembly's agenda. On the sidelines of the 70th session of the General Assembly of the United Nations in New York, Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir, met with many of his counterparts and important world officials.

title: Saudi Arabia Calls for Support on Uprooting the Terrorism
content: Minister Al-Jubeir called on all countries to provide necessary support for the Counter-Terrorism Centre, which was established by the Kingdom under the umbrella of the UN. "Based on the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia's conviction that the best way to confront this great evil is through collective and organized work, it has been keen to comply with the requirements of UN Security Council Resolutions 1267, 1989, 2161, 2170 and 2178 in addition to taking internal measures adopted for the purpose of countering terrorism and extremism.,???Ć?Minister Al-Jubeir said. He added that in the context of its anti-terrorism stance, the Kingdom condemns false statements made by some terrorist that make the religion of Islam a pretext for their horrendous acts, stressing the fact that Islam is the religion of peace, tolerance and moderation. In remarks at the Leaders' Summit on Countering ISIL and Violent Extremism hosted by the United Nations, Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir reaffirmed the Kingdom???Ć?Ös resolve to uproot the terrorism phenomenon.

title: Foreign Minister Receives Chinese Minister
content: Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir received at his office in Jeddah today the Deputy Foreign Minister of China Chang Ming. During the meeting, they discussed the developments at the regional and international arenas and as well as issues of mutual concern. They also reviewed aspects of cooperation between the two countries and ways of developing them.

title: Minister Al-Jubeir Attends Arab League Meeting in Cairo
content: The Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir arrived in Cairo, heading the Saudi delegation to the 144th session of the Arab league at the level of Arab foreign ministers. The Arab foreign ministers will discuss most of Arab common issues; politically, economically and socially, and the draft decisions submitted by permanent representatives. On the sidelines of the summit, Minister Al-Jubeir and Egyptian Foreign Minister Samih Shoukri held a bilateral meeting.

title: Vice Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Receives Yemeni President
content: The meeting was attended by the Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir and the Chief of General Intelligence Khalid bin Ali Al-Himaidan. For his part, the Vice Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques emphasized the Kingdom keenness, under the leadership of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz, that fraternal Yemen and its people to entertain much more security and stability. The president highly appreciated the Kingdom???Ć?Ös stand towards Yemen and its people, especially backing up the legitimacy and providing the Yemeni people with relief as well as medical assistances. During the reception, they discussed issues of common interest, in addition to reviewing latest developments, in Yemen, especially the warfare situation. Vice Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Prince Mohammad bin Naif received at Al-Salam Palace in Jeddah today with Yemeni President Abdu Rabbo Mansur Hadi.

title: King Salman Vows Full Support to Yemen's Hadi
content: Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz held wide ranging talks with Yemen???Ć?Ös President Abdrabbu Mansour Hadi in Tangier, Morocco today. During the meeting, King Salman reiterated the Kingdom???Ć?Ös full support of Yemen and its legitimate government and its keenness in safeguarding Yemen???Ć?Ös stability and security.

title: Foreign Ministry Advises Citizens to Avoid Travel to Lebanon
content: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs today issued a travel warning for Lebanon. The ministry advised Saudi citizens to avoid travel to Lebanon due to the current crisis and urged citizens in Lebanon to be vigilant and to contact the embassy in Beirut in case of emergency.

title: Kingdom Expands Aid Efforts in Yemen
content: The King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Center, in coordination with Doctors without Borders, is expanded relief efforts in Yemen. The Center began distributing infant formula in Dhale Province and plans to expand its relief activities to include additional Yemeni provinces.

title: UNRWA Thanks Kingdom for Helping Budget Shortfall
content: In May, the Saudi Fund for Development and UNRWA signed agreements worth $111.5 million, bringing to $146.5 million the total of the SFD???Ć?Ös donations to UNRWA so far for 2015. Saudi Arabia is the third largest donor to UNRWA. Last week, Saudi Arabia informed UNRWA that it has approved an assistance package worth US$ 35 million dollars. UNRWA announced on August 12 that "the majority will be spent on education and health in Gaza, on upgrading schools and health centers in Jordan, on the construction of three health centers in the West Bank and on maintaining buildings across the areas where UNRWA operates. It is estimated that $19 million will contribute to reducing the current financial deficit of $101 million in UNRWA???Ć?Ös General Fund for core services such as education and health." The UN Palestinians Refugees Relief and Works Agency (UNRWA) expressed gratitude to Saudi Arabia, Kuwait and the United Arab Emirates for their contributions of $78.9 million that provided the agency with more than half of the total deficit of its 2015 Fiscal Year budget.

title: Kingdom Condemns Attack on the United Arab Emirates Embassy in Yemen
content: The Foreign Ministry expressed the Kingdom???Ć?Ös condemnation of the Houthi criminal assault on the Embassy of the United Arab Emirates in the Yemeni capital Sana'a.

title: Minister Al-Jubeir Meets his Russian Counterpart in Moscow
content: On the issue of Iraq, the Minister Al-Jubeir emphasized the importance of implementing the agreed upon reforms to ensure the rights of all the various and different sects. On Yemen, Al-Jubeir emphasized the importance of non-conditional implementation of UN Resolution 2216 to pull Yemen out of its crisis and to intensify efforts in providing humanitarian aid. At a joint press conference with Foreign Minister Lavroy, Minister Al-Jubeir described the meeting as positive, and reflected the great keenness on enhancing the strategic relations between the two countries. Regarding the situation in Syria, Al-Jubeir said that ???Ć?£despite divergence in the points of view between the two countries on the Syrian crisis, there is convergence toward the importance of uniting the ranks of the Syrian opposition.???Ć?Al-Jubeir said that the Kingdom???Ć?Ös position ???Ć?£has not changed... there is no place for Assad in the future of Syria.???Ć?The minister added. ???Ć?£we think that Bashar Assad is part of the problem, not part of the solution.???Ć?Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir met with Russian Foreign Minister Sergei Lavrov in Moscow today to discuss means of enhancing issues of mutual interests, including the crises in Syria and Yemen.

title: Saudi Foreign Minister Expresses Views on Iran
content: Al-Jubeir also talked about the situation in Yemen and the safety of its people, pointing out that the intervention of the kingdom and the coalition states was based at the request of the legitimate Yemeni President Abed Rabbo Mansour Hadi. At a joint press conference with German Foreign Minister Frank Walter Steinmeier in Berlin today, Minister Al-Jubeir said that Iran's refusal to allow International Atomic Energy Agency's inspectors to enter some military installations only means Tehran's intention to make nuclear weapons and that Saudi Arabia is closely monitoring the Iranian policy developments concerning uranium enrichment. Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir said that Saudi Arabia is keen on developing relations with Iran on condition that Iran changes its policy, which is not to intervene in the affairs of the Gulf Cooperation Council and other countries in the region, considering the negative consequences resulting from Tehran???Ć?Ös continuous intervention in Syria, Lebanon, Yemen, Iraq and Bahrain.

title: King Salman Center Help Repatriates 1,400 Yemeni from Djibouti
content: King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Center is currently in the process repatriating 1,400 Yemeni refugees in Djibouti to Yemen. The Center???Ć?Ös spokesman Rafat Al-Sabbagh said a sicne Augsut 6, four flights have repatriated 628Yemenis to their homeland.

title: Minister Al-Jubeir, Italian Counterpart Hold Joint Press Conference
content: Foreign Minister Paolo Gentiloni emphasized Italy's solidarity with the Kingdom on the Syrian, Yemen and Iran issues and on its efforts to combat terrorism. The two ministers hailed the progress achieved by the Coalition Forces in Yemen in support of the legitimate government. Minister Al-Jubeir said the international community should not ignore Iran's continuous support of the Al-Assad, a fact that aggravates the crisis.?é?? Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir and Italy's counterpart Paolo Gentiloni held a joint press conference, following their talks. The foreign ministers discussed regional issues including Yemen, Syria and the nuclear agreement between Iran and the P 5+1 Group.

title: Saudi Arabia and Fiji Establish Diplomatic Relations
content: Saudi Permanent Representative to the U.N. Ambassador Abdullah bin Yahya Al-Mualami and Fiji???Ć?Ös Permanent Representative to the U.N. Ambassador Peter Thompson signed an agreement in New York today on the establishment of diplomatic relations.

title: Deputy Crown Prince Arrives in Jordan
content: Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense, arrived in Jordan for an official visit at the invitation of King Abdullah II bin Al-Hussein. The Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed is scheduled to meet with King Abdullah during his visit.

title: GCC Foreign Ministers, US Secretary of State Hold Joint Meeting
content: The Ministers of Foreign Affairs of Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) and U.S. Secretary of State John Kerry started their joint meeting in Doha today. The Saudi delegation is headed by the Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir.

title: King Salman Arrives in Morocco on a Private Vacation
content: The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz arrived in Tangier, Morocco today on a private vacation. King Salman was received by Morocco Prime Minister Abdelillah Benkirane and Governor of Tangier Mohamed Yaqubi.

title: Kingdom to Open Embassy in Maldives
content: Saudi Arabia embassy in Mal?ā??, Maldives, will begin operations on August 2, 2015. The Maldives opened its embassy in Riyadh on May 12, 2008. Saudi Arabia and the Maldives established diplomatic relations in 1981.

title: Saudi Arabia Condemns Attack by Israeli Settlers in Douma
content: Minister Al-Jubeir offered the Kingdom's condolences to the family of the infant, the government and people of Palestine, and expressed his wishes for a speedy recovery of the injured. Foreign Minister Al-Jubeir called on the international community to fulfill its responsibilities and take the necessary measures to protect the Palestinian population from the aggressive practices of Jewish settlers. Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir today strongly denounced yesterday???Ć?Ös terrorist attack carried out by Jewish settlers in the village of Douma in the West Bank, burning to death a toddler and severely burning his parents and 4 year old brother.

title: Saudi Arabia, Egypt Sign ???Ć?£Cairo Declaration???Ć?to Boost Ties, Regional Security
content: Based on the directives of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques, the Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz and President Abdel Fattah Al-Sisi of Egypt signed an agreement in Cairo today (Cairo Declaration) aims to increase military, economic, and cultural cooperation between the two countries. The agreement calls on both countries to develop a package of executive mechanisms in the following areas:

title: Saudi Arabia and Namibia Establish Diplomatic Relations
content: Saudi Permanent Representative to the U.N. Ambassador Abdullah bin Yahya Al-Mualami and Namibia???Ć?Ös Permanent Representative to the U.N. Ambassador Wilfried Emvula signed an agreement in New York on the establishment of diplomatic relations.

title: OIC and GCC Secretary Criticize Nuri Al-Maliki's Statement on Saudi Arabia
content: The OIC general secretariat issued a statement in which it said it considered the Al-Maliki???Ć?Ös comments irresponsible, especially coming from a senior official of the Iraqi government. It added that the vice president???Ć?Ös statement feeds into the sectarian and denominational agenda at a time when concerted efforts must be made by all OIC member states to confront common challenges; combat terrorism, extremist ideology and sectarian division. Dr. Al-Zayani said these provocative and unrealistic statements come within the known efforts of Al-Maliki to sabotage Iraq's relations its Arab neighbors. GCC Secretary General Dr. Abdullatif Al-Zayani strongly condemned Maliki's remarks, against Saudi Arabia, describing the remarks as irresponsible, false and heinous accusations that do not help to strengthen the Gulf-Iraqi relations. The General Secretariat of the Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) and the Secretary General of Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) criticized the anti-Saudi statement made by Iraq's Vice President and former Prime Minister Nuri Al-Maliki on July 17 on the Iraq-based Afaq TV channel.?é??

title: King Salman Holds Meeting with Pakistani Prime Minister
content: Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz held a meeting at Al-Safa Palace in Makkah today with Pakistani Prime Minister Nawaz Sharif in which they discussed aspects of bilateral cooperation between the two sisterly countries and latest developments at regional and international arenas. The meeting was attended by Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif.

title: Saudi Arabia Confirms the Importance of Implementing Conventions to Ban Chemical Weapons
content: Ambassador Haimid called for a firm stance against those who commit crimes by using these chemical weapons, bringing them to international trial and promptly issuing the final report of the fact-finding mission on the use of chemical weapons in Syria. Addressing the 79th Session of the Executive Council of the Organization for the Prohibition of Chemical Weapons (OPCW) Saudi Ambassador to the Netherlands and Permanent Representative to OPCW Abdulaziz bin Abdullah Abu Haimid expressed the Kingdom's concern over international reports that the Syrian regime continuous to use chemical weapons against population. Saudi Arabia confirmed the importance of carrying out conventions to ban chemical weapons. It stated that it was among the first countries to accede to international treaties related to the ban of weapons of mass destruction and conventions to ban chemical weapons.

title: Kingdom Condemns Terrorist Attack on Egyptian Prosecutor General
content: The Minister stressed the Kingdom's stand by sisterly Egypt in combating these terrorist acts aiming to destabilize its security and stability. Minister Al-Jubeir expressed the Kingdom's condolences to the families of the deceased and victims, and the government and people of Egypt and wished a speedy recovery for the injured. The Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir has expressed the Kingdom???Ć?Ös strong condemnation and denunciation of the criminal terrorist attack on Egypt???Ć?Ös Prosecutor General Hisham Barakat which also killed and a number of innocent victims in a cowardly act contrary to the principles of Islam, all religions and human morals.

title: Saudi Soldier Killed, another Wounded from Shellfire
content: The Ministry of Interior announced today a Border Guard was killed and another injured from shellfire launched from Yemen. In its announcement, the ministry said that the two solders where on patrol when their vehicle can under attack around 9:00 am Saturday morning.

title: King, Crown Prince Offer Condolences to French President on the Lyon Terror Attack
content: Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif bin Abdulaziz, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior, sent a cable of condolence to the French president in which he condemned the terrorist attack and offers his condolence. Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz called French President Francois Hollande today to offer his condolences on yesterday???Ć?Ös terrorist attack at a chemical plant near Lyon. During their conversation, King Salman offered his condolences to the government, the family of the victim, and the French people.

title: King Salman Offers Condolences to Tunisian President on the Sousse Terror Attack
content: The King denounced and condemned the terrorist attack, calling it a criminal and coward act and offered condolence and consolation to the government, the families of the victims, and people of Tunisia. King Salman also expressed his wishes for the full and speedy recovery of all those injured. Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz this evening called Tunisian President Beji Caid Essebsi to offer his condolences on Friday???Ć?Ös terrorist attack on the Tunisian seaside resort of Sousse, which killed at least 38 people and wounded at least 39 others.

title: King Salman Center, World Food Program Discuss Aid Distribution in Yemen
content: General Supervisor the King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Center Dr. Abdullah Al-Rabeeah met today with the regional director of the World Food Program, Muhannad Hadi. During the meeting, the two officials stressed strategic partnership and coordination between the King Salman Center and World Food Program in the delivery of humanitarian aids to the Yemeni people.

title: Deputy Crown Prince Meets French Foreign Minister
content: Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, Second Deputy Premier and Minister of Defense met in Paris today with French Minister of Foreign Affairs Laurent Fabius. During the meeting, they reviewed bilateral relations between the two countries, and discussed the overall situations at regional and international arenas. The meeting was attended by Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir.

title: Minister Al-Jubeir: Kingdom Value and Appreciate Saudi-French Relations
content: Minister Al-Jubeir described Saudi-French relations as historic, strong and strategic. ???Ć?£We value the relationship so much and the exchange of visits by officials at a very high level reflect the extent of the status of these relationships and the open channels existing between them in consultation in all matters between the two countries,???Ć?Minister Al-Jubeir said. Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir described the Saudi-French relations as historic and strong . In a statement to Al-Ekhbariya News Channel today, the minister said the visit by Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense, to the French capital will witness the signing of a number of agreements.?é??

title: Interior Ministry Issues Travel Warning for Macedonia
content: The Ministry of Interior today issued a travel warning for Macedonia due to the deteriorating political and security situation in Macedonia. The Ministry of Interior urges Saudi citizens against travel to Macedonia.

title: GCC Foreign Ministerial Council to Hold Session in Riyadh on Thursday
content: The Council of Foreign Ministers of Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) will hold its 135th session in Riyadh on Thursday. On the meeting???Ć?Ös agenda is the strategic dialogues between the GCC and other regional intergovernmental unions. The meeting will also discuss the latest regional and international developments related to regional security and stability, and the situations in Yemen.

title: King Salman Holds Talks with Gabonese President
content: Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz met today with visiting President Ali Bongo Ondimba of Gabon at Al-Salam Palace in Riyadh. The meeting was attended by Prince Khalid Al-Faisal, Advisor to the King and Governor of Makkah Province; Prince Miteb bin Abdullah bin Abdulaziz, Minister of National Guard; Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif bin Abdulaziz, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior; Dr. Musaid bin Muhammad Al-Eiban, Minister of State; Minister of Culture and Information Dr. Adel bin Zaid Altoraifi; Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir; and Saudi Ambassador to Gabon Adnan Al-Mendeel.

title: Foreign Minister Attends Anti-ISIS Meeting in Paris
content: On the sidelines of the meeting, Minister Al-Jubeir held separate meetings with Iraqi Prime Minister Haider Al-Abadi, Australian Foreign Minister Julie Bishop, German Foreign Minister Frank-Walter Steinmeier, and Federica Mogherini, High Representative of the European Union for Foreign Affairs and Security Policy and Vice President of the European Commission. During the meetings, the meeting's agenda in addition to regional and international issues of common interest were discussed. Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmad Al-Jubeir today represented the Kingdom at the anti-Daesh (ISIS) group in Paris. The objective of the meeting was to discuss the coalition's strategy against ISIS, including training Iraqi soldiers and moderate groups in Syria.

title: Minister Al-Jubeir Holds Meeting with Egyptian Counterpart
content: With regard to terrorism, Al-Jubeir said that Saudi Arabia has taken strong stances against terrorism and extremism as well as their supporters in order to protect the Saudi people and the whole world. ???Ć?£We look forward today to building normal relations with Iran, but this depends on Iran's behavior, non-interference in the affairs of the region's countries, not supporting terrorism, and not taking actions that harm the interests of the region's countries. We will not be silent on Iran's interventions in the region,???Ć?Al-Jubeir said.
