Al-Jubeir confirmed that the meeting discussed foreign intervention in the Arab region, particularly the intervention by Iran as it is the only country that intervenes in the affairs of the region's countries, including Lebanon, Syria, Iraq or Yemen, stressing that the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia rejects negative acts carried out by Iran as well as Iran's support for terrorism. Minister Al-Jubeir said that Saudi Arabia's contacts with Russia match Egypt's, which aim at convincing Moscow to abandon Bashar Al-Assad or to use its influence in order to convince him to give up power as soon as possible. Al-Jubeir also said that Egypt is one of the first countries to participate in the Arab coalition and that coordination between the two countries on Yemen and Syria are ongoing. Regarding Yemen, Al-Jubeir addressed the efforts to continue political negotiations and to solve Yemeni crisis. The minister said that the Egypt and Saudi Arabia support efforts that aims at finding peaceful solution to the crisis and support any effort that increases humanitarian aid for the Yemeni people. Minister Al-Jubeir stressed that Saudi and Egyptian visions regarding Yemen and Syria are close to each other, adding that the two countries are seeking to remove Bashar Al-Assad from power after he lost his legitimacy, and are seeking to restore peace and stability in Syria. Minister Al-Jubeir also said that the two countries are seeking to protect the government and military institutions in Syria to be able to deal with the challenges in a post-Assad regime Syria. During a joint press conference following their meeting, the ministers said there is no daylight between them with respect to Yemen and Syria and agree to continue bilateral coordination at various levels to enhance bilateral relations in a way that serves the interest of the two countries and their peoples. Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir met in Cairo today with Egyptian Minister of Foreign Affairs Sameh Shoukry. During the meeting, they discussed means of enhancing bilateral relations in various fields and reviewed a number of regional and international issues of common interest, particularly the situation in Syria and Yemen.

title: One Soldier Killed, Seven Injured from Shellfire from Yemen
content: A security spokesman of the Ministry of Interior announced today that a border guard was killed and another seven injured today while on duty at Al-Harth sector in Jazan region after their position was hit by shells from Yemen. Saudi Land Forces responded to the source of the shelling.

title: Foreign Minister Al-Jubeir Meets with his Yemeni Counterpart
content: Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir met today in Riyadh with Dr. Riyadh Yassin, the Foreign Minister of Yemen. During their meeting, they discussed the latest developments and Yemen-related issues. The meeting was attended by Prince Torki bin Mohammed bin Saud Al-Kabeer, Undersecretary of the Foreign Affairs for Multilateral Relations.

title: Deputy Crown Prince Concludes Visit to Kuwait
content: Prince Mohammed bin Salman was received at the airport in Kuwait by Pime Minister Sheikh Jaber Al-Mubarak Al-Sabah, First Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Sheikh Sabah Al-Khalid Al-Sabah, Deputy Prime Minister and Interior Minister Sheikh Mohammed Al-Khaled Al-Sabah, and Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense Sheikh Khalid Al-Jarrah Al-Sabah. Prince Mohammed bin Salman???Ć?Ös delegation included Minister of Culture and Information Dr. Adel bin Zaid Altoraifi and Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir. During his visit, Prince Mohammed met with Sheikh Sabah Al-Ahmed Al-Jaber Al-Sabah, the Emir of Kuwait. The meeting was attended by Crown Prince Sheikh Nawaf Al-Jaber Al-Sabah. During the meeting, they discussed bilateral relations and regional and international developments. Deputy Crown Prince and Minister of Defense Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz arrived in Kuwait today on a one day official visit.

title: King Receives GCC Leaders, Heads of Delegations and French President
content: The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz received the leaders and heads of delegations of the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) and French President Francois Hollande at Al-Dir'iya Palace before the start of the 15th Consultative Meeting of the GCC leaders.

title: Deputy Crown Prince Receives French President
content: Deputy Crown Prince Mohammad bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense received at Al-Dir???Ć?Öiya Conference Palace today French President Francois Hollande. During the meeting, they reviewed bilateral relations in addition to the latest developments in the Middle East. The meeting was attended by the Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir, French Minister of Foreign Affairs and International Development Laurent Fabius; Minister of Defense Jean-Yves Le Drian, and the French Ambassador to Saudi Arabia Bertrand Besancenot.

title: Kingdom Participates in Eager Lion 2015 Military Exercise in Jordan
content: In addition to Saudi Arabia, other participants include Qatar, Kuwait, Bahrain, Iraq, the United Arab Emirates, Egypt, Lebanon, Jordan, Britain, France, Italy, Pakistan, the United States, Canada, Belgium, Poland, Australia, and the command of NATO are participating in the exercise which lasts until May 19. The military exercise aims to promote cooperation and joint action among the participating countries, build their capacity, enhance their readiness and enable them to manage various crises and deal with them. The military exercise Eager Lion 2015 began today in Jordan with the participation of ten thousand soldiers from 18 Arab and NATO members, including Saudi Arabia.

title: Lebanese Prime Minister Commends Generous Support Provided by the Kingdom
content: He added: "We say thank you to the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz and other leaders of the Gulf Cooperation Council countries for all what they have provided to us over the years.???Ć?He added: "Yes, on behalf of our people, hundreds of thousands of Lebanese residents in the Arab Gulf states, all our armed forces, which began receiving new weapons and equipment, I thank the Saudi gift." Lebanese Prime Minister Tammam Salam commended the generous support provided by the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz to Lebanon to help it meet the challenges in the political and economic spheres. In a speech during the opening session of the 23rd Arab Economic Forum held today in Beirut, Prime Minister Salam said, "the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia has stood by our side and is still hosting our citizens and still supporting our stability and institutions."

title: Crown Prince Meets with French President
content: The meeting was attended by Minister of State and Cabinet's Member Dr. Saad bin Khalid Al-Jabri; Minister of Culture and Information Dr. Adel bin Zaid Altoraifi; Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir; Chief of General Intelligence Khalid bin Ali Al-Humaidan; Director General of Investigation General Abdulaziz bin Mohammed Al-Hwaireini; and Saudi Acting Charge' D???Ć?Öaffaires to the France Dr. Ali bin Mohammed Al-Qarni. Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif bin Abdulaziz, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior met with French President Francois Hollande at the King Saud Guest Palace today. During the meeting, they discussed bilateral relations and ways to enhance the relationship, especially with regard to anti-terrorism cooperation, and the latest regional and international developments.

title: President Francois Hollande Meets with Former Lebanese Prime Minister in Riyadh
content: President Francois Hollande of the French Republic, currently on a visit to the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, received at his residence here today former Lebanese Prime Minister Saad Rafiq Al-Hariri. During the meeting, they discussed cordial relationships between the two friendly countries. The meeting was attended by the Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir.

title: GCC Leaders Arrived in Riyadh for 15th Consultative Meeting
content: The leaders and heads of delegations of Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) arrived in Riyadh today to attend the 15th Consultative Meeting of the GCC leaders. Participants included Sayyid Shihab bin Tariq Al Said, Representative of Sultan Qaboos bin Said of Oman; Sheikh Mohammed bin Rashid Al Maktoum, Vice President of the United Arab Emirates, Prime Minister and Ruler of Dubai; Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad bin Khalifa Al-Thani, Emir of the Qatar; Sheikh Sabah Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah, Emir of Kuwait; and King Hamad bin Isa Al-Khalifa of Bahrain.

title: Saudi Arabia to Establish Unified Coordination Relief Center for Yemen
content: Mr. Al-Jubeir warned that Houthi militias and forces loyal to former Yemen President Ali Abdullah Saleh will try to exploit the ceasefire and prevent the people of Yemen from receiving aid. He reiterated that Saudi Arabia will respond to any violations of this ceasefire by resuming air attacks targeted at Houthi militia groups. Minister Al-Jubeir said that Saudi Arabia is consulting with coalition members and all countries supporting the coalition???Ć?Ös efforts in Yemen in order to determine specific areas in Yemen where humanitarian aid to be delivered. The foreign minister added that all air operations would cease at specific times in these areas to allow the delivery of relief supplies in accordance with UN Security Council Resolution 2216 (2015). Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir announced today that Saudi Arabia and its coalition partners are in the process of establishing a unified center to coordinate relief efforts for Yemen that would enable United Nations delivery of $274 million in humanitarian aid donated by Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz on April 17, 2015 and in accordance with the framework of continuing and enhancing humanitarian relief efforts in Yemen.

title: French President Hollande Arrives in Riyadh
content: French President Francois Hollande arrived in Riyadh today on an official visit to the Kingdom. He was received King Khaled International Airport by Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz, Governor of Riyadh Prince Faisal bin Bandar bin Abdulaziz, Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif bin Abdulaziz, Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, and Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir.

title: Foreign Minister Al-Jubeir Meets with French Foreign Minister Fabius
content: Minister Fabius is in the Kingdom part of President Hollande???Ć?Ös delegation. Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir met today with visiting French Minister of Foreign Affairs and International Development Laurent Fabius. During the meeting, they exchanged cordial talks and discussed the developments on Arab, Islamic and international arenas as well as means of enhancing bilateral relations.

title: Minister of Defense Receives Message from French Counterpart
content: Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, the Minister of Defense, Head of the Royal Court and Special Advisor to the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques, received today a message from his French counterpart Jean-Yves Le Drien. The message was delivered by French Ambassador to the Kingdom Bertrand Bisanceau. The meeting was attended by the Director General of the Minister of Defense Office and the French Military Attach?ā?? to the Kingdom Bertrand De Lanyard.

title: Coalition Announces Success of Operation Decisive Storm and End to Airstrikes
content: The Ministry of Defense appeals to Allah Almighty to preserve the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz, the Supreme Commander of Armed Forces, and to maintain the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia's security and stability. Since the beginning of the Operation Deceive Storm, the Saudi Armed Forces distinguished itself in battle efficiently and capably, resulting in imposing air control to prevent any hostility against the Kingdom and countries of the region. The Ministry of Defense announces that the airstrikes, with participation of the Saudi brave hawks with brothers in the coalition countries, have successfully managed to thwart the threat on the security of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and neighboring countries through the destruction of the heavy weapons and ballistic missiles seized by the Houthi militias and troops loyal to former president Ali Abdullah Saleh, including bases and camps of the Yemeni army. The Saudi Ministry of Defense issued the following statement today:

title: King Salman Receives Former Lebanese Prime Minister
content: The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz received at Al-Yamamah Palace today, former Prime Minister of Lebanon Saad Al-Hariri. During the meeting, Al-Hariri expressed his thanks to King Salman for the Kingdom's keenness on Lebanon's security and stability as well as its support for the Lebanese Army to increase its preparedness to confront terrorism and defend the country's borders. He also thanked King Salman on the occasion of the Lebanese Army's receiving of its first military aid within military assistance worth $3 billion.

title: Border Guard Killed along Border with Yemen
content: A Border Guard officer was killed and two others were wounded from gunfire and mortar shells fired from inside Yemen late Sunday night, the Ministry of Interior announced. The martyred soldier was identified as Private 1st Class Saleh Mania Mohammed Al-Muhamedh. He wad killed by while manning the A???Ć?Ödam outpost in Najran Sector.

title: King Salman Receives Tony Blair
content: The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz received at Al-Yamamah Palace today Special Envoy of the International Quartet for Middle East Peace, Tony Blair. During the meeting, they discussed the developments of the Palestinian issue, and the Quartet???Ć?Ös efforts in achieving a just and comprehensive peace in the region. The meeting was attended by Crown Prince and Deputy Prime Minister Prince Muqrin bin Abdulaziz, Deputy Foreign Minister Abdulaziz bin Abdullah bin Abdulaziz, and Minister of State Dr. Musaid bin Muhammad Al-Eiban.

title: Operation Decision Storm will Restore Stability in Yemen
content: For Operation Decisive Storm updates, please visit?é?? . The video of the press conference can be viewed  The ambassador stressed that the coalition forces have been very cautious in targeting military targets and minimizing civilian damage. He said several times that reports that Saudi-led airstrikes have hit civilian targets are false. "The Houthis have no choice but to give up their quest to take over Yemen by force because they will fail. We will make sure of this," said Amb. Al-Jubeir. "There is no circumstance under which the Houthis and former president Ali Abdullah Saleh will prevail in any way, shape or form in Yemen."?é?? The ambassador commended the UN Security Council for passing a resolution barring the Houthis from receiving any outside weapons or aid. "We're very satisfied with the resolution that was passed, as you know, 14-0," he said. "The resolution we see as a victory for Yemen and for the Yemeni people and for the legitimate government." The ambassador reiterated Saudi Arabia's support for the people of Yemen and their legitimate government. "Our objective is to ensure that Yemen can become a prosperous, thriving, safe, secure country in which all Yemenis, irrespective of their ethnicity or their religious background can live as equal citizens, including the Houthis," the ambassador said. "We seek nothing but good for Yemen and for the Houthis, and that for them to achieve their objectives, they really should accept the political process." The ambassador reiterated Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz's statement that Operation Decisive Storm will continue until its objectives are achieved. He said the operation has seen tremendous results in just three weeks. The coalition has cut off the Houthis from receiving any supplies or reinforcements, causing them to scatter and disband. Saudi Ambassador to the United States Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir said today that military operations in Yemen have been successful in thwarting the Houthi militia's goal of taking over Yemen. He provided an update at a press conference at the Saudi Embassy. "Operation Decisive Storm to support legitimacy in Yemen is very successful and exceeded its set goals," stated Amb. Al-Jubeir. "The popular committees in the south have been increasingly more successful, as they have been receiving arms from the coalition and they have been able to push back against the Houthis in a number of areas... We are also beginning to see cracks in the Houthi military structure. We're seeing commanders defect back to the regular Yemeni military."?é??

title: Two Saudi Border Guard Killed
content: The exchange of fire resulted in the martyrdom of First Private Mohammed bin Hamoud bin Mohammed Al-Harbi and Private Abdurrahman bin Meeri bin Mohammed Al-Qahtani. The Ministry of Interior announced today that two Border Guards was killed as a result of heavy fire emanating from Yemen. The ministry said that on Friday at about 4pm the Alhosen Border Post in Abha came under heavy gunfire from Yemen. The Border Guards returned fire.

title: King Salman: Yemen Military Campaign to Continue until Goals are Achieved
content:
King Salman called on the Arab world to unite against terrorism while addressing other conflicts ???Ć?Äacross the region, including Palestine, Syria, and Libya. He stated, ???Ć?£The scourge of extremism ???Ć?Äand terrorism are among the challenges facing the Arab nation, targeting the security and stability ???Ć?Äof our countries, and requiring us to be vigilant and be in solidarity when taking necessary actions ???Ć?Äto confront and eradicate its roots.???Ć????Ć?
???Ć?£Riyadh opens its doors to all Yemeni political parties committed to Yemen's security and ???Ć?Ästability, to meet under the umbrella of GCC and within the context of maintaining the ???Ć?Älegitimacy, rejection of any legitimacy breaching, ensuring the return of the State control over all ???Ć?ÄYemeni territory, returning to the State all weapons, and holding no threat the security of ???Ć?Äneighboring States,???Ć?said King Salman. ???Ć?£Operation Decisive Storm will continue until these ???Ć?Äobjectives are achieved, so that the Yemeni people are able to enjoy security and stability.???Ć?Ø???Ć?
???Ć?
???Ć?£The excruciating reality of terrorism, internal conflicts and bloodshed experienced by a number ???Ć?Äof Arab countries are only the inevitable result of an alliance between terrorism and sectarianism, ???Ć?Äwhich is led by regional powers whose blatant interventions in the Arab region have resulted in ???Ć?Äundermining security and stability in some of our countries,???Ć?said King Salman. ???Ć?£In Yemen, the ???Ć?Äforeign intervention enabled the Houthi militias to overtake the legitimate authority, occupy ???Ć?ÄSana???Ć?Öa, and disrupt the implementation of the Gulf Cooperation Council Initiative, which has ???Ć?Äsought to preserve Yemen's unity, stability and security.???Ć?Ø???Ć?
???Ć?The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz today vowed that the ???Ć?ÄSaudi-led coalition against Houthi rebels in Yemen will continue until the country is stable and ???Ć?Äsafe, blaming foreign intervention for the unrest. The comments came during a speech at the 26th ???Ć?ÄArab League Summit in Sharm El-Sheikh, Egypt.???Ć?

title: Saudi Navy Evacuates Embassy Staff from Yemen
content: The Royal Saudi Navy Forces on Wednesday evacuated the staff of Saudi Embassy in Yemen and Saudi ???Ć?ÄConsulate in Aden in addition to a number of foreign diplomatic missions in Yemen. A total of 86 diplomats were ???Ć?Äevacuated by two navy ships of the coast of Aden. The evacuees arrived early today at King Faisal Naval Base in ???Ć?ÄJeddah.???Ć?

title: King Salman Meets with Egyptian and Yemeni Presidents
content: Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz met today with Egyptian President Abdel ???Ć?ÄFattah El Sisi and Yemini President Abdrabu Mansur Hadi on the sidelines of the 26th Arab ???Ć?ÄLeague Summit in Sharm El-Sheikh, Egypt.

title: King Salman Returns to Riyadh with President of Yemen
content: The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz returned to Riyadh following his participating in the 26th summit of the Arab League which was held in Sharm el-Sheikh, Egypt. King Salman was accompanied by ???Ć?ÄYemeni President Abdrabu Mansur Had.

title: King Salman Held Telephone Consultations with World Leaders
content: King Salman spoke with King Felipe VI of Spain, King Mohammed VI of Morocco, King Abdullah II of Jordan, King Hamad bin Isa Al-Khalifa of Bahrain, Emir of Qatar Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al-Thani, Emir of Kuwait Sheikh Sabah Al-Ahmed Al-Jaber Al-Sabah, French ???Ć?ÄPresident Francois Hollande, US President Barak Obama, Egyptian President Abdul Fattah El-Sisi, Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan, Sudanese President Omar Al-Bashir, Pakistani Prime Minister Nawaz Sharif, British Prime Minister David Cameron, Spanish Prime Minister Mariano Rajoy, ???Ć?ÄGerman Chancellor Angela Merkel, and Crown Prince of Abu Dhabi and Deputy Supreme Commander of the United Arab Emirates Armed Forces Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al-Nahyan. Over the past three days, Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz held telephone ???Ć?Äconsultations with a number of regional and world leaders where they discussed, amount other things, Operation ???Ć?ÄDecisive Storm against Houthis rebels in Yemen. During the telephone calls, the leaders expressed full support for ???Ć?Äthe Kingdom's actions and expressed readiness to provide any support needed by Saudi Arabia.???Ć?

title: Yemeni President Arrives in Riyadh
content: President Abdrabu Mansur Hadi of Yemen arrived in Riyadh today. President Hadi was received at Riyadh Airbase by Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, Minister of Defense, President of the Royal Court and Special Advisor to the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques; and Chief of General Intelligence Khalid bin Ali Al-Humaidan.

title: Saudi Arabia Launches Military Operations in Support of Legitimate Yemeni Government
content: ???Ć?£May God Almighty protect the brave soldiers and may He grant them success in their noble mission,???Ć?Al-Jubeir said. Based on the appeal from President Hadi, and based on the Kingdom???Ć?Ös responsibility to Yemen and its people, Saudi Arabia, ???Ć?£along with its allies within the GCC and outside the GCC, launched military operations in support of the people of Yemen and their legitimate government.???Ć?Al-Jubeir said that in letter dated March 7, 2015, President Hadi of Yemen made a request of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz to convene a conference under the auspices of the GCC to which all Yemeni political factions seeking to preserve security and stability in Yemen would be invited. ???Ć?£The Houthis rejected this invitation and continued their violent onslaught in Yemen to the point where they were threatening to occupy the city of Aden, which had become the temporary capital for the legitimate government of President Hadi after he was able to escape from Sana???Ć?Öa. In a letter, dated March 24, 2015, President Hadi requested, based on the principle of self-defense, enshrined in Article 51 of the U.N. Charter, as well as in the Arab League charter???Ć?Ös collective defense mechanism, a request for immediate support ???Ć?by all means necessary ???Ć?including military intervention to protect Yemen and its people from the continued Houthi aggression and to support it in fighting al Qaeda and ISIL.???Ć?The ambassador added that ???Ć?£in spite of repeated efforts by the GCC, G10 countries and the Special Representative of the U.N. Secretary General to seek a peaceful way to implement the GCC initiatives and the outcomes of the national dialogue that define the political transition in Yemen, the Houthis have reneged on every single agreement they have made and continue their quest to take over the country by violent means. They captured the capital city of Sana???Ć?Öa, they placed the legitimate president, prime minister and cabinet members under house arrest, they seized the security services and they continue to expand their occupation of the country.???Ć????Ć?£The operation,???Ć?Ambassador Al-Jubeir said, ???Ć?£will be limited in nature, and designed to protect the people of Yemen and its legitimate government from a takeover by the Houthis. A violent extremist militia.???Ć?Saudi Arabia has launched military operations in Yemen, as part of a coalition of over ten countries in response to a direct request from the legitimate government of Yemen, Saudi Ambassador to the United States Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir announced at a press conference at the embassy in Washington this evening.

title: Crown Prince Receives British Foreign Secretary Hammond
content: Crown Prince Muqrin bin Abdulaziz, Deputy Prime Minister received at Al-Yamamah Palace today British Foreign Secretary Philip Hammond and his accompanying delegation. During the meeting, they reviewed bilateral relations and cooperation between the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and Britain, and discussed latest developments at regional and international arenas and the positions of the two countries towards them.

title: King Salman Hold Meeting with British Foreign Secretary Hammond
content: Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz received at Al-Yamamah Palace today British Foreign Secretary Philip Hammond. During their meeting, they reviewed aspects of bilateral relations between the two countries and ways of enhancing them, and discussed the latest developments of regional and international situations

title: King Salman, Crown Prince Muqrin Condole Iranian President on Death of his Mother
content: Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz and Crown Prince Muqrin in Abdulaziz today sent separate cables of condolences to Iranian President Dr. Hassan Rouhani on the death of his mother. In their cables, King Salman and Crown Prince Murqin said expressed their ???Ć?£deep condolences and sincere sympathy, asking Allah Almighty to bestow His mercy and forgiveness upon her soul."

title: Prince Saud: Iran Should not get an Undeserved Deal
content: Foreign Minister Prince Saud Al-Faisal said today that efforts to resolve the Iranian nuclear issue through peaceful means is possible, however, Iran should not get an "undeserved deal.???Ć?During a joint press conference with British Foreign Secretary Philip Hammond in Riyadh, Prince Saud stated, a peaceful solution to Iran???Ć?Ös nuclear program is ???Ć?£possible through the League of Arab States to make the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) and the Middle East region free of weapons of mass destruction and that in return, it is not possible to grant Iran an undeserved deal.???Ć?

title: Prince Saud Meets UK Foreign Secretary Philip Hammond
content: The meeting was attended by Vice Foreign Minister Prince Abdulaziz bin Abdullah bin Abdulaziz, Undersecretary of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs for Multilateral Relations Prince Dr. Torki bin Mohammed bin Saud Al Kabeer, Undersecretary of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs for Information and Technology Prince Ambassador Mohammed bin Saud bin Khalid, Minister of State for Foreign Affairs Dr. Nizar Madani, Undersecretary of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs for Bilateral Relations Dr. Khalid Al-Gandan, Undersecretary of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs for Economic and Cultural Affairs Dr. Yousef Al-Sadoun, and Director of Media Department at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs Ambassador Osama Nugali. Other topics at the forefront of regional issues addressed in the press conference included the tragedy in Syria, the Palestinian-Israeli conflict, strengthening Saudi-Iraqi relations and joint efforts within the international coalition to fight Daaesh ISIS. On the escalating crisis in Yemen, Prince Saud reiterated support to the Yemeni government and stressed the importance of the call by President Abdrabu Mansur Hadi to hold a conference in Riyadh under the GCC umbrella and attended by all factors of Yemen who are interested in maintaining security and stability. ???Ć?£Our aim is to provide the vehicle for the president to return peacefully to Yemen and provide the leadership as required to bring this country back,???Ć?he stated. Foreign Minister Prince Saud Al-Faisal met today with British Foreign Secretary Philip Hammond in Riyadh. During their meeting, they discussed bilateral relations as well as international and regional issues of common interest. While discussing efforts to resolve the Iranian nuclear issue through peaceful means, Prince Saud stated, ???Ć?£[solution] is possible through the League of Arab States to make the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) and the Middle East region free of weapons of mass destruction and that in return, it is not possible to grant Iran an undeserved deal.???Ć?

title: Saudi Health Centers Provide Medical Assistance to over 2,600 Patients a Week in Zaatari Camp
content: There are currently 13 ???Ć?Äspecialized health centers operating in the camp and are part of the Kingdom???Ć?Ös national campaign at ???Ć?Äsupporting the Syrian refugees and to provide health care to the camp residents. Specialized health centers ???Ć?Äat the Zaatari camp comprise 13 medical clinics including specialized cardiology, dermatology and surgery ???Ć?Äunits. The Saudi National Campaign to Support Syrian Brothers funded medical centers in Zaatari refugee camp in Jordan treats 2,629 Syrian patients per week, Medical Director of the Saudi Specialized Clinics Dr. Muhammad Ismail Zoubi said today.

title: King Salman Receives Telephone Call from Iraqi Prime Minister
content: Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz received a telephone call today from Iraq's Prime Minister Haydar Al-Abadi. During their conversation, they discussed bilateral relations, issues of common interest and developments on regional and international arenas

title: Kingdom Calls for Bringing Syrian War Criminals to International Justice
content: Ambassador Trad reiterated Kingdom???Ć?Ös commitment to the sovereignty, independence, national unity and territorial integrity of Syria, and is committed to the implementation of Geneva I (30 June 2012), which aims at achieving a peaceful transition of power in Syria. Ambassador Trad reiterated the Kingdom???Ć?Ös stance towards the finding of the Independent International Commission of Inquiry on Syria which criticized the foreign military support the Syrian regime receives from Lebanese Hezbollah, the Badr Brigade, Abu al-Fadl al-Abbas Brigade, the Iranian Revolutionary Guards and extremist militias. Saudi Arabia agrees with the Commission's report that restricting support for the Syrian moderate opposition has allowed the emergence of extremist organizations such as Daesh (ISIS), al-Nusra Front and al-Qaeda. Ambassador Thad said that the failure of the international community to take a decisive stand end to the brutality of the ongoing crimes of the Al-Assad regime is the main reason for the emergence of armed terrorist groups that found Syria a fertile environment and whose crimes are no different than those the Syrian regime has been committing, Ambassador Trad said. The Organization for the Prohibition of Chemical Weapons' fact-finding mission concluded that the Al-Assad's regime used chemical weapons in three villages in northern Syria, in addition to the use of barrel bombs. Saudi Arabia's Ambassador to the U.N. in Geneva Faisal Trad said that during the nearly four-year Syrian crisis, the Syrian people have suffered the most heinous crimes. Ambassador Trad added that the Bashar Al-Assad's regime lost legitimacy and is still waging indiscriminately brutal attacks that have claimed more than 221,434 of his own people - mostly children, women and elderly - and displaced more than half of the population. Ambassador Trad stressed that this regime has gone too far in brutality as it used internationally banned weapons (chlorine gas).?é?? Saudi Arabia today has called for bringing Syrian war criminals to international justice and making public the names of those suspected of involvement war crimes since the outbreak of the Syrian revolution.

title: Kingdom Demands End to Government Discrimination Against Rohingya
content: The Kingdom demanded the government of Myanmar to do more to promote greater understanding and amicability among the country???Ć?Ös different communities in the country through activating the memorandum of understanding (MoU) signed with the Jeddah-based Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) as well as plans to provide infrastructure development assistance to Myanmar and specifically to the Rohingya region. The Kingdom has already donated $50 million to this effect. The Kingdom's representative to the UN Human Rights Commission, Abdulaziz bin Ali Al-Aqla, said that institutionalized discrimination against the Rohingya Muslim minority could lead to mistrust and undermine efforts to enhance dialogue and understanding among peoples on the other. Abdulaziz bin Ali Al-Aqla, member of the Saudi Human Rights Commission and member of Saudi Arabia's delegation to the UN Human Rights Council, said in a speech before the UN Human Rights Council in Geneva that, after having careful review of the UN report on the situation of human rights in Myanmar, citing that the internationally-authorized writer of the report has not noticed any improvement in the situation of the displaced Rohingya minority in Myanmar compared with her previous visit to the country last July following allegations that they were mistreated by the Buddhist majority in the Myanmar State of Rakhine, the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia suggests that this tension would lead to escalation of violence, killings and horror and that the Muslim minority would be the biggest loser because of the decision issued by Myanmar authority depriving all holders of temporary cards from their rights as most of the Rohingya are non-nationality holders. Saudi Arabia today expressed regret for the continuing wide-spread systematic violence in Myanmar, particularly against the Rohingya Muslim minority according to the Special Rapporteur on the situation of human rights in Myanmar, Ms. Yanghee Lee.

title: King Holds Meeting with Afghan President
content: Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz held official talks at his palace today with Afghanistan's President Dr. Ashraf Ghani Ahmadzai. The talks dealt with bilateral relations between the two brotherly countries as well as the latest developments on the Islamic and international arenas. President Ahmadzai arrived earlier in the day in Riyadh.

title: Kingdom Recalls Ambassador to Stockholm
content: An official source at the Foreign Ministry said the move is in response to the offensive remarks made by Swedish Foreign Minister Margot Wallstrom which are a blatant interference in the Kingdom???Ć?Ös internal affairs. Saudi Arabia considers her remarks as contrary to international conventions and diplomatic norms and do not conform with the friendly relations between the countries. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs announced today that due to the offensive remarks made by Sweden???Ć?Ös Foreign Minister against the Kingdom, the Saudi Arabia has recalled its ambassador to Sweden.

title: King Salman Holds Talks with Turkish President
content: The official delegation accompanying President Erdogan included Deputy Prime Minister Noman Kortellmh, Deputy Prime Minister Yalcin Akdogan, Minister of Foreign Affairs Mevlut Cavusoglu, Minister of Economy Nihat Zeybekci, and Deputy Chairman of Development and Justice Party Yassin Oktay. The Turkish President arrived in Riyadh earlier today and was received at King Khalid International Airport by Crown Prince Muqrin bin Abdulaziz, Deputy Prime Minster; Prince Faisal bin Bandar bin Abdulaziz, Governor of Riyadh; and Prince Mohammed bin Naif bin Abdulaziz, Deputy Crown Prince, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior. Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz held today official talks session at his palace here today with Turkey's President Recep Tayyip Erdogan. During their talks, they discussed means of enhancing bilateral cooperation in various fields, issues of common interest and the developments on regional and international arenas.

title: Voter Registration Commences
content: Voter registration began in Makkah and Madinah on August 16, a week ahead of national due to the arrival of pilgrims in the two holy cities. The third municipal elections are scheduled for December 12. Since the last elections, several changes have been made, including increasing the number of elected municipal council members from half to two-third of the total number of members; reduced the voting age from 21 to 18 years; and granting women the right to vote and run for office.

title: Vice Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Chairs Cabinet's Session
content: Minister of Social Affairs and Acting Minister of Culture and Information Dr. Majed bin Abdullah Al-Qasabi said following the cabinet meeting that the Council of Ministers was briefed on the topics on its agenda, including topics in which the Shura Council participated in their study, and on the reports of both its General Committee and Experts' Bureau regarding them, and decided the following: approved Shura Council's Wildlife Protected Areas Regulation; approved the conclusions of the 25th Universal Postal Congress, including amendments to the general system of the Universal Postal Union, the Universal Postal Convention, the final protocol, and the agreement on postal payment services; authorized the Minister of Foreign Affairs, or his deputy, to negotiate and sign a draft general agreement for cooperation with the Ivory Coast; approved the accession of the Kingdom to the Protocol to Eliminate Illicit Trade in Tobacco Products; and approved a number of measures to protect beaches and coastlines. The Cabinet also praised the humanitarian truce declared by the coalition command in Yemen to allow the delivery and distribution of humanitarian assistance to the Yemeni people. The Cabinet expressed appreciation to the King Salman for his directives to provide urgent assistance to the Yemeni people and initiation of an airlift to transport humanitarian relief assistance provided by King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Center. The Cabinet expressed the Kingdom's condemnation and denounced the Israeli occupation forces and Jewish extremists' desecration Al-Aqsa Mosque, including preventing Muslim worshipers from entering the mosque and assaulting worshipers inside the mosque. The Kingdom appeared to the international community to fulfill its responsibilities and take the necessary measures to protect the Palestinian people from Israeli aggressive practices. The Cabinet reiterated the Kingdom's condemnation and denunciation of the heinous terrorist crime carried out by Israeli settlers in the Palestinian village of Douma in the West Bank, burning to death a toddler and severely burning his parents and 4 year old brother. The Cabinet was briefed on Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense to Egypt and his meeting with the President Abdel Fattah Al-Sisit. The Cabinet praised the Cairo Declaration as a confirmation of the deep and close relationship between the two countries and their determination to enhance security and stability in the region and working together on the protection of Arab national security. The Cairo Declaration is also a rejection of attempts to interfere in the internal affairs of Arab countries. The Vice Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Prince Mohammad bin Naif bin Abdulaziz chaired the Cabinet's weekly session at Al-Salam Palace in Jeddah this afternoon. The Vice Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques began the session by expressing his thanks to the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz for his continuing efforts in the service of the Two Holy Mosques and their visitors.

title: King Leaves Kingdom on a Private Vacation; Crown Prince to Run the Country
content: The Royal Court announced today that the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz will travel outside the Kingdom on a private vacation today and that Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif, will administer the affairs of the state during the King???Ć?Ös absence.

title: Makkah Governor Inaugurates Exhibit on King Faisal
content: Prince Khalid Al-Faisal, Advisor to the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques and Governor of Makkah Region, inaugurates ???Ć?£Faisal: A Witness and Martyr'' exhibition in Taif today. The exhibition includes various aspects of the late King Faisal's life displayed though photos, videos and documents.

title: King Salman Appoints New Judges
content: The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz has issued a royal order appointing and promoting 25 judges at the Board of Grievances and 100 judges at the Ministry of Justice at various ranks.

title: Royal Order to Appoint New Housing Minister
content: A royal order was issued today to appoint Majed bin Abdullah bin Hamad Al-Hogail, as new Minister of Housing.

title: Chief of the Royal Court Relieved of his Post
content: A royal order was issued today to relieve Hamad bin Abdulaziz Al-Suwailem of his post as chief of the Royal Court and named Khalid bin Abdulrahman Al-Issa, the Minister of State and Member of the Cabinet, was as Acting Chief of Royal Court, effect immediately.

title: Prince Mishaal bin Abdullah bin Juluwi Appointed Governor of Northern Border
content: The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz issued today a royal decree relieving Prince Mishaal bin Abdullah bin Abdulaziz bin Musaed bin Juluwi of his post as Advisor to the King and appointing him Governor of Northern Border at the rank of minister.

title: King Salman Performs Funeral Prayer for Prince Saud Al-Faisal
content: Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz today performed the funeral prayers for Prince Saud Al-Faisal at the Grand Mosque in Makkah. Following the funeral prayer, King Salman and the brothers and sons of Prince Saud received condolences. Prince Saud was buried at Al-Adel Cemetery in Makkah.

title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet Session
content: The Council also offered its condolences to the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques, the Crown Prince, the Deputy Crown Prince and the royal family on the death of Prince Abdullah bin Abdulaziz bin Musaed bin Jelawi Al Saud, the Governor of Northern Border Region and to his brothers and sons, praying to Allah Almighty to bestow His mercy upon the deceased soul. The Council also reviewed a number of topics on local affairs and thanked the King for disbursing more than one billion and eight hundred million Saudi Riyals for the social insurance beneficiaries, outside of Zakat finances, as aid during the holy month of Ramadan. The Cabinet welcomed the statement issued by the emergency meeting of the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) Ministers of Interior held July 2 in Kuwait in which the ministers confirmation that the safety and security of GCC peoples are an integral part of the GCC???Ć?Ös mission. The Cabinet also re-emphasized the Kingdom's condemnation of the terrorist attacks on military checkpoints in Egypt's North Sinai July 1 in which dozens of people were killed, including civilians. The Cabinet expressed the Kingdom's condolences to the families of the deceased and victims, and the government and people of Egypt and wishing the injured a speedy recovery. Following the meeting, Minister of Culture and Information Dr. Adel Al-Toraifi said that the Council of Ministers was briefed on the message sent by King Salman to King Abdullah II bin Al-Hussein of Jordan and the latest regional and international developments. King Salman also requested from all governmental and local authorities to double all efforts during the holy month of Ramadan to well serve all pilgrims and visitors, by improving the services and infrastructure of all the holy sites. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman Bin Abdulaziz, chaired the Council of Minister???Ć?Ös weekly meeting at Al-Safa Palace in Makkah this evening. At the beginning of the meeting, King Salman expressed his gratefulness to God Almighty for the influx of visitors to the holy cities of Makkah and Madinah to perform Umrah during the blessed month of Ramadan. King Salman also expressed his gratitude for the services rendered to the visitors and for the various projects being undertaken around the clock to expand of the two holy mosques and other related projects.

title: Royal Court Announces Death of Governor of the Northern Border Province
content: The Royal Court today announced that Prince Abdullah bin Musaid bin Jalawi, Governor of the Northern Border Province, has passed away. Funeral prayers for Prince Abdullah will be held tomorrow following Isha (night) prayers at the Grand Mosque in Makkah.

title: Kingdom Condemns Unfounded Statements Regarding the Raef Badawi Case
content: The Foreign Ministry stresses that the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia totally rejects any form of interference in its internal affairs and any impingement on its sovereignty or the independence and impartiality of its judiciary. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs today condemned statements by some countries and international organizations regarding the case of Raef Badawi before a final ruling in the case has been issued by either the judiciary or any official body.

title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet Session
content: The Council of Ministers approved the application of a number of decisions of the Supreme Council of the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) issued in its 35th session, held in Doha earlier his year, including the adoption of the updated plan for non-communicable disease prevention and control; continuing work as a guideline with all the unified rules and principles for the integration of financial markets in the GCC which had already been adopted by the Supreme Council until the completion of the unified rules system for the full integration of the financial markets of the Council's states, and ensuring harmonization and compatibility with each other; adoption of the GCC Declaration on Human Right; adoption of the GCC Unified Food System as a guideline for two years; and approval of the outcome of the GCC Customs Union Commission with respect to some requirements of the final phase of the customs union. Minister of Culture and Information Dr. Adel bin Zaid Altoraifi said following the meeting that the Cabinet took the following decisions: approved an amendment to the wildlife trafficking regulations that would require the names of offenders to be published in local newspapers; authorized the president of King Abdulaziz City for Science and Technology, or his deputy, to discuss with the French side a draft memorandum of understanding for scientific and technical cooperation, sign it and submit the final version for further regular procedures; authorized the Acting Minister of Housing, or his deputy, to discuss with the Russian side a draft memorandum of understanding for cooperation in the field of housing, sign it, and submit the final version for further regular procedures; authorized the Minister of Agriculture, or his deputy, to discuss with the Russian Ministry of Agriculture a draft memorandum of understanding for cooperation in the fields of agriculture, sign it, and submit the final version for further regular procedures; and authorized the president of General Civil Aviation Authority, or his deputy, to sign a draft agreement with Cameroon in the field of air transport services, and then submit the final version for further regular procedures. The Cabinet stressed that King Salman???Ć?Ös approval to expand the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia Award for Environmental Management to include members of the Organization of Islamic Cooperation reflects the importance the Kingdom places on environmental issues. The Cabinet hailed the Saudi armed forces???Ć?successes in repel numerous attacks on Jizan and Najran Sectors from Yemen and responding forcefully against shelling and rocket fire from Yemen. The Cabinet appreciated King Salman???Ć?Ös order to treat the citizens who sacrificed their lives and prevented a terrorist attack on worshipers inside Al-Hussein Mosque at Al-Anoud neighborhood in Dammam as the martyrs and to award them the Medals of Bravery. The Cabinet expressed the Kingdom's denunciation of the continuing policy of racial discrimination and ethnic cleansing against Rohingya Muslims in Myanmar. It reiterated the Kingdom's call for the need to address such a policy in the framework of the resolutions of the Organization of Islamic Cooperation, the United Nations and the principles of universal human rights. The Cabinet expressed the Kingdom's condemnation of attempts to destabilize the security and stability of Bahrain commended Bahraini security authorities for thwarting a terrorist plot associated with foreign terrorist parties aimed at undermining security and stability in the country. The Cabinet emphasized the importance of concerted efforts to eradicate terrorism in all its forms. King Salman briefed the Cabinet on the message he received from Sheikh Sabah Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al Sabah, Emir of Kuwait, and the outcomes of his meetings with the Prime Minister Tammam Salam of Lebanon and Malaysian Prime Minister Mohammed Najib Abdul Razak. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud chaired the Cabinet's session at Al-Salam Palace in Jeddah this afternoon. At the outset of the session, King Salman expressed his thanks and appreciation to the leaders and presidents for their condolences on the death of Princess Jawaher bint Abdulaziz, praying to Allah Almighty to protect them from any harm.

title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet Session
content: The Cabinet then approved the following: authorized the General President of Youth Welfare, or his deputy, to negotiate and sign a cooperation agreement with Iraq in the field of youth and sports; authorized the Minister of Foreign Affairs, or his deputy, to negotiate and sign a draft memorandum of understanding on political consultations Peru; the addition of a representative of the Royal Commission for Jubail and Yanbu to the National Committee for Legalization and Standardization of the Operation and Maintenance; the reduction of the prices of jet fuel at King Abdulaziz International Airport and King Khalid International Airport, and SR 0.20 ($0.053) per liter a the rest of the nation???Ć?Ös airports. The Cabinet then reviews a number of domestic and international issues. The Cabinet welcomed the appointment of Ismail Ould Cheikh Ahmed of Mauritania as U.N. Secretary General???Ć?Ös Special Envoy for Yemen and wished him every success in his mission. The Council also hailed the results of the consultative meeting of the GCC Ministers of Interiors and their adoption of a GCC unified terrorist list to enhance joint security. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz chaired the weekly session of the Council of Ministers in Riyadh today. He began the session by welcoming Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif bin Abdulaziz, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior, and Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, Deputy Crown Prince, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense, and the new ministers and officials. King Salman expressed his appreciation to Prince Muqrin bin Abdulaziz for his long and dedicated service to the nation.

title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet Session
content: The Cabinet then approved the following: ministerial project contracts and purchase orders greater than SR 100 million ($26.67 million) must be approved by the King, including modifications or addendums to existing contracts, authorizing the Minister of Foreign Affairs, or his deputy, to negotiate a draft memorandum of understanding (MoU) on political consultations with Kazakhstan and MoU on cooperation with Nepal; and the Ministry of Civil Service???Ć?Ös recommendation to exclude some civil service jobs from competitive class; and the renewal of membership members of board of directors of Grain Silos and Flour Mills Organization for three years. The Cabinet also denounced the terror attacks on Egyptian security forces in northern Sinai and expressed its condolences to the government and people of Egypt and families of the victims. The Cabinet condemned the Houthi militia???Ć?Ös continued terrorization of the Yemeni population and the indiscriminate shelling of civilian centers. They also welcomed the moves by Yemeni military unit commanders and personnel who took the initiative to communicate with their legitimate government and declared their loyalty to it. The Cabinet welcomed the decision by Yemeni President Abd Rabbo Mansour Hadi to appointing Prime Minister Khalid Bahah as vice president, calling the appointment an important step at restoring security and stability to Yemen. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz chaired the Cabinet's weekly session at Al-Yamama Palace in Riyadh this afternoon. King Salman began the meeting by briefed the Cabinet on his talks with foreign leaders and ministers. The Cabinet was briefed on the latest regional and international issues, including Operation Decisive Storm.

title: Deputy Crown Prince Receives Freed Saudi Consul in Aden
content: Earlier, the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz instructed concerned authorities to secure a house for Al-Khalidi and his family in Riyadh. The meeting was also attending by Vice Foreign Minister Prince Abdulaziz bin Abdullah bin Abdulaziz and Prince Mohammed bin Salman, Minister of Defense, Chairman of the Royal Court, and Special Adviser to the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques. Prince Mohammed bin Naif bin Abdulaziz, Deputy Crown Prince, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Interior Minister, received today Saudi Consul General in Aden, Yemen, Abdullah bin Mohammed Al-Khalidi upon his arrival at King Khalid International Airport in Riyadh. Al-Khalidi arrived in Riyadh from Najran where he underwent medical checkup following his release by his Yemeni captors after three years of captivity.

title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet Session
content: The Council also authorized the Minister of Labor, or his deputy, to negotiated a memorandum of understanding in the areas of labor with Mexico; authorized the Minister of Civil Service, or his deputy, to negotiated a memorandum of understanding in the areas of field of civil service with Kazakhstan; authorized the President of King Abdulaziz City for Science and Technology (KACST), or his deputy, to negotiate a draft framework memorandum for cooperation in the field of creative economy and society based on knowledge between KACST and Korea???Ć?Ös Ministry of Science, Information Technology, Communication and Future Planning. The Council of Ministers then approved the following: a General Agreement for Cooperation the Comoros, signed in Jeddah City on 20/08/1435 AH; an agreement on cooperation in the field of mutual recognition of driving licenses with Morocco, signed in Marrakech on 11/05/1435 AH; the renewal of the membership of Dr. Abdullah bin Hussein bin Abdulrahman Alqadhi, Amer bin Abdul Hamid bin Fauzan Al-Dulaijan and Dr. Faisal bin Mohammed Amin bin Tawfiq al-Tamimi to the National Committee for Geographic Information Systems, for three years. The Cabinet also condemned criminal acts carried out the Daesh (ISIL) against Iraqi civilians and their cultural heritage in Mosul. The Cabinet condemned the continued Israeli violation of the legitimate rights of the Palestinian people and urged the international community to assume its responsibilities in working to move the peace process stalled because of Israeli intransigence, and put an end to the conflict by ending the occupation, and establishing the State of Palestine with Al-Quds (Jersualem) as its capital. The Cabinet then reviews the latest domestic, regional and international events. The Council of Ministers expressed regret over the situations in Yemen and expressed it support for President Abd Rabbo Mansour Hadi, the legitimate president of Yemen. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz chaired the Cabinet's session at Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh this afternoon. At the outset of the session, King Salman briefed the Cabinet on his talks with Egyptian President Abdul Fattah Al-Sisi, King Abdullah II bin Al Hussein of Jordan, Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas, and Sayyid Fahd bin Mahmoud Al-Said, Deputy Prime Minister for Cabinet Affairs of Oman.

title: Supreme Court Announces Thursday the Start of Ramadan
content: The Royal Court announced this evening that the Supreme Court, which is assigned to determine the start of Ramadan, has announced that the first day of Ramadan will be Thursday, June 18.

title: Foreign Minister Hold Bilateral Meetings with Foreign Ministers of Egypt, Senegal and Somalia
content: Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir met separately in Jeddah today on the sidelines of the of extraordinary meeting of the Council of Foreign Ministers of the Organization of Islamic Cooperation on the on the Situation in Yemen with Foreign Minister Sameh Shoukry of Egypt, Foreign Minister Mankeur Ndiaye of Senegal, and Foreign Minister Abdusalam Omer of Somalia. The meetings addressed topics on the agenda of the ministerial meeting, in addition to bilateral relations and regional and international issues of common interest.

title: Supreme Court Calls on Muslims in the Kingdom to Sight Ramadan Crescent
content: The Supreme Court has called on all Muslims in Saudi Arabia to sight the crescent of the blessed holy month of Ramadan on Tuesday evening, 29 Sha'ban, 1436 AH (June 16). The Supreme Court requested in its announcement whoever sees the crescent by the naked eyes or binoculars to report to the nearest court and register his testimony.

title: Minister of Defense Meets with Former Swedish Defense Minister, Special Swedish Envoy
content: Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, Minister of Defense, President of the Royal Court and Special ???Ć?ÄAdvisor to the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques, met yesterday evening with Baron Bjorn von Sydow, former ???Ć?ÄSwedish Defense Minister and special Swedish envoy. During the meeting, they discussed bilateral relations ???Ć?Äfollowing the offensive remarks made by Sweden's Foreign Minister against Saudi Arabia.???Ć?

title: King Salman Receives Letters from Sweden's King and Prime Minister
content: The letters were deliver by Baron Bjorn von Sydow, former Swedish Defense Minister and Special Envoy during a ???Ć?Ämeeting with King Salman at Al-Oja Palace this evening.???Ć?The Prime Minister also said in his letter that the role of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques in protecting Islam ???Ć?Äand its Holy Places is clear, stressing that his government is concerned over the crisis in the relations between the ???Ć?Ätwo countries and is looking forward to welcoming the return of the Saudi Ambassador to Stockholm in addition to ???Ć?Äbeginning work to enhance the relations between the two countries according to a dialogue that is based on mutual ???Ć?Ärespect.???Ć?Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz received two letters today, one from Sweden's ???Ć?ÄKing Carl XVI Gustaf on the depth of bilateral relations and his keenness on ensuring their continuity, and another ???Ć?Äfrom Sweden's Prime Minister Stefan Lofven in which he expressed deep sorrow and regret over the current crisis ???Ć?Äin the relations between the two countries, stressing the keenness of his government to maintain the relations ???Ć?Äbetween the two countries.???Ć?

title: Kingdom Rejects International Criticism Over Raef Badawi Case
content: The source added that the Kingdom does not accept assaults on its constitution, which is based on Islamic Sharia in the name of human rights. Islamic Sharia protects human rights and preserves life, property, honor, and dignity. An official source at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs today rejected international criticism over the Raef Badawi case as an infringement on its national sovereignty. The Foreign Ministry stresses that the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia totally rejects any form of interference in its internal affairs and any impingement on its sovereignty or the independence and impartiality of its judiciary. The source added that judges have absolute powers in their rulings and that all cases under review before courts are dealt with without distinction, exception, or improper influence. The source added that the Badawi case has been politicize or exploited by those want to undermine the Kingdom???Ć?Ös sovereignty.

title: Grand Mufti Calls on Muslims to Combat Terrorism
content: The Grand Mufti of Saudi Arabia, Sheikh Abdulaziz Al-AsShaikh called on all Muslims in the world to use the internet and social media to cooperate in achieving the Muslim nations' interests and aims of combating terrorism. The Grand Mufti stressed that it is important to combat terrorism and misleading ideologies by cooperating with authorities and intellectuals and members of the society to raise the upcoming generation in a good manner.

title: National Dialogue to Focus on Cultural Issues Next Week
content: Sheikh Saleh Al-Husaiyen, the President of the King Abdulaziz Center for National Dialogue, said told the English daily that the forum aims to recognize the most important cultural elements ???Ć?£that enhance our cultural discourse and its influence on national, regional, Arab and international levels.???Ć?The forum, which will be composed of 32 women and 31 men, will address cultural issues, including religion, education, media, citizenship and identity. The King Abdulaziz Center for National Dialogue will hold a forum in Al-Ahsa Tuesday and Wednesday to discuss a number of pressing issues affecting Saudi society, Saudi Gazette reported today.

title: IMF Report Commends Saudi Fiscal Policies
content: Finally, the Kingdom was commended for the ???Ć?£substantial assistance???Ć?it has extended to developing countries, including its support for the Heavily Indebted Poor Countries (HIPC) and Energy for the Poor???Ć?initiatives. The Executive Board expressed its support for the Saudi government???Ć?Ös ongoing efforts ???Ć?£to implement second-generation reforms in the judicial, education, and financial sectors, aimed at further improving the environment for private sector development???Ć?and welcomed ???Ć?£the continued liberalization of the trade regime, consistent with the authorities???Ć?commitment to a free and open trade system.???Ć?They pointed out that Saudi Arabia was ranked first among Arab countries and 16th globally by the latest World Bank Doing Business Report. The IMF credited proactive fiscal policies for the Kingdom???Ć?Ös economic resiliency, particularly commending authorities for approving a massive stimulus package that ???Ć?£appropriately focused on capital spending???Ć?and contributed ???Ć?£both to diversified domestic growth and the global recovery.???Ć?The report also lauded Saudi officials for ???Ć?£their leadership role in stabilizing world oil markets by maintaining their capacity expansion plans despite lower oil prices.???Ć?As a result of strong government action, it noted, the Saudi economy ???Ć?£delivered another strong performance in 2008 despite global headwinds. Real GDP, buoyed by a sustained broad-based expansion in the non-oil sector (4.3 percent) and higher oil production, grew by 4.4 percent.???Ć?The IMF found that the Kingdom???Ć?Ös inflation and domestic debt are declining, while its foreign direct investment remained high at about $23 billion in 2008. Furthermore, it said, the Saudi banking system ???Ć?£has weathered the global crisis. It remains profitable and well capitalized with low nonperforming loans.???Ć?The International Monetary Fund (IMF) applauded Saudi Arabia???Ć?Ös fiscal policies and characterized its economic outlook as ???Ć?£broadly positive???Ć?in a new report assessing the Kingdom???Ć?Ös economic conditions. In its Article IV Consultation Report, concluded July 13, 2009, the IMF Executive Board found that, ???Ć?£Saudi Arabia confronts the current global crisis from a position of strength, reflecting a track record of prudent macroeconomic policies and structural reforms that have enhanced the economy???Ć?Ös resilience.???Ć?

title: U.S. Official Praises Saudi National Dialogue Initiative
content: Erdman praised the efforts of the King Abdulaziz Center and stressed the benefits of learning from the Saudi experience. He also commended the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz for transforming a successful domestic dialogue initiative into an international phenomenon. Erdman pointed specifically to the King???Ć?Ös sponsorship last year of inter-religious dialogue conferences in Madrid and New York. Deputy Education Minister Faisal bin Moammar received in his office at the King Abdulaziz Center for National Dialogue in Riyadh on Tuesday U.S. Charge d???Ć?ÖAffaires to Saudi Arabia Richard Erdman. Mr. Erdman was briefed on the Center???Ć?Ös achievements and its current activities aimed at promoting a culture of dialogue between the various segments of Saudi society.

title: Grand Mufti Calls for Acceptance of Differing Viewpoints
content: KACND aims to promote a culture of dialogue, tolerance and moderation as well as gain and implement new means of communication between the center and different strata of society, according to its Vice Secretary-General, Fahd Al-Sultan. The three-day forum was organized by the King Abdulaziz Center for National Dialogue (KACND) and consists of 10 sessions on various topics, including the importance of mosques in promoting dialogue and the role of universities and the ministries of education and information in promoting dialogue. During his address, Minister of Culture and Information Dr. Abdulaziz Al-Khojah discussed the ???Ć?£role of the media in promoting a culture of dialogue,???Ć?noting that there are verses in the Qur???Ć?Öan that promote and urge dialogue. ???Ć?£Women are given such rights in the Qur???Ć?Öan,???Ć?he added. Al-Khojah also pointed to the role that the new electronic media is playing in enhancing communication, revealing that steps are being taken toward establishing an independent channel dedicated to dialogue. Al-Asheikh asserted that dialogue can have a positive effect on national cohesion. ???Ć?£A person engaging in dialogue, with all his intellectual and physical power, must work on strengthening society, not on destroying it,???Ć?the daily reported, adding that it should be based on the teachings of the Qur???Ć?Öan and Sunnah. However, Al-Asheikh warned, dialogue should be pursued in good faith and not used to create chaos and confusion in society. Speaking at the opening of the Certified Trainers Forum for Promoting Dialogue July 4, Sheikh Al-Asheikh said Islamic scholars are "willing to answer questions, listen to advice, and discuss and debate for the benefit of people." He added that the idea is to "cooperate by accepting other viewpoints and not enforcing one point of view for the sake of the unity of society.???Ć?Saudi Grand Mufti Sheikh Abdulaziz Al-Asheikh stressed the importance of accepting differing viewpoints, rather than forcing a single perspective on society, Arab News reported Sunday.

title: Fourth Anniversary of King Abdullah???Ć?Ös Ascension to the Throne Celebrated
content: For his part, Minister of Health Dr. Abdullah Al-Rabeeah noted that under the leadership of King Abdullah, the Kingdom has surpassed the development goals set by the U.N. Millennium Declaration in 2000. Minister of Economy and Planning Khalid Al-Qusaibi lauded the Kingdom???Ć?Ös economic growth during the reign of King Abdullah, noting that the Saudi economy ???Ć?£ranks 19 on the list of the world???Ć?Ös largest economies and is the largest in the Arab World.???Ć?He also detailed the large number of development projects currently underway in the Kingdom. Assistant Minister of Culture and Information Prince Turki bin Abdulaziz commented on the Kingdom???Ć?Ös increasingly influential position in regional, Islamic and international arenas. He attributed that rise to the Kingdom???Ć?Ös policies of moderation, non-interference in the affairs of others, adoption of the language of dialogue, unity against terrorism in all its forms, and provision of energy in a way that balances the interests of both producers and consumers. In his own remarks, Chairman of the Allegiance-Pledge Commission Prince Misha???Ć?Öal bin Abdulaziz commended the major reform efforts led by King Abdullah in the political, economic, educational and social fields. ???Ć?£The Kingdom is no longer today an oil country affecting the world economy only, but it is a state that contributes to making decisions regionally and internationally,???Ć?said Prince Misha???Ć?Öal. He pointed to U.S. President Barack Obama???Ć?Ös decision to begin his tour of the Arab world in Saudi Arabia as an indication of ???Ć?£the Kingdom???Ć?Ös position and the extent of the influence of its leader . . . in making the future of the region and the world.???Ć?Prince Saud noted that the conferences brought together top political and religious leaders from around the world and initiated an ongoing interfaith dialogue process. King Abdullah has often promoted constructive engagement, Prince Saud added, as evidenced by his call for dialogue between Palestinian factions and Iraqi sects, his plea for Arab reconciliation, and his creation of a National Dialogue Center at home. Foreign Minister Prince Saud Al-Faisal reflected on the King???Ć?Ös commitment to cross-cultural understanding in an article entitled ???Ć?£The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques and the Culture of Dialogue.???Ć?He highlighted King Abdullah???Ć?Ös sponsorship of the World Initiative for Dialogue between Followers of Religions and Cultures, which held two summits last year ???Ć?one in Madrid in July and another at U.N. headquarters in New York in November. Saudi Arabia celebrated today the 4th anniversary of the allegiance-pledge to the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz, who ascended to the throne on 26/6/1426 H. Speeches were delivered and articles written to mark the occasion by a number of governors, ministers and other officials.

title: $267 Million to Upgrade Math and Science Education
content: Asiri said the project is expected to be completed in three years. Muhammad Asiri, secretary-general of the project, said in order to successfully implement the project, classroom teachers and education supervisors need to be trained in modern teaching techniques. Asiri said 4,000 teachers have already been trained and about 550 education supervisors are undergoing training in the new teaching methods, Al-Watan reported. The new math and science curricula will be introduced in the first and fourth standards of primary schools and the first standard of intermediary schools beginning next academic year. The new curriculam will be introduced in secondary schools the following year.?é?? The Ministry of Education will launch a SR 1 billion ($267 million) project to upgrade math and science syllabi in the Kingdom???Ć?Ös schools, Al-Watan newspaper reported today.?é??

title: King Abdullah Makes Major Government Appointment
content: Other major appointments include: Ibrahim bin Shayie Al-Hoqail, chairman of the Court of Grievances; Ali bin Abdulrahman Al-Hamad, Deputy Chairman of the Court of Grievances; Bandar bin Mohammed Al-Aiban, chairman of the Human Rights Commission; Abdullah bin Suleiman Bin-Munie, Abdullah bin Mohammed Al-Mutlak and Abdulmohsen Al-Obaikan, advisers at the Royal Court with the rank of minister; Mohammed bin Ahmed Al-Kenhal, president of Saudi Food and Drugs Authority; and Mohammed bin Fahd Al-Dossary, chairman of the Higher Administrative Court. King Abdullah also appointed Lt. Gen. Hussein bin Abdullah Al-Qubail, Deputy Chief of staff and Maj. Gen. Abdulrahman bin Abdullah Al-Murshid commander of the Land Forces. Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdullah today issued a Royal Decree appointing former Justice Minister Abdullah Al-Asheikh as chairman of the Majlis Al-Shura (Consultative Council); Dr. Saleh Bin-Humaid chairman of the Supreme Judiciary Council; Abdulaziz Al-Humain head of the Commission for the Promotion of Virtue and Prevention of Vice; Abdulrahman bin Abdulaziz Al-Kelya chief justice of the Supreme Court; Muhammad Al-Jasser governor of the Saudi Arabian Monetary Agency (SAMA); Faisal bin Abdulrahman Al-Muammar Deputy Minister of Education with the rank of a minister; Dr. Bandar bin Abdulmohsen Al-Qanawi executive director of National Guard Health Affairs; and Khaled bin Abdullah Al-Sabti Deputy Minister of Education For Boys???Ć?Affairs.

title: Council of Senior Ulema Reconstituted
content: A Royal decree was issued today relieving Sheikh Dr. Yousuf bin Mohammed Ali Al-Ghafees, a member of the Standing Committee for Research and Fatwa, an affiliate of Commission of Senior Ulema, from his post upon his request. King Abdullah also extended the term of members of the committee for four years, effective 3/3/1430 H., corresponding to February 27, 2009, unless otherwise indicated. Grand Mufti Sheikh Abdulaziz bin Abdullah Al Aasheikh was reappointed chairman of the Commission of Senior Ulema. Current members: King Abdullah relieved Sheikh Dr. Abdulaziz bin Mohammad Al-Abdulmunem, upon his own request, from his post as Secretary General of the Commission of Senior Ulema, and appointed Sheikh Dr. Fahd bin Saad Al-Majed as his replacement, at the excellent grade. Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz today issued a number of Royal Decrees reconstituting the Council of Senior Ulema (Muslim Scholars) by appointing new members and extending the tenures of others. Royal Decrees were issued on February 14, 2009, appointing members to the Commission of Senior Ulema and extending the terms others.

title: Shura Council Members Appointed
content: Dr. Abdullah bin Muhammad Al-Ashaikh (Chairman of Majlis Al-Shura) Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz today issued Royal Decree appointing new members of the Majlis Al-Shura (Consultative Council) for a period of four years effective from the end of the current period of Majlis Al-Shura.

title: New Virtue Commission Head Appointed
content: The King Abdullah today relieved Sheikh Ibrahim bin Abdullah Al-Ghaith, director general of the Commission for the Promotion of Virtue and Prevention of Vice, at his own request, from his post, and appointed Sheikh Abdulaziz bin Humayen Al-Humayen as his replacement.

title: Judiciary Reconstituted
content: Sheikh Dr. Mohammad bin Abdullah Al-Shanqiti was relieved from his post as chief of the Court of Grievances, upon his request, and Sheikh Ibrahim bin Shayie Al-Haqil appointed to the post at the rank of minister. Sheikh Ali bin Abdulrahman bin Abdulaziz Al-Hammad was appointed Deputy Chief of Grievances Court. Sheikh Abdulrahman bin Abdulaziz Al-Colleyia was named chairman of the Supreme Court at the rank of minister; Sheikh Mohammed bin Fahd bin Saad Al-Dosari was appointed president of the Supreme Administrative Court at the rank of minister; Sheikh Dr. Saleh bin Abdullah Bin Humeid was appointed chairman of the Supreme Judicial Council at the rank of minister; Sheikh Dr. Abdullah bin Mohammed bin Ibrahim Al-Asheikh was relieved of his post as Minister of Justice and appointed chairman of Majlis Shura (Consultative Council) effective 3/3/1430 H, corresponding to February 27, 2009; Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz today issued a number of royal decrees appointing members to the Supreme Judicial Council and Supreme Court and extending the terms other.

title: New Head of Human Rights Commission Named
content: A Royal Decree today relieved Turki bin Khalid Al-Sudeiri, upon his own request, from his post as President of the Saudi Human Rights Commission, and appointed Bandar Al-Aiban as his replacement.

title: Ministry of Finance Issues Statement on FY 2009 Budget
content: d. New fiscal, institutional, and structural reforms have been introduced in 2008. These included the creation of the Public Commission of Railroads, the Consumer Protection Association, the Cooperative Associations Law, the Public Sector Performance Center, the National Strategy for Health and the Environment and National Water Company. c. There were 13 new IPOs during 2008, increasing the number of companies listed in the stock market to 127. The CMA licensed 30 new brokerage and portfolio management firms. Capital Market The broad money supply during the first ten months of FY 2008 grew by 14 percent. According to Central Department of Statistics and information, total exports of goods and services are expected to grow by 31.2 percent, reaching SR 1.226 billion [$326.9 billion] in 2008. Non-oil exports of goods are expected to grow by 10 percent, amounting to SR 115 billion [$30.7 billion], representing 10.2 percent of total goods exported. In addition, private sector contribution to GDP is expected to be 46 percent in constant prices. According to Central Department of Statistics and Information, GDP is projected to grow in 2008 by 22 percent in current prices and 4.2 percent in constant prices, reaching SR 1.753 billion [$467.5 billion].
